title: Return to Office, Reframed
content:
Working from the office should not be seen as a burden or an obligation. Being at the office from Tuesday to Thursday is the guidance for the CFO world and since large part of our business at Pune is that, irrespective of your functional group that would me be my recommendation. If you are part of Mike’s world it is not a choice to not come on Tuesday-Thursday.

If you schedule personal appointments unless inflexible or urgent, set them up for Monday or Friday. As required also take the day(s) off.

Many of us come even on Mondays and Fridays, so while I don’t expect anyone to be here 5 days a week (it may become a mandate at some point) if you cannot make it on Tuesday, Wednesday or Thursday then find another proxy day.

I have seen that many in the younger generations are forgetting that we went to schools and colleges, we attended for 5 days and in many cases 5.5 days. Sometimes we attended vivas and special lectures even on a Sunday. All of a sudden there is an entitled mindset. Not sure why???

If any of you owned your business you would run it exactly like this. So help me run this setup as if we all owned this place together. Share these thoughts (voice over recommended) with your teams as required.

title: A Snowflake Use Case for the CFO World
content:
What a great question to ask the CEO of Snowflake and even a greater response. Before you see that Here is my take: I want to see a Snowvation use case that ingests large data set (event logs, authentication/authorization logs or telemetry data sets) with SNOWPIPE, transform as required, modeled as UNISTORE capable, with an ML model built to train the data set and a possible app/UI to leverage SNOWPARK/STREAMLIT and respond to below thoughts from Mark Ronald. The app should build conversational AI interpretations of the logs to respond to user queries. Thoughts/Takers??

Mark Ronald Okay. As a follow-up, Frank, I wanted to ask you on the topic of generative AI and large language models. Can you frame up the opportunity there for Snowflake because I would think with the Unistore, you could probably handle all these chat logs. You could be training some of these models on very large data sets. I would think that Snowpipe's can pull in social media feeds and kind of help to improve those models. You see any customer activity around that vector or any tailwinds you think that could be developing in time? Frank Slootman Well, it's still early in the cycle. Obviously, like everybody else, we're all over it in terms of evaluating where these technologies can make a difference. I mean the things that we sort of hone in on short term, is called completion code optimizations, things that are very, very clear business returns.

One of the challenges with these new technologies that people come up with a lot of interesting questions, but without a solid business model that's not going to take off. So we take a very pragmatic view. We do anticipate that Snowflake data will be a very, very big driver of large language model in conjunction with many, many other data sources. So we think that the gravity around data will drive a lot of this action activity to our platform. Concluding with an Oxymoron: Your job will not become obsolete if you are thinking about how your skills will become obsolete in 5 years from now.

title: Power of Congruency
content:
Power of Congruency: In the geometry of human intelligence, we touched based on adjacency for skills, products and productivity acceleration. Similarly, for organizations to prosper, congruence at all levels is important. There will often be disagreements with managers and your peers. You need to know what the disagreement is about and need to have a mindset to ‘agree to disagree’ and be on the same page. This avoids bureaucracy and tangential movements, keeping all of us in the same direction. Yet again, having honest introspections and open dialogs is the key, and to know that the key is in your hands and not with someone else. RLHF & AGI : AI models continuously improve their capabilities from Reinforced Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF), this is how they become more human-like. In a nutshell, AI models continue to improve their understanding with how you understand or not from the responses. Future of Artificial Intelligence will lead us to Artificial General Intelligence (AGI), a system that will possess human-like cognitive abilities across a wide range of tasks. This is how GPTs will eventually evolve to be AGI based.

10x thinking: Technology and technologists are perceived to become irrelevant with these AI innovations. We have hired the best of the best talent and are always chasing ambitious goals to deliver for our initiatives. If we think that AI will replace technologists then you are not a good one to begin with. ChatGPT and likes can make you productive by 10x.

1x input => GPT => 10x output; the only thing in question is your “x”.

title: Blockchain and Crypto: Preserving Evidence
content:
Blockchain and Crypto: I haven’t touched this topic in a while. If there is one thing blockchain does well, it preserves evidence perfectly. Tracking transactions in fiat currencies is hard - transactions and fraud tracking becomes an uphill task as money moves through different hands.

Though not yet largely regulated, crypto has amazing ledger tracking abilities. There are companies that provide data, services, and research to government agencies, exchanges, financial institutions and insurance companies to solve criminal cases and ensure safe access to cryptocurrencies. Chain Analysis is one such company.

AutoGPT: Last time, I had mentioned GPT tools talking to each other. Here is a starting point. In this instance, just announced as part of AutoGPT, GPT tools do auto-prompting to identify and implement the next task, automatically. AutoGPT was trying to create an app for a developer, it recognized that the developer did not have Node (Tool kit to create server side web applications)

- It googled how to install Node on its own
- Found a stack overflow article with link
- Downloaded and Extracted for auto-install
- Spawned a server, all of this on its own This will very soon graduate AI tools from being a coding co-pilot, to a Personal Digital Assistant, managing programs/projects and auto assignment of tasks, follow-ups and many more use cases for auto-CRM, planning a holiday and a lot more.

Uphill climb in the Mid-management: Few thoughts for the leaders, especially for mid-management. In this machine led economy, many organizations have changed structures to flatten leadership layers. It is imperative that in addition to leading super productive organizations, you as a leader are constantly demonstrating the ability to make an impact and have strong narratives of what you deliver. On a side note, don’t take a stand on internal or external affiliation that sound like politics.

Speed, agility and readily available masterstrokes are important. This was nicely demonstrated by introducing ML and ChatGPT as an efficiency mechanism in our QA architecture last week. Kudos to Nirmal Raisoni - a topic for our staff next week. Of course, Service Mesh, Templatized Reporting (Data Analytics) and Predictive Intelligence in ServiceNow are also very exciting topics. So what is your Masterstroke - is it ready in your backpocket and how soon before you share it?

title: The Divided Front on AI
content:
By now, most of you have familiarized yourself with chatGPT, if not used extensively. As we peel this onion, there is a divided front in the tech world to decide if we temporarily hold evolution/use of AI tools or we continue with the acceleration. There are concerns primarily regarding whether we should build a framework right now for regulated and safe use of AI tools or react after much of the damage is done. Also, imagine in the world of miscreants, if chatGPT starts interacting with another AI tool endlessly to talk without human intervention. What would that look like?

A leadership thought on Power of Adjacency: Earlier last week, a young woman came to me for professional counseling. She had a dilemma of whether to inherit and run a very successful family business or create a new business from scratch. Her reservation was against how certain businesses especially in male dominant cultures do not appreciate the other gender.

I asked her to speak with her father openly and explain to him about her reservations. I advised that they refine the business model to build a sophisticated facade that takes away the traditional male dominant reservations and to bring a net neutral new-age corporate view to the traditional business that is an acceptable adjacent working model. It will take a few years however not impossible to create.

How does this matter to us? Firstly, if you imagine the landscape of our platform, as we moved from data warehouse on the cloud to the cloud data platform and then to the de facto data cloud, we came through evolution of product capabilities in adjacency. We slowly but surely pulled in adjacent capabilities to make them part of our platform. Remember, how Frank mentioned in his note about us being a platform, not a tool. All this happens with incorporating many new adjacent capabilities.

Leadership works similarly. We struggle from having done the same thing for many years. An easy transition would be for us to build skills in adjacency. Being a data engineer you can be a data analyst, BSA to a PM, QA analyst to a data analyst or even a BSA. This transition is much easier through job rotations in adjacent teams within the organization as you reduce the points of complexity from cultural familiarity. My personal career trajectory has followed the trait for many years and will continue to be so.

title: Who Is Really on Your Team?
content:
Who is in your team? Often, when we narrate about the members of our team we first think of the people who we depend on, ones who make us proud and the ones that usually follow our lead. Simple and easy enough. Not quite.

As you would think of the people that work for you, your manager might think of you as a part of her/his team similarly. So of course, yet another thought to reflect on your manager as a member of your team - I owe it to this person.

One of the 16 things in my sweet-16 list of leadership qualities, peer relationships play an important role. Think about this - the most difficult group to work alongside is going to be peers, whether you are a leader or not, as we implicitly compete with them. Your ability to work with your peers will make your leader’s job much easier. It will allow him/her to work on bigger opportunities. Peer relationship will accelerate the trust within the organization and often against our discomfort this becomes a key skill to develop. Imagine the impact on our productivity and culture, if your leader has to spend a lot of time resolving disputes within the team. On the macro front, many countries including BRICS are evaluating to trade in non-US dollar currencies for their commerce. This will potentially change how the US dollar is used as an intermediary. Besides this there is some more attention largely given to non-regulated currency in the form of crypto, given that the regulated fiat currencies are going through unpredictable movements. Also as a result, gold prices have appreciated 15-20% in the last 6 months. Lastly, being curious is so important. I had met a young man in Goa a few years ago and I saw his name on his uniform, it read ‘Arlindo’. I asked him what it meant - he said no idea. I met another young girl similarly last week during the customer show case in Mumbai. I asked what ‘Eralinda’ meant and she had no idea. I had looked both of them up, ‘Arlindo’ is a name of Portuguese origin and meant an Eagle. ‘Eralinda’ means spirited, of Hebrew origin. She told me that I made her day by talking about it.

title: Lessons From Hosting Customer Showcases
content:
Last week was full of enriching experiences for me as I had the opportunity to host customer showcases at TCS, the Mahindra Group, and Bajaj Allianz, as well as host the all-hands meeting and work on my professional pitch. Speaking with customers certainly requires a lot of practice, and I learned a lot from these experiences. Here are some key takeaways from the week.

One thing that struck me was how much potential there is in the AI industry right now. It reminded me of the early days of social media and content creation, where early adopters were able to create multi-million dollar businesses by being among the first to capitalize on new platforms.

Now, with AI and chatbots like ChatGPT, there is a new gold rush to capitalize on this technology. But there's also another technology that is still in development that could change the game in a big way: superconductors.

Superconductors are materials that allow objects to levitate and allow for incredibly efficient energy transfer. For example, a train could be run with just one push if it was built with superconductors, and a microprocessor based on superconductors would use only 1% of the energy compared to a semiconductor-based chip. Similarly, superconductor-based batteries would lose only 5% of their energy compared to regular chemical composition-based batteries. Although much of the research around superconductors is currently being done at universities, if a material could be developed that can super-conduct electricity at room temperature, it would revolutionize the AI industry. Building mag-lev trains, superconductor-based chips, efficient energy transmission, and batteries would be a piece of cake.

So, it's important to remain invested, at least intellectually, in the progressive development of energy-efficient microprocessors, content/data publishing and hosting platforms (like our Data Cloud), and core AI platform companies. Be ready to welcome the AI age and make it snow, even then.

title: SVB and the Banking Shock, Explained
content:
SVB and 4 other banks severely hit over the last week - sounds esoteric??? Let’s examine Early 2020 - pandemic hits us. Many of the big nations shutdown the economy. This resulted in the governments either offering stimulus or printed currencies, in many cases both. This free flow of cash increased liquidity. Citizens assumed free flow to exist and many chose to remain unemployed. Post pandemic, liquidity of cash is tightened and interest rates have shot up starkly. Poorly governed (run) banks with poor risk management lost a lot of valuation/funds. Governments come back to help again - circle of life. Is this the beginning of an onion peel? Only time will tell.

Is India impacted? Does not seem to be the case yet.

Shifting gears, perspectives on leadership as learnt in a talk by Lt. General Shokin Chauhan

- When leading teams, give guidance referring to examples from your personal experiences
- Be sincere with your team, know them well, let them know you well and most importantly, never lie to them (I plan not to and also hope that I have never)
- Generosity has great power
- Meet people in casual setup to coach at home, cafe or on a walk, so at the office there are no intense conversations (I do it all the time if you have known me well)
- Ask for empowerment from leaders: This is the way I am going to function and you will allow me to do this (I have an example with Sunny if you would like to know)
- Make sure that you know your team well and for them to know that you know them well (Do you think so?)
- This one is mine from my recent experiences especially in preparation for our leader’s visit: she does not want to see your resume when you meet him, she wants to hear of your introspections
- Here is a snapshot of my write-up on “Leadership Animation” Index

title: What the VCs Wanted After SVB
content:
I was speaking with a bunch of venture capitalists in Silicon Valley on Friday morning IST. They had a long day with what transpired with SVB.

From me, they wanted to appreciate evolution of the Data Cloud along with current and future state of cybersecurity. I am intending to attend the RSA conference later next month and I am in the process of scheduling interactions with the larger technology community there.

They asked me if there was still an 18 month lag in Asia for technology adoption life cycle. My immediate response was a big YES just looking at how some of the customers and prospects are still very on-prem based and working to gradually move into their cloud journey. I resonate that with our customers here as they are still not ready for prime time innovations of our product landscape.

Got me to think about a few things.

While the technology S Curve lifecycle still does exist the time horizon is much shorter If we have this 18 month lag across the continent then we will become one lifetime behind in 2 years We all talk about chatGPT and AI evolution and think that we know what’s going on. There is a lot more to it What about businesses that are building on chatGPT, building the likes of chatGPT, how will the content on domains like Quora be capitalised, and what other ecosystem elements will become critical in this space. Think about acceleration of internet, at the beginning of 21st century, through the advent of smart endpoints. Similarly, what else can happen in this time and age?

Thoughts??

I will share more related thoughts on SVB, AI ecosystem and geopolitical assessment next week or during other meetings.

title: AI Meets Law: The Harvey Bet
content:
Sequoia Capital, US based early venture fund with $85B in assets invested $21M in a firm named, Harvey.Ai. If you have watched ‘Suites’ you can imagine what the intersection of AI and Harvey can look like. Good lawyers in the US cost $800/hour and imagine that now a lot of their work can be done by AI tools.

Likes of Harvey.Ai will offer legal services at a discounted rates, perhaps 30-40% lower. This will also mean that good non-tech law firms now have the opportunity to use these tools and provide 30-40% discounts by augmenting AI capabilities.

Organizations, largely, as we look ahead will have an opportunity to increase efficiencies by 30-40% or reduce costs by 30-40%, perhaps something in between.

Let’s go back in time. Imagine when MS Office was launched and we all started using capabilities at some point in time. We would all have been unemployed today if we had not figured out how to use MS Office in the meantime.

AI offers the same opportunity to all of us, technology enthusiasts or not. You choose to use it and you can be 30% more productive, although let’s differentiate between efficient and productive here. Efficiency means you will do the same task in 70% of the time and productivity means you can produce 1.3x in the time spent. Organizations can differentiate between the two. It is that time to take a leap of faith into the future, embrace AI and make a transition. Curate independently, Use AI to Improvise and take the outcomes of AI to Authenticate with your midas touch.

Have you taken the plunge yet?

title: Where Is Big Tech's Organic Innovation?
content:
How much organic (home grown) disruptive innovation have you seen from large technology companies like Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta and Apple in the recent years. Not much as far as I can tell. Apple created iPhone in 2007, Google core search in late 90s, Google Maps in early 2000s, AWS has been around for a long while and Microsoft at best created Windows in 90s. Most growth has come from acquisitions and these companies have become large technology conglomerates as a result. Google announced $70B worth shares buy back from $115B of its cash reserves. Microsoft has executed a lot of share buy back in earlier years. Meta bought back $40B worth of shares.

Why does this happen?

- 
- 
- 
- 
- Valuations are low enough for these companies to benefit from invested in self It is very hard to make foundational platform changes in these companies They are transitioning to being cash cows from main product lines that have worked so well Many of them have announced staff reductions to maintain high levels of profitability If you followed Microsoft and Google historically, there have been so many regulatory interventions for anti-trust which discourages deployment of capitals in competitive or adjacent capabilities
- Meta has had an upshot in its valuations by 90% from this financial engineering Where is Snowflake? We announced a buy back of $2B from $4B (Jan ‘23) cash on hand. One thing that makes us different as a tech company is that we are not a tech conglomerate yet, still a singular platform and we continue to innovate. Our growth is both organic and inorganic through acquisitions, still within the conformity of our platform. Do you remember from Frank’s note that we are a platform and not a tool? US - Sub 1% growth of recession - Jarome Powell In response for the us govt and fed - Cut spending, keep energy cost low, Inflation => function of money supply over amount of goods and services; QE gave us more money and not having enough supplies leads to inflationary situations Record low people coming into the company; domestic citizens don’t want to do regular jobs; Cool of job market, cool of unemployment

title: Aggregation, Bundling and Unbundling
content:
We figured that big companies grew organically and inorganically with strategic adjacency. Let’s understand aggregation, bundling and unbundling effects for them.

Platform aggregators enable businesses through convergence of end users, third party app builders and the platform itself. A few mentions on that note:

- Apple, us as consumers and companies that build applications on AppStore
- Google with YouTube as one of its platform, video publishers and YouTube subscribers
- Facebook for social media, eBay and Craigslist as marketplaces to buy/sell goods and services App builders and service providers are under constant pressure from terms and conditions of the platform providers. This has led to several new businesses created from unbundling opportunities. Here are some examples of new businesses created from unbundling of aggregators capabilities
- 
- 
- 
- AirBnB for leasing your property for the short term Reddit as a sophisticated discussion form Zillow and Redfin as real estate buy/sell. There are equivalent companies in India Tinder and Bumble for relationships In totality the digital markets continue to grow, in many cases market share (and valuation possibly) of the aggregators dilutes. Sometimes the offshoot companies become bigger than aggregators. Take AirBnB and Zillow (Real Estate) for example.

We continue to grow through aggregation though in the confinement of the data platform and there are offshoot companies for data cataloging, data quality, visualization and a lot more. New businesses will continue to evolve from continuous aggregation and unbundling effects. AI will accelerate this process.

Writing this note during rETL of my travel as I come back to post my US Ux score across the hemisphere. You will see this note as soon as Airflow orchestration pushes my workflow on slack. Ha ha, Data lifecycle personified. See you soon

title: Inside the Congressional AI Hearing
content:
There was a congressional hearing in the United States last week to appreciate the perils and the boons of AI. Congressmen/women interacted with Sam Altman, founder open AI, prof. Gary Marcus and Christina Montgomery, Chief Privacy and Trust officer at IBM.

### Here are the key takeaways

- AI is not new but it is certainly having a moment right now
- Composure demonstrated by these leaders was very profound, relatively more open/engaging and humanly, compared to prior technology hearings
- There is a realization that we may have acted slowly with Social Media to have massive organizations created and to have witnessed many manipulations with the Social Media
- There was a consideration to create both national and international agencies to regulate use of the LLMs (Large Language Models)
- Europe was ahead with Social Media and is also relatively ahead of restricting the use of LLMs in geography. They also have presented some version of AI regulation
- Open AI model chatGPT 4.0 and subsequent ones will not be trained on consumer data, which is a good thing
- At this point in time, the LLMs are not validating the use cases for copyrights and patents. For example, I asked chatGPT to create lyrics in hindi for a love song mimicking Javed Akhtar’s work style. Bingo - it did so for me. What the heck?
- At present there are many websites that are entirely created by AI bots. There is a fundamental difference between reproducing and generating content, as reproduction may have copyright and patent infringements
- As hard as it sounds, there is at least a mindset for governments, public companies and scientists to work together in this forum. Thoughts are around management of access, creation of right use cases, moderation of models for culture and language inclusivity, and managing to use AI for progressive use cases
- The hard part is a lot of the smaller LLMs can also run on the edge nodes which will make the whole process of regulations very hard My aim is to remain engaged, appreciate the apprehensions and look for building effective use cases, learn prompt engineering and use of models with real-time data sets

title: The End of Appointment News
content:
Do you realize how a lot of us stopped watching television news or even reading newspaper news? We have moved for news to be real-time, sent to us (compared to us getting to it), and to become independent of traditional media houses. One such change is evident on Twitter spaces. Recently, Ron Desantis and Elon Musk discussed prior’s US presidential candidature on ‘Twitter space’, platform to engage in live audio conversations on Twitter. Many of the traditional media houses cried wolf and botched on a 20 minute delay in this engagement.

Between direct to citizen interactions encouraging citizen journalism, creating break-out rooms and real-time tech enabled streaming, traditional news media is challenged and changed. This trend will soon follow in other geographies, my prediction, including India where the access to smartphones and inexpensive data plans is also changing the game.

On a separate note, the startup game in major technology hubs is becoming a tale of 2 cities: this is a great time for AI based startups and for other pre-AI/pre-bubble burst startups to shut shop unless they are making revenues that far exceed their current investments. New investments are still a function of a dream and the energy levels of the founder.

Speaking about energy levels of a founder, did you know Musk offered to sell Tesla to Apple in 2018 when its valuation was hovering around $60B to where it is now at $600B+ today?

Lastly, I made some notes from Frank’s commentary at the all hands

- Twitter head count reduced from 7,500 to 1,800 and it still performs superiorly
- For all of us as leaders to be close to the action and take charge inside the Data Cloud
- For a consumption centric revenue mode, our forecast dropped by $100M for 3.8% downgrade in revenue and valuation took a rollercoaster ride
- Don’t get to obsessed with your job titles/labels, just come and work together (Don’t create an identity crisis from self-created expectations is my read)
- Talk to everyone, ask questions and operate through influence - the world takes note of this
- Don’t pull your badge out ever
- When you feel objected, unpack the objection and go to the lowest atomic level to gain clarity and resolution (primarily, this came up as a response for sales cycle although this applies to all of us) Happy learning as we unpack a new week

title: Where Is AI? (The Sunday Thoughts Origin)
content:
Earlier in April this year, I started sharing my learnings with my direct leadership team and coined it as ‘Sunday Thoughts’. I am expanding the outreach after receiving some input to share with the larger audience.

Where is AI?

A lot has transpired with Generative AI this year, which has been a key topic of interest for me along with macroeconomics, geopolitics and our beloved Data Cloud. AI coin has two sides like anything else: one that will probably reshape and displace jobs/industries and another side that will stand up to beat the next pandemic, climate crisis, energy challenges and asteroids. We need to embrace AI through constant education and awareness.

More than a decade ago, I was introduced to terms like SDDC (Software Defined Data Center) and SD-WAN (Software Defined Wide Area Network). Virtual Machines enabled Software Defined Data Centers where these logical machines were connected over Software Defined Network. We are seeing a lot more activities in Software and Hardware driven AI capabilities.

Recently, there were major announcements about the NVIDIA Omniverse™ Avatar Cloud Engine (ACE). Let’s examine this a bit more by reviewing a few terms.

Universe: All of the space along with time context represent our universe (Uni for one) Multiverse: Multiple universes exist in a multiverse (parallel), beyond our observable universe (Multi for many) Omniverse: Collection of every single universe, including multiverses represent Omniverse (Omni for All inclusive) Metaverse: This is an immersive virtual world which facilitated by Virtual Reality (hence Meta) Now imagine, NVIDIA Omniverse Avatar Cloud Engine where Metaverse meets AI. This as I understand is a single, shared, immersive, persistent, 3D virtual space where humans experience life in ways they could not in the physical world. Imagine physical factories for Chips (ICs), Batteries, Electric Vehicles and others are virtualized/digitalized in an immersive platform, they are now redesigned and improved in the NVIDIA Omniverse ACE before the actual physical factories are changed. I am personally excited to hear more about this at our Summit where we will have Frank Slootman speak with Jensen Huang, President of NVIDIA. Hopefully there is further intersection of our Data Cloud in this Metaverse at some point. Another topic on the leadership styles to review is the ‘HIPPO Effect’. The HIPPO effect describes a cognitive bias according to which the opinion of the highest paid person receives more attention. We all deal with this situation many times and our leadership posture of going (being) direct will make an oxymoron out of this context. I encourage you all to not build a bigger HIPPO out of the leaders that you interact with. This would mean that you need to find the right place and right time to provide thoughts and inputs for you to be heard. Don’t be a yes (wo)man and figure out a way to share your perspectives and don’t use the watercooler to share your thoughts, go to the source.

Have you seen a Hippo? Are you one?

title: Displacement of Traditional Businesses
content:
If you recall when Uber (and Ola) started, they introduced application based taxi rides booking from the convenience of your handheld devices. Although it did displace the ‘haul a taxi’ business. Similarly AirBnB diluted the hospitality business and WeWork diluted some of the commercial real estate business providing more convenience and flexibility with technology as a medium.

What happens when Tech Businesses and Governments Intersect As I write this, Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in the United States is challenging the operating model of the Crypto exchanges for companies like Coinbase and Binance. They are challenging that 13 assets with Coinbase and 12 with Binance are being traded as securities that are not registered. Understand that Crypto Currencies are not regulated in most of the large economies and this leads to a conflict between the government agencies and crypto companies. Let’s understand some more:

- Currencies like US Dollar, Euro to Rupee which are regulated by the governments are called as Fiat Currencies
- Crypto-currencies are traded as block chain enabled ledger driven digital currencies
- Companies like CoinBase, Binance, and FTX (liquidated now) are global trading exchanges that enable conversion of Fiat to Crypto currencies and vice versa
- Fees paid for the conversion of the currencies are called as gas fees
- These exchanges are not regulated by a specific government as they are global and technological in nature
- FTX went bankrupt as they fraudulently leveraged the crypto deposits of their customers leading to massive losses. A classic outcome of an unregulated business model
- The same SEC that allowed for CoinBase’s IPO is now leading a process to irregularize crypto exchanges pushing these companies to move away geographically and then to IP (as in network) gate and block citizens from trading in Crypto This is an outcome of a situation when technology intersects the governments and its proceedings. Impact on AI tech whether it is LLMs or other relevant AI tech is yet to be seen as this will also disrupt economies. Impacts of employment and associated tax revenues are hard to predict as governments are still putting their arms around what is triggering with the LLMs. In both cases of AI and Crypto the technology moves/moved much faster than how quickly the governments react.

What must the governments do?

This necessitates that governments also lead the technology adoption in parallel or in advance where applicable. Look at how UPI like transactions and the implementation of digital currencies by some countries will lead a new transformation. Will this possibly accelerate de-dollarization? Only time will tell. Next week I will touch upon the Agility and Value of work delivered which I was hoping to write this time. However, the above thoughts already gave a very detailed write-up. If you would like to hear about a topic of your interest DM me and I will try my best to write something.

Enjoy the new week.

title: Agility vs Value of a Work Product
content:
Let’s examine two roles of Program Management vs Product Management, as we think about this topic. A Program Manager manages multiple initiatives with a focus on planning, resources, risks, communications, interdependencies, strategic thinking and alignment of these initiatives. A Product Manager focuses on delivering products for revenue growth keeping in mind customer’s needs, market research data, and product vision while collaborating with engineering, design and marketing functions. In a nutshell Program lifecycle is all about agility and delivery focus and Product delivery is all about the value and growth focus. It does not mean one arm need not know of the other.

Similarly, individuals need to focus on creating value at a very high speed. Sooner you draw this balance to ensure agility of a program manager and value orientation of the products you create, your need to get guidance and coaching to do your job will reduce significantly. This implicit mindset in your thought fabric will drive for superlative outcomes for a long term.

Moving from Value to Savings Have you ever thought of what happens to your PF contributions? About 45% of funds are invested in government securities and 55% in other fixed income securities through public sector and private companies. Up to 15% of the funds can also be invested in equities through Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs). Each of these investment opportunities compound your contribution to make a big payout eventually.

As projected, Social security funds (equivalent to pension or PF funds in India) in the US will be depleted in 2034 as the government constantly spends more on the payout than what it makes from contributions and other sources of income.

What happens when investment strategies fail and the governments are unable to pay recurring or one time pension payments back to its citizens. Sometimes this leads to a combination of increase in taxes collected, printing more currencies (quantitative easing) and introducing austerity measures. Economies and GDPs (Gross Domestic Product) of nations grow based on many factors including political stability and investments in infrastructure, education, and technologies. Look around and you will be able to pinpoint nations where economies will flourish or decline.

I am looking forward to announcing our Snowvation winners shortly. Hope to see you all at the India All Hands meeting on Thursday.

title: Outer Space and Communications
content:
I ran into two things that made me think about our outer space. Firstly, there is a 170 Foot Celestial Body on Collision Course with earth. NASA, the world’s premier space agency, has been closely tracking it and predicts that the asteroid will intersect with earth’s orbit although the impact probability with the earth is very low. To keep the planet safe, there will be necessary intervention for diversion or other measures to deal with this celestial body.

Secondly, on my flight to San Francisco, I was appreciating how we are able to have real-time TV streaming and high speed internet access on the flights. This is made possible with the StarLink network. So what is a StarLink network?

- This is a satellite constellation operated by SpaceX and provides coverage over 56 countries
- This includes 4,000 mass produced small satellites in Low Earth Orbit, and will possibly extend to 42,000 units over time
- This will eventually become a global mobile phone service, although it is hard to tell if this replaces our existing on the ground mobile networks. Just think about how our landlines got disrupted in the first place
- These satellites probably create a lot of clutter that interfere with astronomy. As a result, SpaceX is implementing upgrades to StarLink satellites by reducing their brightness during operation.
- Similar to addressing concerns from the 170 foot asteroid, these satellites will de-orbit at the end of their life and will avoid collisions based on tracking data Communicating your Inner Space Recent visit of Narendra Modi, India’s Prime Minister, to the United States is a solid representation of Symbolism and Status. Symbolism communicates the brand of this relationship and the success is defined in the narrative through joint commitment between the two nations on topics like Jet engine design, semiconductor production, combined work on AI/ quantum computing platforms and other trade commitments for aerospace and defense.

Measure of your personal brand is a function of how you symbolize your brand presence through awesomeness, body language, communications and dapper (remember these ABCDs). This certainly needs to be complimented by your content. Value narratives of your work products combined with how you stylize these representations with your finesse makes your brand holistic.

Responding to a Snafu As I was watching the live stream of BBC news on my flight, I came across yet another topic. A BBC reporter was providing coverage on Glastonbury music festival talking about Elton John, Arctic Monkey, Guns N’ Roses and others. A watcher possibly under the influence of certain substances walks into the conversation and uses profanity. Both the BBC reported and the show host immediately were not stunned, remained calm and apologized even though what transpired was not in their control. Do you symbolize your brand with narratives and dapper? Do you keep calm after a snafu and recover ground?

title: Machines That Are Tipping Physical Businesses
content:
We continue seeing physical businesses that are disrupted by Robotics, AI, and IoT. One such thing is a fully automated robotic kitchen. Combining thermal sensors, IoT, AI recognizing actions and ingredients in chef recipes, and Robotics, these robotic chefs recreate chef’s gourmet recipes. Many of these chefs will receive royalty for machines using their recipes, like in the music industry. New recipes can also be created from machine learning of the popular recipes.

In another use case, the number of driverless cars are increasing on the roads especially in the Bay Area. We now see many driverless cars from Cruise (General Motors owned) and Waymo (Google owned). As these traditional businesses are getting tipped over by machines, are we going to start tipping ($$) the machines as they provide us services?

A Players Do you ever feel that you are not learning enough at work?

It is said that we are an average of five people we spend the time with.

Also, we are now exposed to more content than ever before. Our proximal development is now sped up by it in addition to the people around us. We often feel that we need not rely on peers and leaders to learn as we can find answers online when there is a question.

This element is further accelerated when you don’t need to look up for content as with the Large Language Models (LLMs) the answers come to you.

Separately, we should have many ‘A players’ around as all of you have been through a very comprehensive selection process. Being an ‘A player’ is not just being intellectually genius. It also involves the ability to influence, coach and partner with others to create outcomes, including leveraging the digital machines above.

Here are some questions to answer, am I surrounded by ‘A players and machines’? Am I an ‘A player’? What do you think?

title: Creating Products in Outer Space
content:
"Micro-" means "very small." Microgravity is where the pull of gravity is not very strong. A company named ‘Rocket Lab’ designs and builds Photon spacecraft to provide power, communications, propulsion, and attitude control for Varda Space Industries (Another company intending to manufacturing products in the outer space) 120 kg in-space manufacturing satellite which will produce high-value products in zero-gravity and return them to Earth in a re-entry capsule.

Manufacturing in microgravity enables low cost and high precision creation of

- High quality crystals which are used for drug development by pharmaceuticals industry
- Uniform high quality fibers used for Fiber Optics and
- Bioprinting 3-d structures of cells for creation of complex organs without the influence of gravity Creating Outer Space for the Growth Mindset Distracting gravitational forces from unnatural outlets like social media, OTT platforms or water coolers often impact personal productivity. I am determined to spend at least half of my weekend for personal development through watching podcasts, reading, learning new skills and writing objectively. Getting into microgravity enables astronauts to push objects that weigh in hundreds of pounds.

How do you find microgravities for your acceleration? Do you?

title: Monopoly, Threads and Competition
content:
Monopoly is a situation where there are no close substitute offerings for a product or a service. You saw that with Microsoft Windows, Google Search, perhaps even a local electricity company or a national railway agency.

Differentiating and Competing with Threads Comparatively, you have Facebook, YouTube, Whatsapp, Instagram, TikTok, SnapChat, Pinterest, Reddit, Linked, Twitter and many others representing a sea of offerings as competitive social/professional media for the individuals. To add to this wonderland, Meta recently announced its Threads platform and you would wonder why.

- Twitter is working to limit number of views of its platforms in order to avoid harvesting of its data sets so it turns out to be a an opportunity for a new player
- At the same time, to be a player in the LLM game, you need a platform rich of textual (language) data which neither Facebook nor Instagram is
- Using Twitter as it competes with traditional media and enables citizen journalism, Threads will become another player
- Threads can remain more agnostic of fake news compared to the other social media platforms Of course, there is a wave of subscribers that jumped on to meet this new kid on the block. Whether it offers a new differential value or not is another question that will only be answered with time. Differentiating and Competing with ChatGPT Similarly, ChatGPT had a reduced number of users and usage in the recent past. Has the excitement for the new kid on the block faded, are there more competitive players and there isn’t much of a differential offering or is it that given the summer holidays students who used OpenAI/ChatGPT, reduced the usage. ChatGPT has just about 4 Million paying subscribers and of these only 500,000 users use non-prompt based interface to invoke API calls in OpenAI.

Mind you, a lot of advances in physical sciences, healthcare (creation of new drugs), and making software development autonomous will be some of the significant transformations with API based usage of the LLMs to provide a differential value and offering. As such the number of ‘consumer’ use cases will reduce while increasing the consumption in the ‘enterprise’ businesses, which is a big success criteria for a software business.

Differentiating and Competing with Self This raises a question as an individual: is there competition that I deal with - what is my resilience to the perceived competition. What is my differential offering? Be like a racehorse, put the blinders on to not look sideways and aim for your goal. Play to win like a Golf player, make your next shot better than the previous one and create a better version of you.

title: The Science of Creating Art (Meta's CM3leon)
content:
Earlier last week Meta announced CM3leon (Chameleon), a transformer-based autoregressive model designed for multi-modal tasks, specifically for text and image generation. Let’s examine in depth

- Transformer based means neural network architecture converts one input form to another. Text to Image for example
- Multi-modal in this context means supporting many modes: converting text to image, text guided image editing (example: add sunglasses to a person), or asking questions about the image (example: explain the architecture of construction shown)
- Autoregressive model predicts pixels of an image based on past pixels. CM3leon Model was trained with licensed shutterstock images and associated text data. Other models include Diffusion and GAN (Generative Adversarial Networks) models
- Diffusion model creates a solid image as a mix of noise and pixels such that noise is removed one pixel at a time
- GAN (Generative Adversarial Networks) model is where 2 AIs compete - one is rendering for perfection and other is determining if the image is fake till the time AI that is detecting fakeness cannot claim so anymore
- CM3leon will compete with the likes of Stable Diffusion, Dall-E (from OpenAI) and Midjourney which are other prompt & AI based image management tools Partners in the Ecosystem Microsoft announced availability of Meta’s OpenSource Llama 2 models along with an array of offerings from Huggingface (OpenSource platform with 120k models) and OpenAI’s language models. If Microsoft has enabled OpenAI why would it enable access to Meta’s LLMs?

Like our datacloud, it is building a frenemy in Meta to enable increased usage of Azure platform where it can now converge LLM providers, NVIDIA and the Azure cloud.

Partners in our Ecosystem As partners in our ecosystem, we often gather leaders across functions. Earlier last week, our IT and finance leadership team got together for a team building event. We will host our India Sales leadership team in the near future at the Pune office for their H2 planning event and be able to co-mingle with them. There is no perspective of frenemies here - we are here to serve a single purpose of marching towards a $10B goal.

Inspiring Machines from Arts An artist is inspired from another artist when she creates a piece of art whether it is a picture, sculpture or music. A lawsuit filed against the image creation AI companies did not hold its weight on the grounds that why would a machine similarly not be inspired if it used art only for the reference purpose. Inspiring (Wo)men from Smarts Often we see an individual or a leader as an artist at its best. It is really okay to reference a leader’s style to inspire yourself as long as you do not become a stereotypical copy or a fake, in some cases to ensure that you are not following footsteps blindly. Use all the good things as a reference to hit a good next shot in your golf life. Do you?

title: Superconductivity and the Semiconductor Mix
content:
Before we jump into what is new this week, let’s review some key definitions:

Conductors: Those substances through which an electric current flows are called conductors (Copper, Aluminium and Gold) and through which an electric current cannot flow are called insulators (Wood or Plastic) Semiconductors: Comparatively, Semiconductors used in Integrated Circuits are smaller, faster and cheaper alternatives used in electronic circuits and conduct electricity more than glass or plastic and less than Copper or Aluminium Superconductors: A superconductor is a material that can conduct electricity with no resistance. Superconductors, unlike conductors, do not release heat and do not experience any loss of electricity in transit. Essentially electrons do not collide with atoms of the material during the transit of power. Here is what transpired earlier this week. For the first time in world history superconductor behavior was synthesized, by research scientists in South Korea, at room temperature and at ambient pressure from a modified lead-apatite (LK-99) structure. Why is this important?

- Past experiments indicate that Superconductors necessitated both very low temperatures and very high pressures for seamless superconductivity
- Earlier to this discovery, there was also an experiment that proved superconductivity at room temperature using hydrogen sulfide and yttrium super-hydride, however it necessitated application of tremendously high pressure for this superconductivity
- This breakthrough takes us closer to creation of magnetic field for building at scale magnetic levitation based frictionless trains, superconductor based powerful microprocessors and infinite battery storage
- Imagine how much acceleration this will provide in our respective AI journeys, Quantum Computers (which require ultra cool surroundings today to minimize impact of heat on the quantum states from being destroyed) and to never be able to charge your batteries Between exponential accelerations in AI, Computing, Connectivity, Data Transfer and now Power, we will get many steps closer to dealing with environmental crisis De-Unionizing from the Mix for Superconductivity
- Unions were built in yesteryears to create monopoly of labor against monopoly of businesses
- Recently, many of the actors and story writers have become ‘strikers’ against the adoption of AI based tools. Not choosing to co-exist with these tools will push the studios and owners to adopt the tools even more so
- While the governments are working with the tech companies to build responsible AI solutions, we need not wait for the king to announce to stop the tides
- This is the time to de-unionize the mind from the resisting the new age tech and to be obsessed with something that you can give 60 to 70 hours of your week, very specifically focusing on self-development
- Your reference bar is not around the worst person who hasn’t been impacted but the best person who has been impacted. Create a mindset that is exponential (10X) and not referential. Do you?

title: Technology Macros: Then, Now and Next
content:
Technology Macros: Then, Now and the Future About a decade ago, the combined Market Capitalization of Facebook (~$100B), Apple (~$425B), Amazon ($140B), Netflix (~$20B), Microsoft ($280B) and Google (~$300B) was valued at ~$1.2 Trillion. Today Meta (~$800), Apple (~$2.86T), Amazon (~$1.43T), Netflix (~$191B ), Microsoft (~$2.44T) and Google (~$1.62T) amass Market Capitalization in the order of ~$9.3 Trillion.

Comparatively, in the last 10 years, the United States’ GDP grew from $13T to $26.2T and India’s GDP grew from $1.85T to $3.75T (estimated).

As you can see, the technology sector with the 6 companies in perspective, grew disproportionately by a factor of ~8 compared to the GDP growth by a factor of ~2. This represents 17.5% annual growth of technology businesses for 10 years in a row.

In the duration of Covid years, largely from mid of 2020 up until mid 2022, technology and countries experienced massive boost from the free money flowing into the system through stimulus spends, quantitative easing and very low interest rates. Besides the super high and unrealistic technology valuations, this led to a surge in how crypto currencies and digital assets like NFTs were appreciated. Last 18 months changed the game. Between the Ukraine-Russia war, rising inflation, rising cost of fuel prices, supply chain crisis and rising interest rates, the liquidity tightened significantly. As a result, as witnessed, a lot of technology companies are restructuring or doing down rounds and the free money is going away. Many of the technology company valuations, which were valued at 15-18 times the revenue in mid 2020, saw a valuation drop to 3-4 times the revenue, at this point.

As we look into the future, major technology players will continue to prosper and possible valuations can grow to be 2.5 or 3 times in the next decade (from $10T to $25T-30T). As the valuations continue to grow, the players and the underlying enablement of technology platforms will go through a series of changes enabled by AI, Data, Compute, Power Efficiency and other Platform leverages.

Recently, I discussed 16 leadership development factors with my management team. One of the key perspectives that I expect leaders (and all the individuals) to be aware of is ‘Macro/Industry trends’. It is imperative that we stay abreast of the modern technology stack, the data cloud, LLM shifts, leverage of LLMs on the new data sets, impact of compute (GPUs and Quantum Computing), strengthening our Security posture, Power Efficiencies, and evolution of Technology Macros in each of the Industry Verticals. These narratives will help us drive our strategies, thought leadership and representation of our brands/identities as individuals.

We also need to understand a Macro view of us as an individual, to know our past, our present and the trajectory into the future. Do you?

title: Youth and the Economies
content:
In honor of the recently celebrated ‘Youth Day’ (August 12th), I am making an unusual or rather brave attempt to analogically compare youth with the economies.

Certain economies (nations) live by the day and some think of the long term and invest in the future. Fiscal spendings of many of the first world countries is very high in terms of what it takes for pension payouts, health care of the aging population, interest on debt, investments in infrastructure and defense. All these added together are big amounts representing 40%+ of the expenditures compared to the total GDP (Gross Domestic Product). Many of these countries have accumulated debts which can be anywhere between 1.5 to 3 times their GDP.

Let’s draw an analogy. You as an individual generate a certain value as a personal GDP from earnings (direct and indirect) and there is potentially an obligation to repay for your loans, installments to return money to your parents/families and other interest payments. Sum of the outstanding loans represent what you owe as a proportion of dues/debts compared to what you make (similar to the stated debt above). Total debt usually can be a lot more compared to annual earnings and as a result there is a big portion of your income that is used to pay interest on your personal debts.

Recently, one of the agencies (Fitch) downgraded the rating for the United States to AA+ from AAA. Rating is usually driven from the government’s smooth functioning, fiscal responsibilities, interest rates (high is bad), and debt/gdp ratio. Many factors that softly define these measures include aging population, engineering and entrepreneurism posture, freedom of speech, defense spending and yield on the government issued bonds, which determine confidence of domestic and foreign investors of the nation. This will deviate many of the foreign economies from investing in the government bonds to possibly making investments in ventures. Entrepreneurship and Innovation posture of the US still remains to be strong. I have a broader view of certain disadvantages and possible positive outcomes for the US in a separate coverage so DM me on that note if you are interested.

This one rating drop from AAA to AA+ is not going to slow the US down. Additionally, there are still 9 other countries that have a AAA rating.

So similarly, what does a youth do to be resilient to the forces of uncertainty:

- 
- 
- 
- 
- Maintain a solid entrepreneurial posture as individual and communicate effectively Make long term fiscal and intellectual investments Youth needs to crank good stuff through innovation and focus, savage like Persistence is the biggest predictor of success, slay it 25 year old who is well informed with perseverance can make really big dents, so be woke and glow up
- Be the one that leaders will venture into So, Are you AAA? Do you have endorsements? Do you know your personal rating?

title: The Nine AAA Nations
content:
Few inquisitive souls asked me for the list of 9 countries that still hold the AAA credit rating. They are Germany, Denmark, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, Switzerland, Luxembourg, Singapore and Australia, with 7 based in Europe in addition to Singapore and Australia. This week, I am pulling together dimensions of Geopolitics, Macroeconomics and Technology representing shared intricacies between them.

Global order for Geopolitical Warfare, Economies and Digitalization in today’s world is led by different entities.

Geopolitical Warfare Order: 5 decades back the power of warfare resided primarily between the United States and USSR. After the collapse of the USSR, the United States remained to be the powerhouse for quite some time. In recent years, following China’s rise both in economic stature and warfare, many of the Asian countries are becoming stronger allies of the United States and are increasingly dependent on the United States. Similarly, following the Russia-Ukraine war the NATO driven EU countries are similarly becoming increasingly dependent on the United States. Defense/Wars have increased our dependence on technology to support Telecommunications (remember, the Starlink reference in Ukraine), cybersecurity and technology enabled weaponry.

Macroeconomic Order: This posture is multipolar compared to the bipolarity of the arms race illustrated between the US’ ability to support wars globally and China’s dominance in Asia. For the last few decades the US and China were interdependent for the supply chain and economic growth. Following disinflation (which can be a far bigger economic curse than inflation) and other macroeconomic shifts the today’s world has many players that challenge the economic positions of the top 2 economies of the US and China. Between many of the big EU countries, Japan and the emergence of India, the Macroeconomic power remains distributed and will continue staying subdivided to be in a multipolar state. This renders a leaderless Macroeconomic posture for the coming decades.

Digitalization Order: This is a very unique aspect which is not run by big economies but by companies, many of which have much larger market capitalization than the GDP of many countries. If the companies build alliances with specific countries then their ability to compete in the global markets will dilute and the world will remain digitally divided and isolated. There is a staggering amount of world power that sits in the algorithms of these companies whether they drive AI based platforms, social media platforms or healthcare based innovations. Our world cannot afford this subdivision to deal with the crisis that the planet will face from weather and virus based threats.

Looking back at the Macroeconomic view, is it really good for the world to be multipolar with a leaderless posture? I think so. Here is an analogy to draw. Since we practically diluted the HQ mindset of Snowflake, the geographic and functional subdivisions of the company have a great ability to act in independence for all of us to think and build the HQ mindset in our being. Similarly for many countries, challenges of over-dependence on manufacturing, energy and technology powerhouses will reduce significantly. So moving from countries, to companies and to individuals, how does this apply to each of us? Are you super-dependent on a few people for intellectual and knowledge nourishments? You can craft time out to nourish your mind, body and intellect to become increasingly independent. Do you?

Thoughts provoked through Courtesy of one of 2023’s top 10 Ted Talks:

https://youtu.be/uiUPD-z9DTg?si=LekKXXZLlGZ66paW

title: Why Jackson Hole Moves Markets
content:
Not sure how many of you registered that there was a mention of ‘Jackson Hole’ briefing at our company meeting, the outcome of which strongly ties to our stock price and global markets. Jackson Hole meeting is an annual event where central bankers, policy makers, economists and academicians from around the world get together to discuss various economic and financial topics.

Jerome Powell, Chairman of the Federal Reserve in the United States, shared his points of view in this meeting.

Recently, We have seen a series of interest rate hikes in the United States, which have been the basis of driving the inflation to below 2%, which Jerome Powell addressed as a big objective. Why does this matter?

- Posture of many of the large economies has shifted from Quantitative Easing (introduced to increase liquidity in Covid era and even prior to that) to Quantitative Tightening. QE to QT
- During the pandemic, inflation/prices rose up to record levels as due continuous available of monies and low availability of resources
- We saw continuous increase in interest rates globally to tame this inflationary economy
- There is something called Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Inflation which excludes all of our expenses like goods (cars, electronics), housing costs (mortgage interest) and non-housing services (transportation, healthcare, and rents). PCE inflation declined from 5.3 to 4.2% in July, which is a good news although not good enough
- Housing demand and demand for goods have come down as the interest rates continued to increase. Non-housing services take a little lag to slow down as there are components like leases which don’t align with the cycles right away
- To ensure that inflation levels of 2% is obtained compared to 4.2% in July, interest rates will need to rise further
- Fed is not clear if this will lead to further tightening (increasing rates) or keep it to the current levels
- The determination will only happen once we have sustained PCE inflation to a desirable level
- As a result, stock markets in developed economies will remain uncertain which is evident in prices of some stocks, including ours What makes certain companies like NVIDIA and possibly us in the future as outliers despite the economic headwinds?
- This again is a function of demand and supply, just as a qualified demand for a high quality scarcely available supply
- Exceptional demands are generated from exceptional performers, which are rare to find
- Taking it at an individual’s level, Are you one of them?

title: OpenAI's Road to a Billion
content:
AI has come back in the spotlight again this week.

OpenAI started with a big bang and had a slow down during the summer months from reduced consumption. In the ‘up ride’ again, it is now tracking to get to a $1B annual revenue. Announcement of chatGPT Enterprise version will offer yet another growth avenue. History of time tracks that success for most software organizations is driven through consumption by the enterprises.

On a separate note, Tesla announced the launch of its supercomputer built with 10,000 NVidia H100 GPUs, which will be one of the most powerful machines in the world. If you can imagine the feed from 8 cameras on the outside and 1 from the inside for each of its cars (Model 3 and Model Y). The video capture from 9 cameras from more than 2 million vehicles will create an enormous dataset worth 200 PetaBytes, a lot larger with an LLM context. Use of this dataset will certainly further strengthen FSD (Full Self-Driving) capabilities.

Lastly, we all have seen and realized that AI generates a lot of deepfakes leading to copyrights infringement, fake news and many more adversaries coming into play. AI companies are responding to these challenges by building techniques to introduce watermarks. Challenge with the traditional overlaid watermarks is that they can be cropped or edited for removal. In the newest innovation SynthID, by Google, creates a watermark using 2 neural networks. One network takes the original image and produces an identical image with a few pixels subtly modified to introduce the watermark, which is invisible to the naked eye. Second neural network is used to determine the presence of the watermark or not. Other AI companies are introducing similar capabilities for watermark creation and detection.

Now imagine the pace at which the changes are happening and how we are able to keep up with them, or not. This week, at my staff meeting I have proposed a topic for group discussion titled ‘PACE it or face it’. For me PACE (Performance, Attitude, Culture and Excellence) represents both the individual and team aspects to represent Performance and Attitude for us as individuals; Culture and Excellence as outcomes for the teams from top Performances and Attitudes of individuals. We will have a conversation at length and hope that all of us can resonate this as we continue building the rocket ship. Ask your manager (in IT) or me to know more.

title: G7, G20 and the Developing World
content:
G7, established since 1975, as an organization is made of seven large economies that include Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom and the United States. To take into consideration the interest of developing countries G20 was created in 1999 which just got upgraded to G21 to include the African Union, under the leadership of Narendra Modi, India’s Prime Minister. G21’s agenda is to understand and track challenges/opportunities related to the economy, population, culture, population growth and environment of the member countries. North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) was formed much earlier in 1949 as a political and military alliance between 30 countries based in North America and Europe to counterbalance cold war based threats from the Soviet Union and its allies.

To keep the economic and diplomatic interests of developing nations, BRIC was formed in 2001 as an alliance between Brazil, Russia, India and China. South Africa joined this group in 2010 and recently 6 more nations (Argentina, Egypt, Ethiopia, UAE, Saudi Arabia and Iran) are proposed to join this group.

Here are some salient characteristics of the new BRICS organization with 11 member countries

- 
- They can coordinate opportunities and challenges related to food, oil, currency trade to reduce dependence on US dollar and trade routes
- This alliance is more of economic interest than warfare related contrary to the NATO formation
- As much as dollar based trade independence is aimed for, most of the BRICS countries that conduct business with the US will operate with the terms defined by United States
- It is hard to bring peace as an underpinning as some of the nations have a strong weaponry posture in addition to the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war
- All the original 5 BRICS countries are part of G21 alliance The India narrative
- India’s was #13 in the GDP ranking at an absolute value of $493B in 2001 to where it is at ~$3.5T in 2023
- India is the world’s 5th largest economy so it should ideally be a part of the G7 grouping
- India is on a high growth mode to become the 3rd largest economy of the modern world It is also leading the G21 alliance
- India for years has not had challenges or worries from domestic civil wars and economic strifes
- The posture for India is shifting from notions of economic, civic and government insecurities of a developing nation to representing independence and sovereignty of the state similar to that of any developed nation This clearly articulates the growth narrative, global positioning and a positive shift of India as a nation. So what is your growth story? Do you have one?

title: The Premise of Energy
content:
Energy has been a root cause of economic growth and of many wars. Non-renewable sources have, if not depleting, have been impacting the environment in a big way. This necessitates significant innovation in both consumption efficiency of non-renewable energy sources and creation of renewable energy from solar, wind, and nuclear technologies.

Renewable Geothermal Energy Supply A big new research underway is in leverage of geothermal energy. In the United States companies like Fervo Energy have initiated research to tap into clan, abundant power of geothermal resources using drilling techniques adapted from the oil & gas sector.

- Traditional geothermal plants tap into natural hot water reservoirs underground to convert heat energy to electrical energy. However, hot water reservoirs don’t exist in abundance
- What exists is geothermal heat in hot and dry rocks below earth’s surface so now we can drill into these rocks and create geothermal reservoirs by pumping water which can heated through fracking and produce geothermal energy
- This energy can be provided 24x7 unlike solar and wind sources and emits no greenhouse gasses
- More research is required to reduce the cost to scale this method, ensure that this gets state sponsorship from countries around and to understand the impact on water usage and to avoid earthquakes triggering from drilling activities
- This innovation is a natural extension of oil & gas companies for them to be participants Efficiencies of Electric Vehicles (Non-renewable Energy Sources) Over the years technology companies have continuously increased efficiencies and reduced costs of their offerings. Snowflake has done similarly. It is possible only when the total markets, customer base, market share, and innovative offerings continue to increase. This way individual customers/consumers benefit while the companies grow as a whole.

Tesla’s model S price reduced from $105,000 to $75,000 and model X price reduced from $121,000 to $80,000 between the start of the month and 1st September. This happens alongside when there are continuous hardware and software improvements. They continue to innovate to get closer to FSD (Full Self-Driving).

- General Motors market cap is worth $45B for generating $169B annual revenue, delivering 692k cars in Q2 2023 and from generating $2.5B in net profit for the same quarter
- Comparatively, Tesla’s market cap is worth $800B for generating $90B annual revenue,delivering 466k cars in Q2 2023 and from generating $2.1B in net profit for the same quarter GM from making more revenue and selling more cars is still about 6% of Tesla’s market capitalization. Whether this is an anomaly for the growth potential or an irony, only time can tell. Takeaway Brands, growth mindset & cultures do matter in creating new markets, new products and acquiring new customers. Are you attached to such a brand? Are you proud?

title: IPOs and Direct Selling
content:
We all have seen IPOs (Initial Public Offerings) of companies or are greatly aware of Snowflake’s IPO at the least.

IPOs involve a regulated process where usually an underwriting bank helps issuing new shares of a company to raise capital, equivalent to 15-20% of company’s value.

Direct selling allows shareholders to sell their shares directly to the public without raising new capital and is not as stringent regulatorily. Some companies that were listed through direct selling include Slack, Spotify and Coinbase.

IPO Listings Then and Now Number of IPOs in 2023 so far has been about 600 equivalent to a market capitalization of $60B compared to 2400 IPOs in the full year 2021 equivalent to a market capitalization of $450B. Not only the number of IPOs have halved ytd but also the valuations have dropped to almost a quarter of what the market looked like in 2021.

We saw 3 tech IPOs in the US last week for Arm, Instacart and Klaviyo raising just about $5.5B in totality for less than 10% for each of the company’s valuation.

At the face of it, IPOs sound and look great, however the long term performance of the company is a much better yardstick then what happens at the IPO. Long term performance, working against adverse market conditions and growth through new product/customer baseline will define the future. More than 99% of Apple, Google and many other technology companies’ valuation growth is a reflection of the long term business’ performance.

What does it mean for us?

With these thoughts, I want to ask some questions and look forward to your responses. 1. How have you seen growth of products and customer acquisition in (y)our business? 2. Which product features and industry verticals do you feel stoked about? 3. In which instances, do you see the work that you do directly/indirectly connects to product ideas, customer acquisitions or even offering scale to support a $10B (or equivalent in your world) revenue business 4. Are there other places in your exposure that demonstrate 10x thinking and growth?

title: The 3 Gs of a 200-Year Life
content:
3 Gs of Pathway to ‘200 years’ long life: ‘Gut Health’, ‘Gene Therapies’ and a ‘Good Diet’ Gut Health

- Our body is made up of 10 trillion cells and there are about 10-40 trillion cells in your gut biome (intestine)
- Gut Biome is a bacterial ecosystem similar to a rainforest filled with a range of plants, animals and other living beings
- Lot of the human diseases including diabetes, anxiety, energy levels and some more unknowns are a reflection of your gut health
- ‘Supergut’ (www.supergut.com, use as an academic reference and not as advertisement) consumption can increase the count of good bacteria and shrink bad bacteria to improve overall gut health
- You can also transplant a gut biome representation of a healthy individual to another human using a treatment called Fecal Microbial Transplant (FMT), which is a transplant through feces of a healthy human
- As predicted, in another 10 years more research around gut health will significantly improve not just defense mechanism to a lot of diseases but also lead to a much longer life Gene Therapy
- Gene therapy is a medical technique that uses genes (good living viruses) to treat or prevent diseases by modifying or replacing faulty genetic material
- 3 distinct eras of medicinal treatment a. First era was replacing chewing of leaves for certain illnesses with specific chemical compounds from the leaves in the form of medicinal intake through swallowing of pills or intravenous infusions. These medicines essentially are absorbed by the bloodstream in the GI tract. It then affects the shape and function of the proteins in the body to reduce the symptoms of a disease b. In the second era chemicals are replaced by the protein itself in a specific form and a shape to give a therapeutic cure. These are either enzyme treatments or antibody therapies. Humira to treat arthritis is an example with a market of about $21B c. ‘Gene Therapy’ - In the third era, with a lot of research in progress, living medicines are introduced using viruses, cells and bacteria. With this you can limit the impact in a single location of your body, contrast to something like Chemotherapy where your whole body feels like trash. We can engineer circuits like behavior from these ‘living viruses’ based on certain conditions for precise delivery of treatment Good Diet While these treatments evolve, we can maintain a healthy well balanced diet in moderation and take care of our mental and physical well being through meditation and exercise. Eat and sample what works for you and solve it for yourself, largely leveraging non-processed foods What tips and tricks do you use to maintain a healthy lifestyle in the form of physical exercises, mental wellbeing and a healthy diet? Share your thoughts here

title: A Year of ChatGPT, and Multimodality Arrives
content:
Time Flies: We are close to marking the first anniversary since ChatGPT 3.5 was released and 25th anniversary of Google launch. So much has advanced in both the phases.

AI/LLM Multimodality now a Reality: Sam Altman, founder of OpenAI, announced multimodality and as a result your ChatGPT can now see, hear and speak. Voice capabilities are available on iOS and Android. Also, OpenAI has incorporated DALL.E 3 to create images within the native OpenAI web interface. Google Bard similarly for multimodality can connect to google flights, gmail, google docs and google services to provide a holistic experience as a Bard based Personal Assistant.

Adobe, Canva, Meta and many other technology-enabling companies are making similar improvements to support multimodality of inputs and outputs.

Smart Devices and LLMs: Cell phones have evolved to begin with mobile voice communications, to short text messages, browsing the internet and to where we are at in a ‘App’ based digital economy. OpenAI is now interested in developing a hardware project to create a more natural way to interact with AI, with a $1B in funding from Softbank and design inputs from Jony Ive, who designed the iPhone. This can potentially replace the main smartphone interface with voice or visuals. Imagine just ordering an Uber or your food with pure play voice instructions compared to the current app based interface. Fast Forwarding LLMs: Andrej Karpathy, a computer scientist and ex-AI leader at Tesla, shares a perspective that LLMs are shifting to a kernel-like representation beyond chatbots, representing a new type of Operating system. LLMs will soon represent a new type of driver similar to an operating system sitting on top of the computing infrastructure.

LLMs from the traditionality of chatbot interface are becoming multimodal for inputs and outputs, have code interpretation ability to write and run software programs, access the internet and support embeddings for both, prompting and referencing a data set.

LLMs personified with Snowflake: It is now possible to create an NVIDIA based GPU compute pool, loading of tuning data inside Snowflake to pre-tune/pre-train using an out-of-the-box LLM using SnowPark Container Service.

An additional SnowPark Container Service enables prompting and prompt learning using the embeddings of the above mentioned dataset.

A chat based UI can be built on Streamlit to interface with the pre-tuned/pre-trained data set enabling continuous learning from the data set.

A Narrative View: I plan to use the above contextual framework in my narrative at the LLM executive panel at the DCWT in Mumbai later this week. If you have created a thought story framework in your past or if you are now inspired to create something similar then I will look forward to hearing from you.

title: Sanctions and Relationships
content:
Have you been a big brother or a big sister or a parent?

In any of these situations, we tend to dictate and impose behavioral expectations. We set expectations on what should or should not be done? Similarly, powerful nations tend to impose sanctions on dependent nations.

In either of the scenarios, whether as a human or a nation, you are creating a submissive relationship for the dependent entity and limiting their potential.
