In a multipolar world where there are many influencing forces, whether being an individual or a nation in this context you will critically limit your own growth potential.

Organizations work similarly and we need to work to avoid these pitfalls. Being a people leader, it becomes significantly critical to build trust based relationships, empowered mindset built with authentic engagements.

A video (Link at the bottom) I saw recently shares that mammals’ mental and physical well being is a function of attachment (to others) and in being authentic.

Message and the Messenger Now being a leader necessitates yet another attribute. We tend to get too opinionated about who said what as opposed to what was said. What this really implies is for the leaders (actually all of us) to focus very strongly on the message to understand what it meant irrespective of who said it and how it was said. Get your Leaders to hear your Message(s) Another video, which I shared with my leadership team, speaks about how to effectively communicate with the leaders. Sometimes, when I meet with individuals and teams, I hear about perspectives on what cafeteria food options should be, whether we should increase meal allowances or provide transportation to the workplace.

We have a very talented workplace orgranization that is very aware of the opportunities and nuances of our collective wellbeing. Your ability to share and pick valuable insights in interactions with leadership will help both, us as individuals and organization, to accelerate our respective journeys. This talk (Link at the bottom) encapsulates 5 critical leadership communications techniques. Here is the nutshell: 1. Escape the minutiae - leadership is not interested in detail orientation or deep implementor's view 2. Exude confidence - demonstrate certainty - grab the seat at the table 3. Execute rainmaking communications - avoid long winded narratives - don’t try too hard to impress 4. Elongate your timeframes - what you will be in 3 years - think really long term - work portfolio over promotion/money - leaders think in form of decades 5. Exercise business acumen - think of their Big Rocks How Being Too Nice Can Destroy You: A Story You've Never Heard with Dr. Gabor Maté 5 Rules for Communicating Effectively with Executives

title: A Pill Instead of Food? (Ozempic)
content:
Have you ever imagined or talked about a pill that could be a substitute to eating food? Here we go.

👍

- Agonists are drugs that activate receptors. Ozempic is an agonist medical drug which increases insulin release and reduces glucose production, and treats type 2 diabetes
- Another byproduct effect of this medicine is hunger suppression that makes you eat less and subsequently lose weight as a result
- We all have heard of 2000 calories worth of daily food intake. Now, magically with this drug imagine you need only 1200 calories worth of food. This drug generates energy to burn your body fat
- All of a sudden this diabetes cure drug becomes a weight loss treatment
- However, the weight loss can come first from burning of body fat and at some point it also starts burning your muscle weight, about 20-40% lean muscle mass burn out
- In a non-medicinal way, weight loss can be accomplished from both a healthy diet and a good exercise regime
- Similar to stopping exercise and healthy diet, stopping the drug intake will also lead to weight gain because your metabolism now needs your 2000 calories back again
- Muscle strength is a function of exercise, healthy diet and protein intake
- Some of the recent improvements have significantly increased the weight loss facilitation with a triple agonist strength
- Over time these medicines become more affordable as the costs reduce significantly
- Older people experience falls especially if the muscle strength has diminished. Weight training and muscle strength get even more critical as we age
- Walmart, largest food retailer in the US, claims that people are purchasing a lot less junk food given the increased use of Ozempic Of course, this is a negative for the economic growth and for Walmart itself
- Perhaps, reduced consumption of high fructose based processed food is a good thing 👍👎 👍 👍 👍 👍 👍 👍 👍 👎 👎 👎 👍 Ultimately, keeping your diet, exercise and a healthy lifestyle is ultimately what will matter 👍

title: Perseverance and the Long-Hours Debate
content:
A lot of professional media was divided, or I must say largely irked with comments on long working hours. Some put a capitalist perspective, and some addressed it with a geographic context, given that some countries limit number of working hours/days.

It got me to think about few of my favourite leaders and here are some perspectives in no particular order. Dominic Gallello, my CEO a few companies back, shared this beautiful quote years ago and it still resonates in my routine life.

“Nothing in the world can take the place of persistence. Talent will not; nothing is more common than unsuccessful men with talent. Genius will not; unrewarded genius is almost a proverb. Education will not; the world is full of educated derelicts. Persistence and determination alone are omnipotent.” – Calvin Coolidge In his just a minute long message, Barrack Obama, erstwhile President of the United States addresses the youth to learn how to get stuff done. There are many of us who are great at explaining problems, why something went wrong or why something can’t be fixed. One must build an attitude to say, ‘let me take care of that’. He/She must learn to handle tasks and get them done. Don’t be impatient, don’t look for a plum assignment and just nail the stuff you are working on. Leaders notice all the good stuff you do. President Barack Obama: Just learn how to get stuff done Many of our leaders also work with great perseverance. Frank, our CEO never misses to address the whole company on Monday mornings with his awe-inspiring message.

I, personally believe in giving my best to the job at hand. Rewards and recognition are by products of my actions and not the goals to begin with.

Your time is yours and you have choices to make above and beyond your baseline job.

Do you think differently? Do you have thoughts or your unique approach for perseverance? I will look forward to hearing from you, on our fridge website, of course.

title: A Tale of Two Tenancies
content:
Traditional business models of Healthcare, Hospitality, Banking, Automotive, Retail are enhanced with Digital Revolution and in many cases challenged by new players like TelaDoc, AirBnB, PayTM, Google-Pay, Tesla, Amazon, Shopify and many others.

There were such opportunities in the tenancy space, one with a commercial office space model initiated by WeWork and other intending to create a global Travel Experiences from AirBnB. Assuming many of you have heard of WeWork readying to file chapter 11 (Bankruptcy), let’s take a look at what transpired.

A long 2+ year tenure during the pandemic resulted in constraints that kept us away from pleasure traveling and of course going to the office.

This is what transpired for WeWork in a nutshell

- WeWork’s business model intended to provide office floors, lounges, desks and meeting rooms on somewhat a la carte lease basis for startups, freelancers and smaller establishments that could not afford significant capital investment
- To lure these businesses, WeWork made significant capital investments, both in establishing long leases with landlords and for buildout of the workspace infrastructure in total of 777 work locations across 39 countries
- Founded in 2010, the company was valued at $47B in 2019 with shares trading over $400. Pandemic dropped the valuation to $9B in 2021 and it tracks at $140M currently with share price dropped to about $1. Pandemic impacted use of corporate offices and a lot more on use of co-working spaces
- The company cannot afford to honor $13B+ annual lease contracts anymore. A significant investment from investors like SoftBank in Japan is valued at pennies to dollars
- Adam Neumann, founder CEO, left the organization and cashed out more than $1.5B upon his exit. WeWork also laid 2,400 employees in the same year In contrast AirBnb has a very powerful narrative
- AirBnB was founded by Brian Chesky along with 2 other co-founders in 2008
- Initially aiming to provide affordable accommodation to conference attendees on air mattresses, the business today accommodates 4+ million people every night in secondary homes, guest rooms and hotel rooms
- As quoted by Brian the company lost 80% of valuation within 8 weeks into the pandemic. More than 2000 employees were laid off
- At IPO without owning any real-estate, Airbnb valuation was larger than combined value of all of Marriott, Hilton and Intercontinental hotels
- Founder’s personification of being a biological parent and to stick around with the business, managing layoffs with empathy, not building a capital intensive business, building an impeccable culture for continuously raising the bar and designing superior customer experiences has enabled Airbnb to still exist as a very successful business In a nutshell, when you look at Brian’s background, he has a fine arts degree in industrial design with a strong belief that creative people can create wonders. As a designer, you design anything from a toothbrush to a space satellite ensuring that your design turns into a very useful product. He is one of the world’s best storytellers, who you can witness in this interview. https://youtu.be/ia6Di_ytiSE?si=LR9XrEDqS5crfdSe He thinks of life goals in 10 year terms and has built products and experiences which are loved by 100s of people as opposed to something that is liked by 10s of thousands. He is obsessed about working with the most creative people in his organization and believes in purpose, health and relationships over climbing the ladder of success. Have you experienced such a mindset in your work life? What parallels do you draw? Do share your thoughts.

title: Recharge, Rejuvenate, Reinvent
content:
Recharge, Rejuvenate and Reinvent, Reboot as required Just the fact that Sunday thoughts became Monday thoughts, made me write about this topic. Recharging and Rejuvenating Whether you are still on your leaves or are back reading this note, gives me hope that everyone got a chance to recharge personal batteries and have some fun. Fun does not mean scrolling social media or watching OTT. Fun should be a feeling and not an activity. Fun should be about playfulness, lowering your guard and connecting. Connecting with people and/or the surroundings. Fun is about traveling, building experiences and seeing places. I had a chance to reconnect with many on social front and have more personal travel planned in the next few weeks.

Reinvent and Reboot, as required Recharging and Rejuvenation bring enlightenment from within to come back to the norm so we are then able to Reinvent, along with Rebooting as required.

- Reinventing includes identification of the purpose or reconnecting with the known purpose if it existed prior to recharging
- Everytime I take a break I find new ways of working, thinking of long term aspects, and making personal and professional investments
- Another aspect I realized is that a lot of creative professionals in arts, movies, instructors and trainers work a very small percentage of time. In our profession, quite uniquely and on the contrary, it does not stop us from being creative all the time
- Occasionally, we need a reboot as well. Rebooting of your natural approach should involve listening to your inner voices, stepping outside of your comfort zone and boosting your confidence
- To boost your confidence try and exhibit bravery for a few seconds to initiate a step in the discomfort zone, take a seat at the table to speak your mind, cheer for other people’s success to be more of yourself, enter an arena in an adjacent track for a new skill and constantly celebrate each time after a personal goal is accomplished
- Also introduce humor into your character after reboot, you will become more productive, less stressed and happier Do you know that many people who use humor are paid more?

Getting this job itself was not a victory in 2020, the work attached to it and accomplishments 3+ years later through this incredible team is monumental. This rejuvenation will help me determine the next set of goals to accomplish.

Did you have a chance to Recharge and introspect for Reinvention? Share your thoughts

title: The Week Microsoft and OpenAI Broke the News
content:
A lot happened this week between Microsoft announcements at Ignite ‘23 and Sam Altman, Co-founder CEO of OpenAI along with Greg Brockman, Chairman & Co-founder getting fired from their respective jobs. I was tempted to write more about the OpenAI story however refrained as the events are still unfolding. “पिक्चर अभी बाकी है !” I also realized that the AI/LLM space is unfolding rapidly and before we all get thoroughly intimidated by these I am providing this re-cap and additions as a refresher and an update for those who would like to remain up-to-date.

Generative AI: Word Generative refers to “Generate”. GenAI generates texts, images, lyrics, music and code based on what the model is trained. As a result Language Models are representatives of Generative AI.

SLMs or Small Language Models: are similar to an LLM but operate on a smaller scale. They are trained on less data with fewer parameters, which makes them less complex. They are built on millions of parameters compared to hundreds of billion parameters for LLMs. An example of a small language model is to solve a limited problem like a virtual assistant on a smartphone where there aren’t enough computational resources and the response from the model is fast owing to not many parameters.

RAG or Retrieval Augmented Generation: Here the tasks are performed as a combination of both retrieval and generation. Generation is Augmented to Retrieval. Imagine you are supporting customers as a television company. Usually, for troubleshooting and support you can use data from the published manuals (unstructured data set) and details like patches etc. This information is proprietary to the company so in a RAG model, first you use proprietary dataset to retrieve specific information and augment LLMs to generate coherent responses. As a result, your retrieval mechanism is based on a specific dataset and generation uses widely available LLMs.

Snowflake supercharging the Data Cloud with LLM capabilities: We announced Snowflake Cortex as the ability for our customers (in private preview) to securely tap into the power of generative AI with their enterprise data. This demonstrates our ability to supercharge our customers data cloud with the LLM capabilities

- Using serverless functions, our customers can build contextualized LLM powered applications.
- Cortex incorporates processing of semi-structured and unstructured documents using DocumentAI capabilities, Snowflake Copilot and Universal Search.
- Snowflake Copilot enables users to interact with their datasets using plain text
- Universal search enables LLM powered search to derive value from most relevant data and apps for their use case
- Similar to Retrieval Augmented Generation, DocumentAI extracts content like invoice amounts or contractual terms from documents and fine-tune results using a visual interface and natural language In the near future, I plan to share updates on Vectorization, Co-Pilots, and LLM enhancements. What else would you like to hear about or if you have something else to share of the AL/LLM world, do write back to me?

title: Expect the Unexpected: Iteration Velocity
content:
While working with one my CEOs in the past, we would do two things in preparation of

### presenting to him

- Write all the research and highlight summaries in 80 words or less, with one slide to show: the Problem/Opportunity, the Ask and Potential Outcomes from making the choice or not
- Secondly, after all the preparation, we would always expect the unexpected outcomes and questions from the meetings Did Sam Altman and Greg Brockman expect to be ousted the way they were fired? Without judging whether the firing happened for the known Q* research potentially leading to AGI (Artificial General Intelligence) or any other reason, let’s examine how they reacted to the situation ● First they quickly shared their appreciation for OpenAI employees, followers and wished everyone well
- Their confidence in their own ability to move forward to the next phase did not deter them from what transpired, this is what I like to call Iteration Velocity
- Sam mentioned in one of his tweets that he pretty much saw Eulogy post his death. He rose back like a Phoenix, quickly (https://twitter.com/sama/status/1725742088317534446)
- Microsoft and the OpenAI internal org responded quickly however both, Sam and Greg, did not expect any such reaction. They demonstrated relentlessness Bringing this back home, we cannot control what happens around us however we can definitely control how we respond to adversities. This needs training of your mind and body to have confident stimuli to events that ordinarily shake the ground below.

This whole narrative reminded me of our core Snowflake values

- Between every event and response there is an opportunity to introduce positivity in the space between. Own it,Think Big
- Sam could not control how they thought to determine his exit however at the face of it, he did Embrace Differences of Others
- His commitment to Being Excellent was not a function of life events Do you recall any situations in your life where you were shaken or remained undeterred when all hell broke loose? Looking forward to hearing experiences you have had and consequent learnings.

Beyond this, since the drama at OpenAI distracted us this past week, to make sure we continue to stay up to date, Here are some recent updates from the world of LLMs ● Google announced Bard AI support for deciphering content from youtube videos to answer specific questions

- Anthropic’s Claude 2.1 will support 200,000 tokens equivalent to 500,000 words or 500 pages in the context window, which is an immediate context input that the model considers while it generates output
- Microsoft announced Orca 2 Small Language Model with enhanced reasoning abilities of Large Language Models. Orca 2 is based on 7 Billion or 13 Billion parameters created by fine-tuning corresponding LLAMA 2 models from Meta. Understand that OpenAI’s ChatGPT 4.0 is built on 2T+ parameters which are almost 300 times more than 7B parameters.
- Inflection AI released the Inflection 2.0 model that claims to be second in performance behind GPT 4.0 from OpenAI
- Elon Musk announced availability xAI’s Grok language model based on the content from the X platform to its premium customers

title: Power Needs of the AI Era
content:
We have figured out that to run our Data Centers, Crypto processes, Eclectic Vehicles based on lithium ion batteries, and LLMs based on extensive computing, necessitate a lot of energy consumption largely in the form of burning of fossils. In the coming years, a lot more power will be required to run our planet and the carbon emission from these transformations continue to negatively impact the state of our planet’s health.

Our need for energy continues to grow and we cannot sustain generating energy from nonrenewable sources. As alternative sources of energy, where the sun is shining we should have solar panels and where the wind is blowing we should have the wind power - which are still not enough to sustain our energy needs and definitely necessitate exploration of additional renewable sources of energy.

Energy Abundance through Nuclear Fusion The Sun is powered by a process of fusion in the core where extreme pressure and temperature cause hydrogen nuclei to collide and fuse into helium, releasing a massive amount of energy in the form of light and heat, powering the Sun and providing energy to the solar system. This exact same process is being experimented to create limitless clean energy through extensive research in creation of fusion based energy plants.

- Essentially, the energy produced by fusion is 200 million times per mass used compared to what is produced by a chemical reaction.
- There are 70 fusion companies currently researching to put fusion reactions in a machine to generate nuclear energy.
- Imagine going to a coal site to take out the boiler. Replace the boiler with the fusion machine, turn the switch on to generate heat/energy, no emission and no fossil fuel involved in the process.

A typical process involved injecting fusion fuel (mixture of hydrogen and helium). Compression of fusion fuels increases pressure, density and temperature. At 100 million degrees leading to fusion conditions fuel becomes plasma – the fourth state of matter where electrons are stripped from their atoms, creating a mix of free electrons and ions. This process releases massive amounts of energy.

These companies are still in the research mode and it will be another decade when we will have directions to new sources and material impact from these transformations. The race to cost effective and harmless power generation in abundance is on.

What powers you as an individual and how do you manage to limit toxicity in your space to be carbon neutral if not net negative? How do you remain agnostic of negative space around? Do share your thoughts.

title: The World Bank and the Banks of the World
content:
I was intrigued by a talk from Ajay Banga about his new position as the President of the World Bank which inspired me to capture these thoughts.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vTeJOzCoXO8&t=636s World Bank: World Bank is, both, a financial institution and a knowledge bank. Last year, they managed $120B in lending to low and middle income countries needing help. Working with these countries, the Bank maintains a high level of knowledge and intellect around issues related to the planet’s health, people’s wellbeing, infrastructure needs, and digitalization progress worldwide.

There is a perception today that the world bank is not fit for purpose. As much as the Bank manages huge lending amounts, there has been criticism around how long it takes to execute projects.

International Monetary Fund (IMF): The International Monetary Fund (IMF) is a global financial institution, comprising 190 member countries. The IMF focuses on providing short-term financial assistance to countries facing economic crises, emphasizing macroeconomic policies. It's lauded for providing crucial support during financial turmoil but criticized for imposing strict conditions on loans.

Central Banks: Central banks are crucial in their respective countries' economic policy-making and have significant impact on global financial markets.

The Federal Reserve (Fed), USA; European Central Bank, Bank of Japan, People’s Bank of China, Bank of England and the Reserve Bank of India are some of the largest central banks in the world.

Government Run Banks: Government-owned or state-controlled banks often play a role in implementing government policies, supporting economic sectors, or providing financial services where private banks may be less active.

Private Banks: To my surprise, First 4 of the Top 5 private banks based on total value of the assets managed are based in China. JPMorgan Chase & Co is the number 5 of the list and is the largest bank in the United States. HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank and Kotak Bank are the top 3 private banks in India by asset valuation.

Not losing traction on developments in the AI world, here are some more updates. Large Vision Models: Similar to the introduction of Small Language Models earlier, I want to quickly mention Large Vision Models. Large Vision Models are used to learn from images however to not complicate learnings from vast complexity of images the Vision Models are built to be domain specific. For example most images on the internet are of humans, objects, abstractions and animals. To not confuse these images from subject matter relations, training models solely from domain specific images will immensely help in the field of pathology, circuitry, aerial views and others. Also given the domain specificity the learning will require far fewer parameters (only 10-30%) than a generic image based Large Vision Model would require. Gemini from Google: Google recently announced its Gemini LLM platform in 3 sizes - Ultra, Pro and Nano. These models as announced are built ground up to be multi-modal, to say that they were trained on text, images, voice and video. The Gemini Ultra model as reported outperformed ChatGPT 4.0 on most parameters, although it will not be available for another year. Compared to Dall.E capability in OpenAI, Gemini cannot generate images today. Google’s Bard will start using the Pro model as the LLM base and Pixel phones will leverage the Gemini Nano model.

Apparently the Duck video image drawing interpretation of the bird, the colors and material detection is kind of fake, as claimed by TechCrunch. I am going - What the duck? Domain specific lvms - circuits; pathology; aerial views and general images like on insta and meta = you can save time as lvms act as pre-built templates and require Only 10-30% labelled data Gemini from google deep mind - in 3 sizes ultra, pro and nano - built ground up yo be multi-modal - text, audio, images and video; ultra outperformed gpt 4.0 - although not available for another year; current releases do not generate images unlike dall.e on ChatGPT - no clarification from google about training data ; bard will use Gemini pro and pixel will use Gemini nano

title: Geopolitical Conflicts and the Global Economy
content:
The ongoing conflict between Israel and Hamas in Gaza is marked by continuing Israeli attacks and humanitarian crises, with no imminent end in sight and international calls for a ceasefire largely unheeded. At the same time, there is a dilution in the attention to unending conflict between Russia and Ukraine. These wars led to high impact on energy costs, slowdown of global economies and significant investments that will be required to rebuild war impacted pockets of all the 4 countries involved.

Yet another conflict in sight is China with Taiwan and the potential impacts on the existence of our technology world. Many predict that this war is inevitable within a decade. Let’s examine a bit more.

Neighbor’s Envy The People’s Republic of China considers Taiwan a part of its territory. Taiwan, whose economy is largely defined by electronics, technology and export, maintains its own government and democratic system, with many of its citizens advocating for a distinct Taiwanese identity separate from China.

AI’s Single Point of Failure Semiconductor chips are the most complex mass produced objects that are built by humanity. They require purest metals, most expensive machinery, superhuman talents, and atom level manufacturing-precision. As such, there are two kinds of chip companies.

1. Fabless companies, which design but do not manufacture their own chips. They include AMD, NVIDIA, Qualcomm and many others.

2. Foundries manufacture chips designed by other companies. There are only 3 major chip manufacturers in the world: TSMC, Samsung and Intel.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Limited (TSMC) is the only one that reliably manufactures AI chips with a market cap of $470B+ and is the 13th largest company in the world. It produces far more chips than any other company in the world.

TSMC makes all of the world’s AI chips including NVIDIA GPUs, Google TPUs, AMD GPUS and AI Chips for Microsoft, Amazon, Tesla and a few others Times Magazine describes TSMC as the world’s most important company that you have never heard of. NVIDIA CEO Jensen calls out to say, ‘basically there is air… and TSMC’. TSMC is irreplaceable in a nutshell.

Impacts from such a war China-Taiwan war can paralyze the AI ecosystem. TSMC’s Fabs or chip manufacturing facilities are located on the West of Taiwan, merely 110 miles from the border of China. In addition, The United States has put massive restrictions on export of AI chips to China. Rest of the world is greatly incentivized in protecting Taiwan from China.

TSMC and other chip manufacturing companies in Taiwan cannot work in isolation as they are greatly dependent on algorithms and designs from other software companies around the world. They are also dependent on materials, equipment and services from other parts of the world. China is also greatly dependent on chips that it brings from Taiwan.

As much as recent time wars were super unpredictable, let’s hope that diplomacy prevails in this dispute.

title: Definitions: Monopoly, Oligopoly and More
content:
1. Monopoly/Oligopoly: Oligopoly is defined as a state when market structure is characterized by a small number of large firms. An example is represented in the form of market dominance smartphones by a few companies like Apple, Samsung and Huawei. Similarly, Monopoly is characterized by one company, something like what the Jio business can potentially look like as a primary telecom provider in the country. 2. Antitrust policies refers to regulations and laws that promote competition and prevent monopolies or unfair practices in the marketplace. These largely protect consumers and ensure a fair and competitive market.

### Players & Actors

Large companies that demonstrate monopolistic or oligopolistic behaviors are one set of players.

On the other side, agencies like EU’s Directorate-General for Competition (DG Comp), United Kingdom’s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA), United States’ Federal Trade Commission (FTC) & Department of Justice’s Antitrust Division (DOJ) and lastly India’s Competition Commission of India represent other set of actors who are responsible for monitoring and enforcing antitrust activities.

Let’s also keep in mind the approximate current GDPs of each of these as $18.3T for EU, $3.13T for UK, $23.3T for the United States and $3.18T for India Conflicts Earlier in 2022, about 15 months prior, Adobe announced its intention of acquiring Figma for approximately for a relatively high price $20B in a mix of cash and stock. Prior to the announcement Figma was valued at about $10B.

Regulatory Antitrust related concerns raised by the UK’s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) blocked this deal. Consequently, Adobe agreed to pay a termination fee of $1 billion to Figma.

Even in the past quite a few large mergers were blocked including AT&T and T-Mobile, Telco players in the US, in 2011 and that of Comcast and Time Warner Cable in 2015.

### Questions that arise from here

1. Is it fair for the UK whose GDP is merely 1/8th of the US GDP to deny this merger? 2. Is it alright that it took the UK’s CMA 15 months to come to this recommendation or conclusion?

3. In 2021, the Apple App Store reportedly reached around $85B in revenues from global consumers worldwide, while the Google Play Store saw $47B in revenues from subscriptions and other in-app purchases. Is it fair for these digital stores to make so much revenue by building pre-installed native app stores in the respective Android and iOS platforms?

Do you know of more examples? Looking forward to hearing more from you.

title: Wrapping 2023: The Middle East Saga
content:
Happy New Year to all. Let’s get ready to ring in the new. A lot is going on in the world as we wrap 2023 and here is yet another global conflict in a nutshell.

Expansion of the Middle East saga The Suez Canal is an artificial sea-level waterway in Egypt, connecting the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea through the Isthmus of Suez. Suez canal enables passage of more than 1,500 cargo ships every month, primarily Chinese container ships moving towards Europe and the United States.

To the east of the Suez canal are Saudi Arabia and Yemen. The Houthis emerged in the 1990s as a Zaidi Shia Muslim armed insurgency group primarily in northern Yemen. They represent a significant but unclear portion of Yemen's population.

As a revenge response to Israel’s military campaign in Gaza, Houthis, with a likely sponsorship from Palestine supporting nations, recently started attacking commercial ships in the Suez canal with drones and missiles.

This has not only disrupted the trade routes but also risked the cargo and the lives of the crew on board of the freight ships. This has alarmed the US, the UK, Canada, France and other countries to raise their guards to protect the commerce.

Impact from this War This has increased the insurance cost for these vessels and the ships are now diverted to go from Cape Cod in South Africa, resulting in a 20% increase in time taken and has dampened the commerce.

Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Qatar intend to make the most out of their natural gas in the upcoming decades to build the future of their countries. The US has backed the Saudis in their war against the Houthis.

This also further impacts China’s already marginalized economy and limits their ability to supply goods to the Western world. Europeans will pay a bigger price for the products causing inflationary effects. Remember, Russia is also a supplier of commodities and will be adversely impacted.

What can happen as a result?

Houthis will not stop their attacks unless Israel stops and Israel will not stop till Hamas is eliminated. This can plausibly lead to a larger war beyond Israelis, Palestinians, Houthis and Saudi.

The cost of the war is also asymmetrical in that the drones that attack the cargo ships cost in the order of thousands and the missiles that defend them cost in Millions of dollars. Air Freight is not an option either. A container ship can carry 10,000 40 Feet containers compared to 7 40 Feet containers that fit in a 747, which is one of the largest cargo planes. Yet again, similar to the China and Taiwan conflict that I wrote about, Diplomacy needs to prevail here. A lot is going on. If you would like to catch a glimpse of all that I read and watched over the holidays then drop me a note.

title: Generative AI in 2024
content:
Generative AI was a hot topic for the investors in 2023 and will remain to be so in 2024. Here is a plunge.

Before we start with AI, let’s look back about 80 years before today. We figured out how to harness controlled nuclear fission in the 1940s. Two opportunities came out of it: for generation of energy from nuclear plants and for building nuclear weapons. North Korea has many nuclear weapons today but no nuclear power generation plant. Japan, the only country against which nuclear weapons were used, has many nuclear power generation plants with zero nuclear weapon footprint.

Noteworthy Impacts of Generative AI Similar to Nuclear fission, AI also has both the dimensions of growth acceleration and harm to humanity. Technology for years has created inequality in wealth. AI will not be dissimilar. Although long term productivity consequences of GenAI are still somewhat uncertain, here are some noteworthy gains

- AI has accelerated software development, healthcare, creative, education, legal and customer support businesses to name a few. There is and will be a definite impact on the labor market with these changes
- Cost of services will decrease and the sophistication will increase for these impacted industries. For example in healthcare, you can expect that ordinary citizens may be attended by a virtual or AI machine doctor and specialized in person care will be given to wealthy populations A lot of these gains will need to be paid back as dividends for AI defense.
- While we may know how the LLMs were built, a given response of AI is unpredictable, more or less child-like. AI can create hallucinations. AI should be used as a guide or a supplement as a result, and not an authority
- We will need to increase our security posture to defend against vulnerabilities from cyber attacks
- There are already conflicts that arise from LLM leverage of content from media companies (refer to New York Times vs. OpenAI lawsuit) Of course, a lot of jobs will shift to increase demands in food service, cleaning industries, physical security, entertainment and others. However, these will not be high paying in line with my earlier remark about disproportionality of wealth distribution.

OpenAI’s Valuation Estimates say that OpenAI is valued over $100B and Microsoft has made a great investment as a result. Is the value justified? Let’s examine some more.

- Currently there are many Large Language Models to choose from with both suppliers, model sizes and controlled access for data privacy in perspective
- LLMs, largely OpenAI, make money from consumers who have subscriptions and from enterprises that use LLMs similar to AWS infrastructure
- Actual cost of using millions of tokens in LLMs is still prohibitive
- Latency from AI tools today is still very high to build production ready code
- External API calls from LLMs take easily more than 10 seconds for handshakes and data exchanges, which is relatively inconvenient and unaffordable impacting user experience
- Existing LLM based startups are over-valued This leads to many questions and some that come to my mind are: Do you see more pros and cons from Generative AI? Do you have an opinion on OpenAI’s valuation? Do you believe governments will need to move towards UBI (Universal Basic Income) for the welfare of its citizens?

title: Bitcoin Enters the ETF
content:
Most of us are aware of Mutual Funds. Especially in India from the campaigns around “Mutual Funds Sahi Hai”.

ETFs or Exchange Traded Funds are similar to mutual funds except that they can be traded all through the day like stocks on a typical trading day. An ETF can track an index (for example S&P 500 index), bonds, or even a commodity like Gold.

Recently, 11 Bitcoin ETFs were introduced as part of increasing the regulatory oversight of one specific asset (Bitcoin) in the asset class of cryptocurrency. Bitcoin represents about $1.7T of the digitally traded assets.

- With these ETFs, Bitcoins will now be subject to rules and regulations of the SEC (Securities and Exchange Commission in the US)
- As a big plus, practices around purchasing, storing, and managing the Bitcoins held by the ETFs will be subject to regulatory standards
- Bitcoin investments will now be accessible outside of Crypto Exchanges in the form of ETFs
- This will encourage more inflow of investments and investors in Bitcoin investments, including pension fund accounts based investments
- Underlying currency still remains unregulated and the Bitcoin ETF regulation is a bridge between traditional financial system and Bitcoin as a cryptocurrency
- This will also help somewhat in not having FTX like episodes and build transparency, compliance and investor resource mechanisms I personally believe that this leads to a mindset that acknowledges the significance of crypto assets in larger economies.

AI/LLM part of Life In my earlier post on October 8th, I had referenced Andrej Karpathy’s mention of using LLMs as a kernel or an operating system. The Rabbit R1, the first dedicated LLM agent device, was announced earlier at the CES (Consumer Electronics Show).

### Here is what it claims to do

- You can teach Rabbit AI to take actions on your behalf. They announced Rabbit AI, the first device enabled LAM, termed as the Large Action Model
- You can authenticate to your apps like AirBnB, MidJourney and other applications using the Rabbit interface
- You can train the Rabbit AI in the teach mode to get trained on generating images using your style of prompting on Midjourney or in another example train it to book your accommodation on AirBnB
- As represented, you do not need apps on this device like on a smartphone and the device will provide a multimodal conversational AI/LLM experience
- They are apparently using multimodality of inputs like screen snapshots, audio and users actions in HTML format to take actions
- They also mention that their device will not necessarily replace a smartphone
- Cursory look of the demo tells me that even though the device has a big scroll button and a camera, there isn’t much of local computing happening on the device like in a SLM (Small Language Model)
- Most of the actions seem to be performed on the cloud. There is a good possibility that most of the functionality demonstrated in Rabbit AI can be implemented by building custom GPTs on the OpenAI portal. The device in that case just might be a graphical interface for the actions on the LLM cloud Only time will tell whether this Rabbit version 1.0 is an entryway into LLM kernel based devices or yet another “What the duck?” moment.

Did you see any other smart gadgets from CES? Like always, do share your thoughts.

title: The Rise of Phoenixes
content:
Have you dealt with failures? Have you fallen down and figured out a way to rise again? Ancient philosophy symbolizes the Rise of Phoenixes for renewal and transformation. The philosophy reminds us how new life and hope can emerge to rise from destruction and the ashes of predecessor. Just like the phoenix bird, we too can rise, reinvent ourselves and soar from the challenges we face.

Authentic Associations I thought of this philosophy and felt that this correlation of the Rise of the Phoenix needs reflection with our associations. Quote from Jim Rohn, “You are the average of the five people you spend the most time with” reflecting the impact on social influence and personal development.

As I interact with people, this naturally builds a psychological inclination of associating largely with people who have accomplished things. When I reached out to my team to hear perspectives on “Average of five people”, I got some beautiful responses. While I don’t deny engaging with people who can influence positively in your life with their experiences, there are caveats.

Firstly in this time and age, while you may not have easy access to successful individuals, you do have the ability of access to digitally available wisdom/content. Our favorite topic of LLMs has made life a lot easier to get to that digital wisdom. How you make use of that content in your space will make your journeys seamless.

In addition to looking for Accomplishments, we also need to look for Authenticity. Speaking about authenticity and people in combination, if you have individuals, irrespective of their experiential success, who can call things out and help you to rise from the ashes like the phoenix bird then they are your keepers. Authenticity is not a function of age or experience, it is purely a reflection of one’s fabric and the mindset.

‘My Role’ for Others Besides associating with authentic and accomplished people to raise your average, what role you play in the life of others is equally important. This similarly is not a function of your age or your experience but of your authenticity.

Sometimes people who are in leadership positions need both assistance in their functional jobs and need authentic callouts.

I want to conclude with two thoughts. One with a reference to an article that I published on authentic interactions and another with a question for you to ponder and respond to look back or look forward to one thing. Are there people in your space that need help? Have you helped to raise people’s averages or do you introspect right now to see how others around you may need help.

https://www.reallyniceguy.net/post/grow-your-blog-community

title: Inside the World Economic Forum
content:
organization based in Switzerland. The WEF is best known for its annual meeting at the end of January in Davos, a resort in the Alps region of Switzerland. The meeting is aimed to discuss global economy, health and the environment related issues.

Lobbying at the WEF provides opportunities for business leaders to sell their products and services to international political leaders.

Most recently, Javier Milei, Argentinian president gave a very impactful speech covering challenges from socialism and collectivism along with the fundamental strengths witnessed from capitalism. Let’s look at some definitions as I give a summary of his keynote. Socialism and Collectivism: Fundamental belief of socialism is around production and distribution of resources through centralized economic planning. Focus is on equitable distribution through taxes, welfare and public services.

Capitalism: In a capitalistic society individuals and businesses have the ability to control properties, drive competition, generate profits and create free markets with minimum governmental intervention. This leads to wealth creation and innovation, alongside challenges arising from disparities.

Gross Domestic Product: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is the total monetary value of all finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period, typically annually. It is a broad measure of a nation's overall economic activity and health. World’s GDP is the sum total of the GDP of individual nations.

Quantitative Easing: Quantitative Easing implies when a central bank of a country buys securities from the market to increase the money supply to boost the economy. Firstly, Milei alluded to the fact that the values adopted by the leaders of the western world are leading us to a socialist state eventually making us poorer. He emphasizes capitalism as the only possible way to end poverty. Here are some of the statistical inferences from the speech

- In the first 1800 years A.D., the world’s GDP remained flat
- Starting 19th century, 1801 onwards, world’s GDP experienced a hockey stick growth (exponential)
- World GDP’s annual growth was 0.66% at the start of 19th century, it grew at 1.66% annually for 50 years, at 2.1% in 1950s, to where it is at a 3% growth in today’s terms
- This would mean that at the current rate the GDP will double every 23 years or so
- In 1800s 95% of the world’s population was poor which is now diagonally opposite to state that less than 5% of world’s population is poor today A Socialist state will lead to coercive (forceful) approach in taxation and slow the progress down, which is only possible in the capitalist state. Good quality products and services will ensure continuous demand and innovation cycles.

He quoted, ‘Thanks to free trade capitalism, the world is now living its best moment.’ Citizens of free countries live longer and far from poverty. He also mentions Neoclassical economic theory, which unwillingly ends up with intervention by the state, socialism and social degradation. States don’t need this control. You can use quantitative easing, debts, subsidies, interest rate controls and regulations to manage monopolies/oligopolies that I had mentioned about in my earlier thoughts. They should not control the lives and fates of individuals. Do you opine differently? As always, looking for your thoughts.

Here is the link to the full transcript, https://www.eurasiareview.com/18012024-speech-of-argentinas-president-javier-milei-at-davos-t ranscript/ How to use ChatGPT?

In school, we learnt there are no bad questions. With ChatGPT, there are good questions and bad questions. Bad questions will give poor quality answers.

Provide Custom Instructions by clicking on your profile icon. Explain who you are, what you want and how you want ChatGPT to respond. You can reuse it all the time. For example - I am a python programmer based in India. I would like you to respond with a humorous response and provide your opinions, no more than 100 words for each response without detailed explanations. You can ask ChatGPT to provide confidence of the response and when any relevant facts are used you would like to know the reference URLs.

You can also teach ChatGPT to write like you would like by providing reference examples. You can ask ChatGPT to store the writing style as MY_STYLE and ask it to write in future using MY_STYLE. ChatGPT will acknowledge its understanding of your writing styles. You can ask ChatGPT to review its writing and be self critical. You can ask it to be analytical and ruthless for its writing, unlike what humans are not easily able to do.

You can ask ChatGPT to self-prompt to optimize its responses. You can ask for 4 or 5 prompts that it recommends, use one of the recommendation or improvised version of that prompt when actually prompting. ChatGPT may ask you questions about more specifics of your task for it to prompt effectively.

You can ask for responses to be textual, tabular, html, python code or image (DALL.E) formats.

title: Ideas and Startups
content:
Do you ideate during your shower time, bedtime or drive time? We all do. Some of the ideas materialize into a product or service to start your own company. Many ideas fizzle out of our thoughts and then there is a day when you see a company with the same product or a service. Perhaps a moment goes and you say, ‘hey, this was my idea’. Ha… ha… ha….

Most of us are aware of the ‘Shark Tank’ television series. It first started in the United States in 2009. Since then it has seen replication in the UK, Australia, Mexico, Japan, India, Brazil and perhaps a few more. Founders demonstrate both the idea as a concept/product and the financials around the being of the startup. Sharks then reject or invest into their ideas, either individually or as a team. Sharks exist in the offline format as well as angel investors or venture capitalists. Sometimes they are in angelic form as friends and family.

Equity Instruments: Shares, Options, and Warrants We all know of shares either through a publicly traded format or shares of a private company. Equity instruments are also qualified options and warrants in addition. Options are typically granted to employees as a part of compensation package to incentivize long-term commitments. Warrants are financial derivatives that give the holder the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell a company’s stock at a specified price before the warrants expire. They are similar to options but are often issued directly by the company. There is also investment through convertible notes and SAFE (Simple Agreement for Future Equity). Convertible notes are debt instruments that convert into equity under certain conditions.

The “Cap” Table or The Capitalization Table Now the fun part. With all the investments in the startup, at any given point in time, there has to be a clear representation of who owns what. Cap Table is a spreadsheet or database that shows the equity ownership of all the above equity instruments across a company’s shareholders. Complexity of these databases (Cap Tables) is efficiently maintained through specialized software platforms like Carta, Capshare and Pulley. They offer tools for equity management, scenario modeling and compliance services.

Managing Equities of a Public Company Public companies have stringent regulatory requirements which you may experience as part of your jobs in many ways. Agencies like DTCC (Depository Trust and Clearing Corporation) in the US, CREST in the UK, NSDL/CDSL (National Securities Depository Limited/ Central Depository Services (India) Limited) in India enable transparent, safe and efficient tracking of equities in digital format.

Between the Cap Table managed by software for private companies' shareholding and above mentioned (and more) agencies for tracking of public companies' shareholding, finance and technologies have intersected to revolutionize how companies manage their equity. Before these software based solutions were developed, imagine that there were a series of management challenges in managing physical share certificates; manual record keeping; paper based transfer of physical shares; risk of fraud and loss through loss, theft, forgery and damage; settlement delays and communication ineffectiveness.

Where else have you seen these digital waves riding massive efficiency and improvements? What else can we deep dive into? As always, share your thoughts.

title: High-Performing and Trustworthy Culture
content:
This article is in response to a request to share how leaders enthuse trust while building high performing teams. Think of two simple dimensions: Trust and Performance. A rockstar employee demonstrates high trust and performs superiorly. If there is a non-trustworthy employee who is not performing then he or she will find it hard to survive over a period of time. Non-trustworthy high performing individuals can be equally dangerous, or more so, as they will build a non-coherent workplace for others around them.

It is easy to understand these pockets at a high level. However to establish a culture that builds consistently high performing organizations requires the whole village to play the orchestra. Factors that Build Trust Here are a few things that come to my mind:

1. Communication: Successful organizations thrive on real-time and face-to-face (in person or virtual) conversations. This is a dialog and not a monologue from a manager to an employee. It needs to be engaging and must happen as close as possible around the event. This necessitates privacy, sensitivity and mutual respect in both directions. It is everyone’s responsibility to initiate these dialogs whether you are an employee or a manager.

2. Respect: Building a culture of objective and honest dialogs is the next level. Always, keep the issues in perspective and not the individuals. Most of us go through an intense recruitment cycle being an employee or if you apply the same mindset to your personal relationships outside your family then they also go through intensive evaluations over a period of time to be that special someone. You are where/who you are for a reason. 3. Integrity: Confidentiality of conversations is extremely important. Coming back to keeping the issue and person separated, sometimes it necessitates leaders to share the origin, outcomes and fix of issues with the larger organization without naming/shaming any individuals. Leaders need to be effective listeners in these situations or otherwise, as listening is a much harder skill to develop for most humans.

4. Support: Providing guidance, tools, navigation path and adequate time for people to be successful is essential. The world around us changes constantly and the methods/tools of yesterday become irrelevant over time. As a result, for a leader, it is extremely important to be cognizant that without an adequate development plan neither the organization nor she can be successful.

5. Feedback: It is important to let individuals know where they stand and what improvements are required. Providing and accepting critical feedback is important to all of us as an individual or a leader. Ensure that this dialog happens in a very comfortable environment, as often as required, non-toxically and in both directions.

High Performing Organizations Assuming that we all understand our role in building our trust with others and the larger organization, here are some key questions we must look at to build a high performing organization 1. Initiative: Are you thinking outside the box? Do you understand the real impacts of your goals? Are you constantly challenging yourself to drive value from your outcomes? Do you understand if your goals connect to the larger goals of the organization? 2. Adaptability: What are your stimuli like to the changing narrative of the world around you? Our life is constantly changing, especially within the organizational context of how many exponential changes happen in this world.

3. Reliability: How do I demonstrate high quality, super-responsive and dependability traits?

4. Collaboration: Most individuals, owing to an implicit competitive mindset, find it difficult to work with peers. High performing organizations thrive to score more than 2 from adding 1 to a 1. 10x thinking can happen in your dreambox however 10x outcomes do not come from individuals. Do you collaborate with others?

5. Initiative: This is my favorite point of view. How do I find new ways for getting things done? Do I play the 10x productivity game? Do I find ways to optimize productivity? Do I share my best practices with others?

In the performance part I ask many questions for personal introspection.

A few techniques for continuous engagement and learning 1. Connecting with Leaders: Powerful and influential people don’t have time. If you get an hour with them then make the best use of every minute. If your conversation with this person becomes boring to her then you have lost their attention. Being a powerful leader, he/she should also build tolerance to enjoy the boring conversations. 2. Social Skills: A friend on social/professional media can be unfriended. You can’t unfriend a noisy neighbor or even a manager/colleague for that matter. If there is a noisy neighbor (or even a manager ) you will need skills to walk into your neighbor’s house with a cake to build a relationship and then address the noise issue. We have to constantly uplevel/upgrade our human skills, just like your smartphone or the apps on the device.

3. Dealing with Unlike mindedness: Social media’s AI engine and watercooler at the office will build natural affinity to your liking for the content or the people you interact with. Real workplaces are not designed like social media to put you in a loop of likings so you will need an open mind to build an open door culture.

4. Humility: Humility is an enduring quality required especially for the accomplished bunch. You need to stop being the center of the world and think about the collective humility of your ecosystem.

5. Self-Development: In the decade prior to right now, you could always predict what the world of employment (or livelihood) looked like for the upcoming 20-30 years with learning and academia in perspective. This is not true today and it necessitates learning and retraining all the time.

Few decades back, imagine if a tractor replaced you on your farm, you could figure out what it would be like to be employed in a tractor manufacturing facility. This was a relatively low skill transition. This is not the case today. New jobs in arts, in creative fields and science streams require superior skills.

If you look back in time, global inequalities have significantly reduced in the last few decades. With the rapid changes in the skills and technology landscape, there will be a burst of changes that will increase these inequalities. Opportunities will have significant gaps to address based on an individual’s phase (middle-age vs. early in career talent), geography (knowledge/opportunities availability) and skill transition differentials (factory worker vs. knowledge worker) Take some time out to ask yourself the questions for a high performing organization and introspect. Do you have other factors that broaden these thoughts? Do you have best practices/examples to share? Do you see (y)our organization and think differently? Looking forward to hearing your thoughts, as always.

Few of the thoughts came from this dialog with Yuval Noah Harari (Author of the Sapiens, Homo Deus, 21 Lessons for the 21st Century) https://youtu.be/9qLHEaZNzbM?si=TsMU2sa8qzJUZ_eQ

title: A Tale of Two Social Media (Meta and Snap)
content:
Meta (known as Facebook then) and Snap both outperformed as a social media enterprise in the Covid and ZIRP (Zero Interest Rate Policy) times.

The Problem Meta increased its R&D focus to invest in headsets and Metaverse over the last few years. Meta realized the untimeliness and/or the relevance of their innovation strategy and made some changes. Let’s look at what transpired.

The Response

- Meta’s superlative R&D spends were not making sense
- Meta reduced its headcount by 22% from 82,000+ to ~67,000 from 2022 to 2023
- Its quarterly profits increased to $14B for Q4 ‘23, valuation reached over $1.2T and the stock price soared to $470 from where it was trading in te $90s range
- Meta caters to over 3.2B daily users in its platform The Rationale
- Shareholders have a say and must be heard. Mark Zuckerberg and team responded to points of view that came from outside of the company from its investors and advisors The Outcomes
- Market capitalization value of a company is represented as an amount equivalent to market share price multiplied the number of outstanding shares
- Each vesting activity of employee shares creates an expense item and it directly negatively impacts against the cash flow generated
- In this instance, for the year 2023, Meta spent $14B in stock based compensation against $71B of generated cash flow. Stock compensation represents 20% of free cash flow
- Meta also bought back $20B worth of stocks through their buyback strategy
- An important measure to track here is that Meta is trades at a value of 25 times free cash flow
- High proportionality of value to free cash flow also represents trust in your brand, which is going to be high with
- Meta’s strategy to manage headcount,
- Responsive reduction in Metaverse investments,
- Meta is the next best advertising platform after Google,
- Very high number users accessing the platform and
- Superlative AI narratives with all that you have heard about Llama models A Comparison Reference point in Snap Many of you have, if not used, heard of Snap and Snapchat.

Contrary to what Meta experienced, appreciated and responded to build a superlative growth narrative, Snap did everything wrong.

- Snap shares are down by 60% from $27 at IPO to $11 right now
- Snap as a company has infinite to zero voting power over common shareholders, who do not have a say in the company
- This creates a zero feedback loop to induce accountability. They have no obligations to receive inputs
- Snap’s revenue increased from $4.1B to $4.5B between 2021 and 2023. At the same time the expenses went up from $3B to $4B approximately. Net losses increased from $0.5B to $1.35B
- Compared to $35M of cash flow generated, it rewarded employees with $1.3B of stock based compensation, which is 40 times the generated cash flow. Remember, what it was for Meta at 0.2 times the cash flow
- Snap serves about 400M customers, just announced 10% headcount reduction (a bit too late and a lot lesser) and does not have an extraordinary growth narrative like Meta Here are a few questions to put in perspective as a result:
- Do you work for an employer that has a growth narrative with product, customers and revenues in sight?
- Do you have a sense of headcount growth or reduction in your organization?
- Do you realize what it means to receive stock based compensation? How does it impact the cash flow and profitability statements?
- Are you as an individual, executive or a leader pulling enough weight to justify your cash and equity based compensation?
- Do you understand your (in)direct contribution in the organization’s growth story? I have done a bit more research in the AI based compute, New LLM modalities and progress in the AI space. If I can I will provide another write between now and the next set of thoughts. As always, I will look for your thoughts and responses.

title: Catching Up on AI (Part I)
content:
I decided to catch up on my AI notes this week so this one will be slightly geeky and macroeconomy based write-up. A lot of details so ensure you are dedicating some time right now to be able to absorb what is going on.

One thing to understand as a keynote of this message is that all LLM models and many of the AI models are represented in the form of a matrix. Capabilities of AI infrastructure are a measure of speed and size of the matrices that are multiplied. This understanding will help you appreciate the LLMs and AI Infrastructure for a very long time to come.

NVIDIA and the Soar of Sora With the skyrocketed revenues and growth, NVIDIA is now the 4th largest in value behind Microsoft, Apple and Saudi Aramco. They reported outstanding revenue growth demonstrating the high potential of the AI world.

OpenAI announced the release of Sora, a prompt based video generation capability. OpenAI’s Platform now has the ability to generate up to 60 seconds long videos based on your imaginative narration.

Unreal vs. Artificial ‘Unreal Engine 5’, a product of Epic Games, which has existed much longer than the realm of LLMs, creates real-time 3D photoreal visuals and immersive experiences. Unreal Engine employs photogrammetry, capturing multiple photographs of a real-world object or scene and creates 3d models, capturing with it textures, colors, lights and other attributes of the physical world.

Sora, as a natural extension of the Large Language model, creates videos from prompts through training the model with a lot of real world examples. It will be interesting to find out how the Sora models were trained. They possibly were built from tagging and labeling the outputs generated from Unreal Engine 5 mentioned above, and perhaps a few more products similar to Unreal Engine 5.

Have you tried using ‘Dall.E’ or Midjourney to create images? Assuming that the experience of using Sora will be very similar, each time you ask to improvise an image or a video, the generated results through improvisation are significantly different. For example if you ask Dall.E or Midjourney to create an image and if you provide a prompt to improvise the image for image or the text change in the image, the entire image gets recreated in many ways. Each instance creates a significantly different representation for improvisation. This is because the rendering in AI tools unlike in photoshop, Unreal Engine or Autocad, is based on Matrices. Traditional CAD and Image Management tools are based on layers which are more controllable in nature. Eventually, this will necessitate marriage of compute/layer trained models like in Photoshop, Unreal Engine or Autocad with AI trained models like Sora and Gemini. The objects rendered by AI/LLM models can potentially become input to the traditional tools so that the black boxes from LLMs can get a more flavors. Other way around is also a possibility.

We have come so far so nothing looks impossible. With that, as promised for a bit longer write-up this time, let’s shift focus on the AI compute narrative.

Future of AI compute Let’s go back to basics as we build this up.

CPUs and GPUs: CPUs (Central Processing Units) are used for general purpose sequential computing. GPUs (Graphics Processing Units) specialize in parallel processing with thousands of cores that help with graphics rendering and machine learning. An example of NVIDIA GPU is H100 which is designed for accelerating AI, High Performance Computing (HPC) and Graphics. TPUs (Tensor Processing Unit): TPU is a specialized hardware accelerator designed by Google for deep learning tasks and is optimized for speed and scale, outperforming CPUs and some GPUs in terms of throughput. TPUs were designed in 2016 to accelerate speech recognition and search algorithms. An important data point to introduce here is 100 ms of improvement in response performance, improves traffic by 8% of a given interaction medium. Introducing Groq and the LPU (Language Processing Unit) Jonathan Ross, who was one of the founding members of the TensorFlow and the Tensor Processing Unit (TPUs) at Google is the founder of Groq.

What are the unique characteristics of Groq LPU (Language Processing Unit)?

- Groq is built on the FPGA (Field Programmable Gate Array) concept used in the build out of TPUs. As implied in the name, Gates (engineers - remember the days of circuit design and gates - this is not a reference to Bill) are built to be Programmable dynamically after manufacturing of the silicon.
- For most of the AI and for all LLMs, matrix multiplication is the biggest problem for improving the speed of execution
- LPUs are built to perform inference at a very high speed
- Understand that the models are already built, both in the open and closed source format and the quality of performance is already addressed. What remains is the speed of execution in using these models
- In the definition of CPUs and GPUs, we discussed parallel processing. However for Generative AI speed requirements far exceed the parallelization expectations. This is
- 
- 
- 
- because to predict 100th token of the response, it can only be done after the 99th token is predicted Each token prediction in Generative AI usually will have the requirement to pick one from 32,000 available tokens. Imagine response speed required to pick one token after the other from an array of 32000 distinct tokens for each token pick GPUs use external memory and LPUs use internal memories so they are actually very fast andare speed intensive, especially to make them helpful for Generative AI Modern GPUs are built at 3 to 4 Nanometer scale and are incredibly expensive, both to build and to buy. Groq LPU is built at about 14 Nanometer size. Understand that smaller the size greater the complexity GPUs generate about 120 tokens every second. LPUs generate about 240 tokens a second and the test version already is generating 330 tokens a second. This number will only improvise What happens next?

Given this transformation, the LPU platform will possibly be acquired or built by the mainstream chip companies. They may be able to compete independently as well.

NVIDIA probably has little threat from the mainstream competitive chip manufacturers however these alternative technology platforms will pose a threat for sure.

I have more thoughts to actually create a part II of this write up for next week. So, what do you think will happen next? Let me know if this narrative made sense after a long break that I had on my AI/LLM updates and if you are excited to hear part II. I am super excited to write my next set of observations and some predictions.

title: AI and Platform Challenges (Part II)
content:
Here is part II of the AI/LLM write-up with addition of a few more surprises and updates from this week.

AI is unfolding competition and platform challenges similar to what had transpired in the search and the browser era. However, the impact on our lives is much greater from these changes at least from what I infer.

Google Gemini: ‘Role Model’ Challenges in the Model Behaviors Google rechristened it’s AI platform from Bard to Duet AI to Gemini now. Earlier last week, Google suspended its image generation capability after it generated embarrassing and offensive results. They had to publicly apologize for missing the mark as it tried to overcompensate for diversity in the responses generated by the models, with the underlying role model behaviors. When we search for something with traditional search modalities, we are offered an array of options usually, including some of the promotional references. AI, on the contrary, has to give only one answer. Given that, Gemini has come on the defensive mode and is asking users to use Google search when responding to sensitive (even the ones perceived to be sensitive) questions.

This might necessitate the reinforcement in the input interactions with the models by providing a lot more contextual prompting, context setting and reflection based response improvement. Models unlike humans will not have delete capabilities, so you keep reiterating by asking the model to improve its responses from reflections. Usually, 3 reflections lead to improvements of a model’s generation (example ChatGPT 3.5 to ChatGPT 4.0) however this also introduces a delay. In this instance, you are using the reinforcement through an individual’s feedback as compared to general improvements that the LLMs make through Reinforced Learning from Human Feedback, more as a collective representation from many humans.

In the email to his employees that publicly appeared on quite a few social media, Sundar Pichai has vowed to make structural changes in the company to address these challenges. This is an early phase of the LLM life so certainly we will see a lot of learning and corrections in the process.

On a separate note, Elon Musk is suing OpenAI and Sam Altman for violating the principles and premise for its not for profit foundation.

Data: Growth, Relevance and Cost of Data Acquisition It is predicted that the data grows at 2.5 Quintillion bytes on a daily basis. 1,024 Terabytes make a Petabyte. 1,024 Petabytes make an Exabyte, also referred to as a Quintillion byte. About 10% of this can be estimated to be available on the internet, which itself will be a large set to absorb and process for learning. Also, more than half the data generated is not of much relevance to the greater world. A lot of the data also sits behind platforms or media. Google (Gemini) has contracts with Reddit ($60M a year) and Stack Overflow to consume data for its learning and use. OpenAI, post the New York Times lawsuit controversy, is similarly making an agreement with dozens of publishers to license content for training. Many questions will come over time to know how these purchases will lead to monopolies like Google search or not? Will investments in humans get restructured to invest in contents? If the models are not deterministic by definition how can one determine the value of the content purchased through the LLM’s Matrixed Black Box? Can/Will the content access be explicit to some providers?

Fullstacks from Silicon Manufacturers vs. Silicon Manufacturing by Hyperscalers AMD GPUs offer competitive pricing and excellent performance for gaming and general tasks. NVIDIA, however, leads in ray tracing technology and AI-driven applications. The best choice to make will vary based on price, performance needs, and software ecosystem preferences, making the comparison not straightforward but rather contextual. NVIDIA has nailed the game for the immediate future for sure as it offers a holistic full stack platform that is beyond the chipsets for excelling in the LLM space.

At the same time, following the lead of TPU/LPU conversation from last week, imagine that AWS, Google, Meta and many more are aiming to build and release their own inference silicon. They already have the power of their full stack platforms. Of course, building a chipset is a very big undertaking.

There is also a risk with NVIDIA GPU demand that if you used a platform from another supplier your GPU orders may get deprioritized.

My Take on the Future: It is only bright As we play at the intersection of Data, AI and Applications, I can only say that the future is bright. This necessitates all of us to embrace and know AI else you will be threatened from not being in the game.

- NVIDIA sold $22B worth of hardware, largely the GPUs in Q4. $13B in profits. Now this is huge, just in a quarter
- This game is expanding. Even with other players/manufacturers of LPUs or otherwise the game is getting bigger in terms of investments in infrastructure
- To implement and make the best use of $22B worth of hardware will need another 3x investment in resources. We are looking at $88B to $100B of project based investments to deploy this hardware, just from a quarter
- This spend cannot be like just the toys for the boys. This will generate at least another few Trillion dollars for economic growth on an annualized basis. Just look back at what Microsoft, Cisco and Oracle based infrastructure provided as propellers to tech based economic growth in the past
- This will also require platform based companies where you have one stop shop that intersects Data, AI and Applications. You cannot be mistaken as a platform by having 50 a-la-carte options. You cannot expect your customers to interconnect your disparate capabilities from inorganic/acquisitive growth I want to end this note here with two key takeaways: Stay Invested and Interested in the Modern AI/Data Stack and Platforms. You will be the creators of the future.

As always, thank you for reading all the way through and will look forward to your responses. Perhaps time to shift gears with an outlook on economies/economics next week. Have a great week.

title: A Tribute to Frank Slootman's 'Amp'
content:
This write-up is a tribute to the ‘Amp’ that Frank (Slootman) initiated personally for me and for many individuals around the world.

In the month of February, 2010, I joined MSC Software to build its ‘India Entity’ similar to starting Snowflake 10 years later. My first day was at our office in Southern California and it coincided with Bob Harder’s 40th work anniversary in the company. Yes, you read it right - 40th. Median age of this reader group is far lower than 40. Bob worked for MSC software for all his professional life and continued until 2 weeks before passing away in his 80s. Similarly, I know of another colleague who worked at IBM for 25+ years, in this case working for the same reporting manager for 25 years. Let’s look at different points of view for what your professional life should drive for and how/when a career shift is initiated.

### Amped Up Life

First, I want to acknowledge and thank Frank for his leadership and for having ‘Amped Us Up’. A lot of us were shocked and thought, ‘Frank, a few more years. Please stay back.’ Very rarely in your lifespan you will come across someone who is so accomplished and inspirational and there are many takeaways. Here are some jewels from his second book, ‘Amp it up’: I had an amazing opportunity to go back to my notes

### For Me

- Raise your standards
- Focus: Mile wide and inch deep does not work. Work on a few Prioritized Goals
- Type Faster: Sense of Urgency, Compress Cycles, Apply Pressure, & be Impatient
- Get Uncomfortable as our roles will not remain the same
- Avoid Mediocrity and avoid Attaching to the Past
- Grow Fast or Die Slowly
- You are a Product: Be Purposeful - Meaningful Experience matters more than an MBA
- Avoid having Short Tenured Jobs. Stick around long enough to be Somebody
- Don’t spend more time on Solutions. Diagnose Problems

### As a Leader

- Communicate: As leader, Over-communicate and be the Chief Reminding Officer
- Ownership: Your team has to feel like owners
- Declare a war against competition and incrementalism:
- Hospitality Companies did not become AirBnB
- Taxi Companies did not become Uber
- Automobile Companies did not become Tesla
- Think about where you want to be in the future and work backwards
- Intellectual Honesty: Recognize Dangers from Groupthink and Confirmation Bias
- Consultants: They will borrow your watch, tell you the time & then keep the watch
- Hire Drivers not Passengers. Get Passengers off the Bus
- Deep Reluctance to Fire People is very common
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- Find the Right Talent and Do not rush to Hire Maintain an active Recruiting Posture: Hire before the Needs (Critical Positions) People Learn from Consequences not Posters Appreciate the Variance between the Stated/Desired Culture and the Reality Go Direct and Build Mutual Trust. Don’t Play Defense Reminder of 5 Dysfunctions of a Team (Patrick Leoncini):
- Inattention to Results
- Avoidance of Accountability
- Lack of Commitment
- Fear of Conflict
- Absence of Trust
- Build Right Products and Repeatable Sales Processes
- Don’t Outsource Recruitment When you look at the leader list above, review with a lens for the leader you will become, the leader you are and the help that you can provide to your leader with these backdrops. (Un)Attaching to People/Work Frank’s departure fits somewhere in Expecting the Unexpected Outcomes in my book of experiences. There is so much that our new leader Sridhar brings in the world of AI/LLM so more about that at a later point.

### What is a good time to Move on

Life Events: Most always, unless there is a compelling life event, you need not look outside for a new opportunity. If you work for a great company and a great culture then you will find something to do even when there are life events unless you are going back to school.
