- There is no substitute for continuous learning. Formal academia though helps give both structure and attention, is not the only source of learning. Remember, meaningful experiences are far more powerful than an MBA degree
- With life events, good companies enable change in job profile, geography or also create part-time opportunities. You need to be a role model employee as well for this to happen
- Your career rotation opportunities will be best presented within an organization, most always When do we actually do it and Why?

Most always, ‘actual’ decisions are not ‘factual’, but rather emotional. We tend to find an escape

### route with discomfort arising from

- I cannot work for this person. We have heard many times that people don’t leave jobs, they leave a manager. While this might be possible, you have a role to play here to address challenges arising from such situations
- A past colleague or an ex-manager is calling you
- Better pay, different job, better work environment and promotions, which may have their own perceptions My point is to not discourage anyone to look for career growth; however they cannot be an outcome of having a poor self-assessment or detaching from your responsibilities to yourself and the greater organization.

What should we actually do?

- Firstly, being honest to self and being self-critical is where everyone must start
- Engage with your manager(s)/management and have candid conversations
- Be strongly aware of your own gaps with skills and expectations
- Ask for help and support
- Aim for superlative performance and a very strong personal brand narrative
- Understand the point of view of what your career will transform into in 3 years, not a raise or promotion that you expect today
- Eliminate distractions about monetary expectations. Remember, these are byproducts of what you do and not preconceived expectations of your self-assessed value
- Build a powerful network of people that you can lean on to all the time. Professional media will not respond to you in your distressed situation
- Build a 90 second elevator pitch of your brand to express how you make a dent
- Most importantly, focus on mental and physical well being, improvise intellect (self-assessed) and look forward to life A big thank you to Prasad Pagar for asking me to write about this.

Did I miss anything? Do you have a personal favorite POV? A story to share? Looking forward to it.

title: Rewards, Results and Returns
content:
Rewards, Results and Returns Remember, when you were a kid there was a task and a reward. You clean your room and get screen time. Schools similarly worked on the premise of studying well and getting good results. Fixed Deposits work similarly and where you get your returns on your investment on a predetermined basis.

Although nothing will happen without perseverance, professional journeys will never be as linearly deterministic. We often fall in the trap of linear expectations of compensation and promotions, many times leading to disappointment. As an employee a lot of factors, controllable

### and uncontrollable, play a big role

- First and foremost, since companies are designed with growth, performance and profit perspectives, your growth will always be in reflection of the need. Need for a position at the next level will determine a significant career change. Your potential to perform at the next level and your exemplary performance in the current position are expected irrespectively
- Sometimes there is a change in leadership and organization structure that can also reset both the understanding of your personal narrative and of the leader’s expectation
- Unlike a classroom where your study groups are filled with comparable individuals, organization is a very comprehensive mix of experience levels and the roles that people perform. Job expectations and criticality of many positions may not be obvious to us, leading to preconceived biases Growth Orientation If you work for an organization that is built on strong values and beliefs then expect that the right results will yield, sometimes needing patience. This necessitates
- Seeking big challenges, asking for autonomy to perform tasks and focusing on results
- Focus on a strong connection of your objectives and outcomes directly relating to transformation, innovation, and growth (product, customers and revenue) else I learnt in Jansen Huang’s keynote with Stanford students.

Accomplished students have high expectations as they have already worked hard in academia. High expectations make you less resilient so it is best to then have super realistic and low expectations, which will make you resilient. Resilience gives you power to quickly stand back on your feet each time you fall down

- Seeking feedback and leveraging on your support network is also critically important
- Grit necessitates working relentlessly on tasks, knowing the meaning and purpose of work at hand and staying strongly connected with it. The day you feel that you are in a dreadful situation, magically a ray of success will appear
- Something Imagine that you are running your own business for a second. Entrepreneurship needs a lot more patience and relentlessness - guarantees are even further reduced due to factors totally out of your control.

In a nutshell continuous learning, long term career transforming goals slated for next 3-5 years, having a low expectation and highly resilient mindset will take you a long way.

title: Passion Chooses You: Lessons From Bezos
content:
I got to see Jeff Bezos’ leadership video again this weekend. It reminded me of the best practices then and I looked at these best practices to introspect and reflect on some of them.

### Passion Chooses You

Jeff reminds us that we don’t choose our passion and our passion chooses us. Some of us are talented with a skill that comes to us in the form of a gift. For example, some of us are naturally gifted with skills to understand numbers or that we may have great grasping and/or analytical abilities to decipher a complex situation.

Talent not Practiced is only good on piece of a paper The idea is to not just be proud of these gifts but to know how we have used these gifts or skills. Did you practice them for good usage and when you look back at life do you feel proud of efforts that led to greatly utilizing these skills? Do you measure tangible returns from your effort investments?

Flexible Mindset Also he mentions that good leaders are ‘right a lot’. What does this even mean? Well, essentially good leaders listen, they change their mind a lot and disconfirm their most profound convictions. It is very easy to reconfirm our beliefs with situations and it is really hard to disconfirm a certain preconceived notion.

When you have gathered more data or with experiences, you can change your mind. Politicians largely are not allowed to change their mind as it can greatly impact their posture in the world of politics. Corporates need to work without political manifestations and if we do not build the ability to change our minds then we are dramatically underestimating the complexity of our worlds. For example, we may have an opinion of an individual based on a notion and as you experience events with this individual, you will learn certain aspects of her skills or intent and really what you preconceived needs to change. I have seen that many times in my personal assessment of people.

Vision and Execution Have a clear vision that is largely fixated however when you execute you will need flexibility with details. Be relentless on the vision and when you execute to try and implement the vision, you will make wrong decisions and you will need to course correct.

Failure and Innovation are inseparable twins. You cannot invent without failures and we need to grow the size of failures as we grow the organizations. As a child, we were always taught of trial and error mode, the grown up world does not need to behave differently.

Two Pizza Teams Any team that needs more than two pizzas for lunch on an assignment are too big and will complicate matters. Ideally, keeping in mind the sizes of pizzas in the US, two pizzas feed 10-12 people, so you get the point.

Memos vs. Slides This is slightly contentious in a way. Amazon practices 6 page written memos in preparation for meetings. Everyone takes the time before the meeting or at the start of the meeting to go through the memos. No power points here. Memos have verbs, grammar and sentences.

### Some Questions here to Introspect

I have personally preferred slides as a good slide/picture can demonstrate 1000 words and we all know that. Also, should I not feed the memos to an LLM to make succinct highlights out of it as opposed to reading the memo or both, read and identify LLM based insights? Have you as an individual or a leader acknowledged a time when you changed your mind or your opinion about something/someone?

Have you encountered failures during your invention cycles and learnt from them? Have you given opportunities for your people to fail or for yourself to fail?

As part of executing your strategies, have you encountered chaos from teething issues?

title: The Growth of Nations and People
content:
India is predicted to be the 3rd largest nation in the race of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth and its GDP is currently projected to surpass $4T for the year 2024. The United States, China, Germany and Japan are ahead of India in this measure.

There are other measures in addition to the GDP of ‘GDP per capita’ (GDP divided by the population), GVA (Gross Value Adds) and PPP (Purchasing Price Parity). While the GDP per capita may be a lower number based on the number of citizens in a country, there is also a factor of Purchasing Price Parity which you can imagine from the ability to determine what a rupee can purchase for you in India compared to a dollar in the United States. A simple example can be in the form of haircut cost in India which can range from 100 rupees to north of 500+ rupees at a fancy salon. Haircut costs in the United States can vary from $10 (830 rupees) to $40 (4100+ rupees), or even more.

How is the GDP calculated?

As calculated with an expenditure based approach GDP of a country is calculated as GDP = C + I + G + (X - M) where

- 
- 
- 
- 
- C I G X M => Consumption by all the households => Investments made within the country => Government Spending => Exports (Like Agricultural products or Technology Services ) => Imports (Like Oil or Defense Spend) While the numbers and measures will always have subjectivity, how much better you are today compared to your yesterday matters the most, whether you are an individual, a company or a country. Let’s use this analogy keeping people or let’s say specifically each of us in mind. Network Growth: An Individual’s View I got a call from a past colleague as she was seeking some advice for a situation. She expressed her inability to deal with a work situation and expressed her vulnerability. We spent quite some time discussing the matter and I provided some guidance to navigate certain situations. She was thankful for me spending precious time out of my busy schedule. So, what? Well, as a starter, this person does not work in my current organization, reached out to me to say hello and to discuss her opportunity areas along with some vulnerabilities. Essentially, she networked with me. Keep this interaction in mind as we build a logic around Personal Network Growth as a formula.

Networking to me is ‘Net’ of your ‘Work’ with a strong focus on human-to-human orchestration. Let’s define a measure as ‘Network Development Plan (NDP)’ with a myopic focus on people and relationships.

NDP = C + R + I + (X - A) where

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- NDP C R I X A => Network Development Plan => Content => Relationships => Investments => X Factor => Abscondment C (Content) refers to your core competence, the work done, and the sole purpose that you are attached to both your position and your organization. You spend years in a job and how you do your job will grow your personal brand. In the last few years, I have hired more than 15% of my team from past references and work relationships. I would not have hired these people and many more that I can still hire unless they built a very strong brand from competence, high degree of commitment and associated accomplishments.

R (Relationships) refers to people we have met in the past associations, people in the current organization and the people that you meet outside of your work. When you see my reference to people here, it does not necessarily only mean people that can influence your career but also includes the ones who you can help and support. You never know who can be of assistance when a need arises. When you initiate a new professional relationship always have a context to build engagement, a well defined broad purpose and and not just an intent that leads to immediate gains.

I (Investments): I get many invites on my professional media accounts and sometimes after you accept the invite within an hour you get a request for a job application or evaluation of a product that they want to sell. Sure, I was sitting here with all the free time, waiting for someone to call me, write to me and for me to hire them or to evaluate their product. You get the point. You don’t build relationships without investments. Now go back to the colleague who reached out for a discussion on a topic related to vulnerability that she wished to discuss. Seeking help is the best way to connect with people and as much as you think that the other person will be busy, it most always is your imagination. You will miss every shot that you don’t take.

X Factor: I asked Abhishek Mishra, our data leader, to come up with a plan that encompasses a framework for both our Snow@Snow initiatives and our go-to-market framework. Details of this point of view demonstrates his X-Factor and the X-Factor that you can create. This is what the

### framework comprises of

- A section that tracks our customer use-cases demonstrating best in class Data Cloud practices along with usage of the New Product Introductions (NPIs) and features
- A section that lists all of the specific customer engagements to showcase these use cases and capabilities to our global customers as a reference library
- List of all the recently completed and planned webinars, whitepapers and blogs
- Our involvement in engaging with how we can support the Snowflake’s Academia Program in the region. There are many who are contributing to this already as their own X-Factor
- On a side note, we also have our ‘AI for Everyone’ initiative that Rahul Jain, another data leader, is running for our teams. This is Rahul and a few others who are contributing effectively to this as their X-Factor The beauty and the beast-y sense of the X-Factors is, most always, you work on them without expecting an immediate tangible return or a reward. You will need to look around within your organization and you will find many opportunities that eventually will drive your NDP (Network Development Plan) score, although this is not going to be a direct financial measure. A (Abscondment) can also be treated as avoidance. This can be a function of not having prioritized this investment, taking the time out of your personal space for personal development, discomfort in reaching out, and sometimes lack in sight of opportunities that need driving the extra-mile. “Opportunity is missed by most people because it is dressed in overalls and looks like work.”, said somebody.

If your NDP (Network Development Plan) is measured as the first degree of your super trusted relationships, what is the current measure of your Net’s Worth and what has the growth rate looked like in the last few years? What does the future hold? Always, looking for feedback to see how many of you want to Network on this note.

😉

title: X-Factors in the Age of AI
content:
Last week, I wrote about the X-Factors required as major ingredients in our personal growth. A few more thoughts on how to make X-Factors relevant in the age of AI/LLMs. Growth from Eras of Industrial Revolution and ‘.com’ Boom In the Industrial Revolution and the ‘.com’ Revolution, machines and computers significantly improvised traditional businesses. Introduction of tractors led to productivity on the farms, reduced costs and increased volumes of high quality agricultural yields. Introduction of Computers, which were assumed to displace jobs as represented by the media publications in the 80s (remember, there was no social media then), actually led to enhanced jobs, increased productivity and identification of sub-industries through Desktop Publications, Computer Aided Design and many more avenues.

Here is a sneak peek into the distribution of GDP (Gross Domestic Products) over the years and by components. Distribution of GDP for the US as a % of Consumer/Personal consumption (50-60%), Government Spending/Investments (20%), Private Investments (20%) and net of Import and Export (10%) has largely remained the same. Growing and Stable economies will show similar consistency in distribution and the overall GDP grew year-over-year. This will be true for most of the large and stable economies.

Perceived Impact of LLMs on Growth Age of AI/LLMs will make these trends look a bit different. Here is how: 1. Technology workers will have to live through this shift making it personal 2. We have already seen reduction of workforces in recent years and unlike the farmers and manual laborers in the industrial revolution, this phase impacts white-collared workers as well 3. Social Media has already begun scaring us all with the widespread narratives of AI/LLMs taking away jobs 4. Covid is behind us and media’s focus and specialization in scaring humans now talks more about AI as opposed to real problems that exist with wars or growing national debts 5. Manual labor was replaced by machines in the Industrial Revolution and white-collar labor will be replaced/augmented by AI/LLMs in this time and age What it should/will look like?

There is a big HOWEVER to all this. So how will it be different:

1.

Firstly, the productivity gain will be 10x to 100x from the AI advent. In the short term productivity gains will be huge, in the mid-term some jobs will be eliminated/changed and in the long range many more jobs will be created 2. If you are an architect you will be able to create a design in 10th of the time, media houses will be able to significantly increase movie productions, healthcare companies will be able to diagnose problems much quicker and many more benefits like these will be evident 3. As such, more work will be done in volumes, with higher productivity, reducing costs and ballooning the economies 4. You will see a definite convergence of Software and Services (Legal, Healthcare, Financial… you name it) with AI/LLMs. More industries will be created similar to the Industrial Revolution and the ‘.com’ Revolution 5. Economies and GDPs will not ascertain growth without these disruptions. Look back and you will know that even though the distribution of GDP factors have trended with a pattern, there is an enormous growth of global GDPs, standing at over $105T in 2024 compared to $34T in 2000s 6. Creative Conceptualization and Co-Pilots will become more common. Agents and Full-fledged Pilots (Fully Autonomous machines) will come to play in the coming years 7. Planet’s health and Major outbreaks like Covid cannot be averted or avoided without these advents What is that you should do?

2.

If you are in the business of new age, you will need to think about problems that can be solved with a similar leverage 1. Earlier last week, Rahul Jain and his data team, who are working with our technical support leadership shared a framework on how we can take interactions with our customers to deduct sentiments of customers by using our data platform and the LLM invocations from within the platform. He aims to build a reusable framework to take a data set, model it to make it interpretable by LLMs, process the data set with Generative nature of the LLMs and derive sentiments through a reasoning exercise 3. In many ways, you can start leveraging LLMs as a co-pilot for accelerating your code development and possibly for conceptualizing many aspects of your job or brand new ideas 4. In the simplest form, use the LLMs to generate responses, reason with them to improvise and engage with LLMs interactively on a daily basis. All of us need to comprehend the fact that we spend a lot more time on social media compared to these tools so the opportunity to leverage these frameworks is huge 5. There is already a portion of the population that depends on the states/governments for livelihood. This portion might increase somewhat with the dominance of AI and you need to figure out which side of the line you want to be on, to be a dependent or to be a creator We recently launched an AI for Everyone program for 50 of our employees to provide introduction to AI/LLMs, access to detailed coursework, sessions with industry experts and potential opportunities for cross-functional teams to identify problems that can be solved with LLMs hosted on our own Data Cloud. Are you participating? If not, do you know what it takes?

title: We Don't Need No Education
content:
About a decade ago a young man, my colleague’s son, came to me for academic guidance to pick a stream of education in engineering. I suggested to him a simple exercise since he came in with a clean slate: make a list of engineering streams and evaluate each of the streams with what the future holds for them with growth, transformation and employment in perspective. Evaluate for another factor to tie the stream with your passion and natural inclination for study. I gave him some pointers for the first part.

He chose to study chemical engineering and finished his postgraduate in industrial engineering to start his career as a software engineer. Imagine how much of a jump start was missed. Another situation, I am interviewing someone with 15+ years of work experience and this individual worked for her current employer for over a dozen years. To my question of how do you build skills for the future, the response was ‘I listen to podcasts of my leaders during my drive time’. I asked her to name a topic or quote an example, nothing specific she could respond with.

When I interview, I often ask about a candidate’s learning ability and intellectual investment, specifically to understand their knowledge media and areas of interest.

So why this preface… ? Let’s unfold.

Cost of Education As depicted in the image, technology costs have continuously decreased over the 20 years in the bottom set while the cost of education has grown exponentially, only to be second to healthcare costs. Even though this represents only the US, other geographies experience similar trends.

If we look at the options available on e-learning platforms, the adjacent and specialized training curricula is available much easily and at a very affordable price point. This is a very logical economically attractive middle ground where informal academia leverages technology. Exaggeration from Academia Latest reported numbers from Hindustan Times* indicate that 36% of 2024 IIT Mumbai graduates are yet to receive a job placement. It is hard to ascertain at this point if this is a one time representation or a trend representing a mismatch between academia and the changing needs of professional organizations, or even the sheer number of jobs out there. I have already seen many graduates from non-prime/non-IIT institutes that are either unemployed or that they move into significantly underpaying/underskilled job opportunities.

Many times we get attracted to a formal MBA like program. Incremental investment in a formal master’s degree can lead to a one time big ticket price or a long term debt that you will repay through loan payments. Whereas, focused learning programs like becoming a flight attendant or nurse offer a very relevant work opportunity with a much quicker/better return on academic investment.

While not discounting the value of education, it is really important to appreciate the skill gained compared to the demands across industries. In some cases, it is also okay to stick to your initial career choice in alignment with your academic qualification. This needs both patience and perseverance.

What holds true

### Here are some guardrails to employ

- If you want to pursue higher education, especially if it involves a big financial commitment and/or a new stream, ensure you have validated with all possible resources. If you are on the other side you can always look to offer help to those aspiring individuals. As with the young man’s reference in my initial mention, do your homework and keep your ears open
- A skill is not mastered in academia. It only offers a foundation. Practice will make you perfect so perseverance is the key. Remember the 10,000 hour references from Malcom Gladwell to become an Outlier
- Education will never stop. If you are in a profession, it needs continuous upkeep, upskilling and leverage on auxiliary training. Nothing works like on-the-job work/learning opportunities, in my opinion even better than an MBA. Imagine getting paid for learning so always look out for additional responsibilities which are not a part of your job’s description
- Our internship programs and job rotation programs (we have one offered in the finance org as of today) are built on the premise to bridge the gaps between academia and professional needs
- Snowflake Academia Program recently launched by our teams in India is already reaching out to a dozen of academic institutions to bring their faculty and students in close connection with our data cloud In conclusion What is unique about your learning journey? What experiences do you have to share? What can your leader/organization do to better them?

*https://www.hindustantimes.com/cities/mumbai-news/36-iit-b-students-fail-to-get-jobs-in-24-101 712084983374.html

title: The Hardness of Soft Skills
content:
Sandeep, our PMO leader, and I were catching up earlier last week and the thought came up on the importance of soft skills. needs improvement - Not only are the soft skills hard to acquire, it is much harder to know how many of these each of us need to improve upon. I will divide the write-up in parts to describe Incognizance or unawareness, how to put them in practice, how they impact our lives and about the hurdles of putting them to practice.

Incognizance A friend had once told me how in his village, of course in India, when the doors opened up at the movie cinemas, people would rush to get in and to his surprise the rush would be to occupy the first few rows to be the first ones to watch the movie. Ha ha… Let’s move the same mindset to the corporate world.

Similarly, have you seen how at the company all hands first few rows of seats remain unoccupied due to the comfort found in the backbenches or from the inherent mindset to avoid facial contact. I have also seen similarly that during leadership dinners or events, very few gather the courage to find a seat next to the host leader. Somehow this undocumented law of corporate physics states that the distance from the leader is proportionate to the layers in the organizational hierarchy of the individual’s position. Even in my staff meetings there is a usual bunch that finds comfort in occupying the couch seats at the back. It is often cited as, ‘oh, this is very comfortable’ and not realizing that this demonstrates your innate inability to pounce on the seat at the table.

This has often caused annoyance especially for the leaders that come from a different culture or geography. My personal expectation is that every employed individual must quickly learn to grab that seat at the table, the first key to developing strength in your soft skills. Expected respect for the elders in some cultures or the implied lessons taught in traditional academic setup lead to these behaviors of not finding your seat and become a roadblock for both the individual and the organization’s growth.

Putting them to Practice Once you have your seat at the table, the first thing is to ensure you are willing to and open to communicating. This necessitates having a clear thought process, being objective and brutally honest about your thoughts and to be able to speak up at the right point in time. Over the years, as much as I expect crispness in a message, I have built a strong mindset to focus on the message more than the messenger, especially if English is not a primarily spoken language of communication for individuals.

Secondly, people live a lot of life around ‘he said so or she said so’ and easily deviate from the topic of purpose in the conversation or meetings. Strong Emotional Intelligence in communicating with a purpose is far more superior than your subject matter expertise. Did the other person sense respect in your tone, did you focus on the core issue and have clearly defined expectations and associated outcomes when communicating?

Another key aspect is your ability to build productive workplace relationships. Do you know people outside of their job designations? Do you stay in the comforting company of usual suspects or build superior relationships and new connections every day? Do you ensure to greet people in the hallway?

On a very positive note, Mike Scarpelli, our CFO was greatly impressed with the culture that we demonstrated in showing up for his all hands. He witnessed an outstanding enthusiasm in the engagement and the conversations. The reason for this mention is to leave a question for each of you: Did you study about Mike and his role prior to his arrival? Did you contribute to the collective enthusiasm?

There are many other facets of soft skills demonstrated in workplace etiquettes, in your grooming, in showing up on-time, in following up for your tasks and even letting people know in a timely manner when the expected timelines cannot be met. Others don’t like surprises as much as our own dislike of them.

Impact of Soft Skills on our lives Organizations and people excelling in these skills create a high performing culture, have flawless and common understanding of complex topics/initiatives at hand, they collaborate effectively with hand-offs and provide great support to each other. Our collective problem solving capacity increases tremendously and no goal remains unachievable.

Hurdles The biggest hurdle in this can only be ‘me’. Positivity rubs on easily. Your being really good in these skills will bring reciprocation from all others with much ease.

What is your assessment? Do you know your blind spots? Do you/we excel with these? Do you ever judge others or just focus on playing your part? Did I miss a skill that could be mentioned here or in another writeup?

title: A Billion People and Their LLMs
content:
In the 18 months since ChatGPT was first launched, more than a billion people have accessed Large Language Models. They used them to answer questions, write poetries, craft emails and as copilots. Some of them used them to create advanced applications on top of these models. Snowflake also announced its Arctic models that are trained cost-effectively, efficiently intelligent, truly open LLM and using a unique Dense-MoE Hybrid transformer architecture. I tried to understand our announcement myself and had to ask some fundamental questions. Let’s examine at length.

What is a Closed Source Model? ChatGPT-4 and other versions, developed by OpenAI, are closed source language models. This means that their source code and training data are not publicly accessible or open for modification by the general public. OpenAI uses proprietary data and techniques to train and improve the performance of ChatGPT models, keeping these methods and the underlying code confidential.

What is an Open Source Model? For example, Llama 3 is open source. It is a large language model developed by Meta and is designed to be accessible to developers, researchers, and businesses for building, experimenting, and scaling generative AI applications responsibly. What makes the Snowflake Arctic model Truly Open then? Snowflake is sharing its research insights through a comprehensive ‘cookbook’ that opens up findings. The cookbook is designed to expedite the learning process for anyone looking to build world-class MoE models. Apache 2.0 license provides ungated access to weights and code. Apache 2.0 allows free use, modification and distribution of Arctic even for commercial purposes.

How is the Snowflake Arctic Model trained effectively? Arctic was trained with many-but-condensed experts with more expert choices. Arctic was trained with a three-stage curriculum each with a different data composition focusing on generic skills in the first phase (1T Tokens), and enterprise-focused skills in the latter two phases (1.5T and 1T tokens). If you look at the phase 1 column below a lot of focus is demonstrated on generic skills learning from the Web and more focused approach on code, SQL and STEM in phases 2 & 3.

How is the Arctic model Efficiently Intelligent? Arctic excels at enterprise tasks such as SQL generation, coding and instruction following benchmarks even when compared to open source models trained with significantly higher compute budgets. This will enable Snowflake customers to build applications and leverage models at a lower cost So what the heck is the Dense MoE Hybrid Transformer Architecture?

A dense transformer architecture like in Llama 2 or Llama 3 allows learning from layers of information to integrate and learn from vast amounts of data.

MoE (Mixture of Experts) divides the model into many smaller specialized parts, each called an “expert”. Each expert is good at handling specific types of tasks based on the question’s content. Similar to different specialist doctors at a hospital for a given medical issue or in the case of a data platform like us whether a question pertains to SQL, Code or following complex instructions.

In Conclusion The Arctic model provides a very high inference efficiency to allow for practical deployment of our models at a low cost. At large, there is a big threat in open source LLMs replacing search engines.

Have you played with our LLM at arctic.streamlit.app? I am still learning as this unfolds. Are you? Is there anything else that can be looked at?

title: Emulating Human Intelligence in AI
content:
Humans Machine’s Intelligence in AI Disruptions Interacting with leaders in Bangalore, earlier last week, I started my talk with these snippets and asked them to identify if there is a fictional representation in any one of these:

- Klarna, a Swedish online shopping company bragged earlier in February that the company’s new OpenAI-powered customer service chatbot was doing the work of 700 people—nearly two years after Klarna laid off about 700 people
- K Krithivasan, chief executive officer and managing director of Tata Consultancy Services, the country's largest and globally second-largest information technology (IT) services company, said that there would be a 'minimal' requirement for call centers due to the rapid adoption of AI
- Cognition, a leading tech company, has introduced Devin AI, the world’s first AI software engineer. This innovation promises to speed development by using the power of AI-powered coding and machine learning Of course, they are all representations of reality.

Where is AI going?

I watched Sam Altman’s interaction video with Stanford students and here are a few snippets as heard from Sam.

- ChatGPT 4 is mildly embarrassing and is the dumbest model any of you will ever have used. OpenAI’s premise is to ship early and often, develop iteratively and deploy quickly. They aim to put product in people’s hands and let the society tell what works or does not work
- They are building to create AGI (Artificial General Intelligence). It will be expensive to make and will be totally worth it. AGI, as previously defined by Sam, can demonstrate competence of a median human in solving tasks. As of this talk, he feels that this is a loose definition. It felt harder to revisit and redefine however AGI will certainly create a tremendous GDP growth
- He responded to questions and said that he is not great at many things however he is good at a lot of things I want you to put two things in perspective for the introspection in the next para. One about AGI, to Sam’s earlier definition, for it to be better than a median human being and second about being good at many things and not great with things, largely speaking.

Emulating Machine in Humans: Where are you going?

We as humans tend to get hyper specialized with one or two skills and generally speaking the breadth of knowledge or awareness is underrated in this world, Sam added at some point in his talk. Assuming AGI (Artificial General Intelligence) will surpass the median human’s intelligence and given the importance of breadth in life, we need to build a stronger ability to connect with more ideas to do something useful and interesting. This will keep us much higher compared to median capability capture by AGI 1. LLMs for Humans: What is your Large Learning Language Model? How often do you train? Do you struggle to infer impromptu? How do you divide between Reflexive (Quick) or Deliberative (Thoughtful) responses?

2. GPUs for Humans: Besides your learning models (training), you need energy sources (equivalent to GPUs) for your inspiration. You can get energy from many things including sports, exercise, meditation, music, friends, learning and travel. Be aware of your energy sources/GPUs that allow you to compute effectively and infer reflexively or deliberatively, as required.

3. Modeled Training: Train your personal model through knowledge acquisition, learning new skills and most importantly utilizing these skills on-the-job in generating value or in building products. To go back to the breadth question from Sam, are you getting trained from Mixture of Expertise or are passionately stuck to just one or two things? 4. Reinforced Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF): When solving problems largely we must trust and use our knowledge gained, in addition to referencing past experiences. Additionally, similar to the models getting trained explicitly from human feedback, we must have a mechanism to seek feedback. My feedback comes from many directions and I greatly rely on feedback from the next generations, which I hope will keep me relevant.

Lastly, a lot of reinforcement comes from learning through our hallucinations or mistakes, much like the models. Best of the models are wrong one out of six times and hopefully we as humans are better than them, at least as of today.

So keep learning new things, more so from your mistakes and build a learning pace faster than the rate at which large language models improvise. For additional reinforcement based learning, have a solid feedback mechanism from your circle of trust, identify a coach or a mentor you can go to and build a real solid professional network. 5. Training and Inference Capacity: This element refers to the need of the hour. It is assumed that a lot of the GPUs purchased in the past six months need to be used for inference and building AI applications, as many models have already been built and the new ones like Snowflake’s Arctic are being built very quickly and efficiently. Similarly, you probably already have a lot of knowledge gained so here is an opportunity to leverage a lot more to interactively infer that knowledge to communicate and generate value similar to AI applications. Do you?

In Conclusion As Unsupervised ML models largely use neural networks to replicate human brains in machines, above was a reversed narrative for humans to now emulate AI like behaviors. Hopefully, these behaviors get us in the higher percentile of intellect and not become a subject of dependence on state based subsidies.

title: The Basics of Cellular Biology
content:
Human body is made up of trillions of cells. Cells maintain the organism’s health and functionality. Cells are divided into many parts including Nucleus, Mitochondria and others. Nucleus includes Chromosomes or (DNA) Deoxyribonucleic Acid. High School Biology stuff, pretty much. DNA is the hereditary material containing the instructions required for the organisms to develop, survive and reproduce.

Human gut microbiome has good and bad bacteria, higher number of good bacteria and lower number of bad bacteria lead to a healthy gut. Bacteria use CRISPR (Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats) for immunity against viruses. CRISPR-associated systems (CAS) proteins are adaptive immune systems in bacteria that protect against viruses. If this was too hard to process, which it was for me, just remember that bacteria need healthy proteins like CAS to fight against viruses. Nucleus of a cell contains DNA which helps us to develop, survive and reproduce. DNA’s health determines an organism’s health. Lastly, Gene is a segment of DNA.

Genes are inside the DNA and help produce the proteins. Remember, we often talk about genes

- being good or bad or even as a personification of personality inheritance. So, why all this? Gene Editing with Protein Language Model Scientists in Harvard and Berkeley in 2010 used CAS proteins to do targeted DNA/Gene editing in plant and human cells. With the ability to edit genes, we can kill diseases. T Cells in our bodies can attack cancer cells or even change the structure of plants to increase yield and make them disease resistant.

Gene editing requires constant search for CAS proteins though underway for the last 10 years, it has been a slow process. Profluent, a company with a tagline: ‘We create bespoke proteins for breakthrough medicines’, created an AI model or Protein Language Model (PML), ha ha, to create a new library of CAS proteins - they took 26 Trillions of Genome data repository. Through the inference of this large data set they discovered openCRISPR-1 protein which is now freely available as open source for use and research. open-CRISPR-1 protein is 400 mutations away from anything found in nature, discovered purely through inference on the Protein Language Model. Do you remember how one or two mutations of Corona virus created havoc in those days. We are looking at 400 such mutations with a positive connotation this time. Proteins make the precise cuts in genes/DNA and AI started to predict which proteins would make a good gene editor to create dozens of gene editing molecules.

In short, Profluent took all of the publicly available open genome databases and created a Protein Language Model on top of this - this dataset can be downloaded and ingested. Profluent took 26T base pairs of data to train their model; they used the model to run inference on it to create CAS systems that are far more powerful and have efficacies greater than CAS proteins available in nature.

In Conclusion Whether it is a language, images, music, videos or proteins in this case, large sets of high quality domain specific data can be leveraged if organized correctly, with parameters labeled and trained to build unimaginable inferences. RLHF (Reinforced Learning with Human Feedback) with robust product distribution, extraordinary experiences, AI enabled applications with a fundamental open source approach will rewrite the history of mankind, perhaps even rescue the planet.

title: Unending Grit at Berkshire Hathaway
content:
Very recently, Berkshire Hathaway sold 115 Million of their close to 900 Million shareholding of Apple shares. They currently hold more than $182B in cash as a result.

Earlier last week, at the annual shareholders day, Warren Buffett, 93, who is the CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, a multinational conglomerate holding company, addressed the audience for an engaging 7 hour long interaction along with his team. Charlie Munger, vice chairman of the company, who passed away in November 2023, lived a fulfilling life of over 99 years and was remembered all the time at the meeting. Here are some snippets from the shareholder’s meeting, jewels of the keynote message from Mr. Buffett and a few other critical details. A single ‘class A’ share of Berkshire Hathaway is valued at $629,000 as I write this note. Class A shares typically offer more voting rights and dividends compared to Class B shares, and are often issued to founders and executives to maintain control over the company.

- Even after selling 115 Million shares, they will have the largest common shareholding of Apple. Besides Apple, the company is also greatly invested in Coca Cola and American Express. Their premise of investment is in the core businesses while not making an attempt to predict the market or the share prices. They look at the companies as businesses in a way they serve and not by the moving averages of their share prices. Warren also reflected on the fact that given the current conditions, they are happy to build a cash position for $182B as getting close to 5% return from interest income is not a bad value proposition till attractive investment opportunities come along.
- Remember, they were one of Snowflake’s early investors and hold close to a $1B worth of the Snowflake's market share
- Answering why Berkshire Hathaway would not consider investing in China, HongKong or another foreign country, he mentioned that the primary investments will always be in the US. Coke, Apple and American Express as part of the companies they have invested in do business around the world. Understanding the US rules, policies, strengths and weaknesses provides a solid comfort. The US started as a 0.5% of world’s population to where it is now managing 24%+ of global outputs ($25T is US' GDP in 2023 compared to $105T for the world). He adds to say that 90%+ of the civilization evolved in the last 2 centuries prior to which there was not much of a global context with industrial revolutions. To be in a country where you have policy clarity, political stability, investment in infrastructure and in people's wellbeing is a great place to be.
- As advice for young people, he stated that you are lucky to be born in a country where there are so many opportunities. Warren stated that according to Charlie Munger, who I mentioned about earlier, you should define your goals based on what you want your obituary to read. He advises to make choices for academics, social circuit, your partner, career and a job as tied to this eventual outcome of your life. Be the source for your partner similarly to achieve his/her objectives. With your career and your jobs you will make mistakes here and there and sometimes you will get lucky. Correct your course as required.
- Memories of Charlie as Buffett’s partner are strongly etched so much that Warren referred to his co-presenter Greg Abel as Charlie. Warren confessed that he will make the same mistake again during the event. In Warren’s words, Charlie was an architect that dreamt, designed the blueprint and supervised the construction of this super successful business. Their partnership lasted for 64 years up until Munger passed away in 2023.
- Beyond investing in core businesses driven based on services the businesses provide, they stay away from investments derivatives, cryptocurrencies and speculative asset classes.

Listening to this event from a 93 year old ‘Oracle of Omaha: the most successful investor in the world’ made me realize the grit that exists at his age, importance of partnerships/relationships, looking at your investments as businesses providing superior products/services, trusting the countries run with a robust governance, and the need for having your actions closely tied to what you want your obituary to say.

How do you resonate? How are you different? Who is your idol? What is your investment strategy? Do you understand your company, country and companies that you invest in?

title: Life as It Exists and as It Existed
content:
Most Sunday mornings Nikhil, our finance leader, goes to bring a delicious misal for his family. This misal at Shrikrishna has batata bhaaji (spiced potato mixture), cooked pohe (flattened rice), pohe chiwda (a savoury snack mix), and shev (gram flour noodles) topped with onions and coriander (cilantro). Yummy, right? There is a caveat though - the restaurant does not pack it for him unless he brings with him a container along. He carries a stainless steel container to bring the gravy back.

Similarly, you may have seen a lot of supermarkets around the world that have containers filled with nuts and grains. You pack as much you would like in a bag that you may carry along. Gen Xers and Babyboomers actually experienced this phenomenon at grocery stores as the primary mechanism.

Let’s also add to this context the prevalence of khadi (traditional homespun cotton cloth), eating homemade ghee, yogurt and many more organic creations.

Everything that we make is made from the resources on the planet and sent back to the planet so what corrupts the planet? I am not an expert on this topic however one thing that has bothered me for a while is the context for this write-up: Microplastics.

Some Definitions Plastic Making: Plastic is made by extracting petrochemical oils and natural gas from the earth as a first thing. These oils are converted to ethylene and propylene which are transferred into polymers like polyethylene, the most common plastic. You have heard of Polythene bags which represent this form.

Phthalates (prefix ‘ph’ is silent): Phthalates are used to make plastics more flexible and found in vinyl flooring, plastic containers, lubricating oils, soaps and shampoos.

Bisphenol A or BPA: These are chemicals used to make plastic more transparent and clear, like in the making of water bottles.

Endocrine Functions: As part of Endocrine function, glands in our body release hormones into the bloodstream to regulate growth, metabolism and reproductive development. They make life happen.

Do you know of the following ‘Corruptions’ by Plastic?

- Consumer Reports testing of many food products have for long shown evident traces of phthalates and bisphenol. Consuming packaged food products in the form of plastic packaging, cans and cardboards introduces traces of these carcinogens, cancer causing elements.
- Manufacturing of meat and processed food involves feed for animals and other elements of supply chain like packaging and movement which also increase exposure to the phthalates and bisphenol
- Traces of these carcinogens exist everywhere in the food, in the air we breath, in the water we drink, in the synthetic clothes we wear, in the phones we use, car tyres and many other unimaginable places
- Although greatly controlled and reduced in today’s terms, the amount of bisphenol A (BPA) found in thermal receipts (prints of your purchases) could be very high compared to what is found in canned/packaged food Concerns have been raised about potential health effects from phthalates and bisphenol , particularly their ability to disrupt endocrine function and their possible links to health issues like asthma, reproductive disorders, and developmental problems in children.

So what?

- It is estimated that we consume 500 micrograms (0.5 gram) of phthalates daily and a lot of it metabolizes through excretion
- The movement of phthalates during the metabolism impacts the endocrine system and further impacts on our reproductive system, body temperature, blood pressure and moods besides being a potential carcinogenic residue
- Accumulation of these polymers from breakage into smaller parts makes them stick in your tissue
- Plaque removed from the bloodstream of 304 patients in Italy during cardiac procedures was examined with electron microscopy. Examination revealed half the patients had 1 part of micro/nanoplastic for every 50 parts of the plaque. This residue makes you 4.5 times more vulnerable for a heart attack compared to those who do not show the residue
- Microplastics from PVCs (Polyvinyl Chloride as in from PVC pipes) and PETs (remember the PET bottles) get bounded to cells and release a substance called Cytokine leading to inflammations and ultimately health hazards What can we do? Where does the world go from here?

In short, you cannot get away from this exposure to a highly corrupted supply chain. You can only manage to reduce risks

- Refuse at the source, Reduce where possible and Recycle. Avoid disposable materials as much as possible
- Glass and Cans are relatively safer however the environmental burden from creating and disposing these is very high
- Be like Nikhil, bring your container where possible
- Don’t be a celebrity to drink Fijian, Glacier or Recruitment Bay (for Snowflakes) bottled water as you are certainly dealing with the container based risks
- Hopefully, there will be regulations from FDA and other agencies to declare exposure to microplastics, phthalates and bisphenols as part of product labeling
- Don’t buy wrapped fruit, most are already wrapped by nature in peels
- Bioplastics (created from living organisms) are the way however far away for practicality and cost efficiencies, similar to energy expectations at scale from solar, efficient batteries, green hydrogen or nuclear fission based powers
- Planet needs to cool down by 1.7 degrees within the next two decades. We desperately need a ‘Quantum’ for Solace which I will share shortly.

If you have different points of view that will help strengthen this message, we are all ears. A big thank you to David Friedberg for his best captures in the science corner and to all the references made by him https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nFaiCmOD6Gg https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c9HEjdjyVn4

title: Quantum for Solace
content:
Last week somewhere in the write-up I mentioned about the need for our planet to cool down by 1.7 degrees within the next two decades. We desperately need a ‘Quantum’ for Solace. Here is a drill down.

Defining Quantum Computers Our computers today, the servers, the laptops and the smartphones are built on bits (binary digits that are either a 0 or a 1). Quantum Computers are built on Qubits which can be zero or one simultaneously, a basic feature of a quantum computer.

Superposition: The state of being zero or one simultaneously is called superposition. Entanglement: These qubits can entangle with each other which allow quantum computers to grow exponentially. 2 Qubits can store and process 4 bits, 3 to 8, 4 to 16 and so on. This exponential scaling makes quantum computers far more powerful than traditional computers. In our computers today, you provide input data in binary (digital) format to process data and generate outputs based on certain algorithms. In a quantum computer, the input is provided in the form of qubits that can interact with one another. Leveraging Superposition (to be 0 or 1 simultaneously) and Entanglement of Qubits allows Quantum Computer to carry many calculations simultaneously making it very powerful. Largest quantum computer today can compute on 1,225 qubits and can represent 2^1225 states simultaneously.

So what? Compared to classical Supercomputer capabilities which can predict weather or do large scale machine learning, to avert greenhouse effect crisis on the planet and deeply impact the molecular structures for drug discovery, significantly increasing agricultural yield quantum computers will be the key.

Race for the Survival of (Wo)mankind: Building a fault tolerant quantum computer is still a slow process and it is expected that we must reduce global warming between 1.7°C and 1.8°C by 2050 to avoid catastrophic climate changes. The challenge with the existing quantum computers is that compared to 1,225 qubits in the largest machines we need millions of qubits to interact and without volatility (inaccuracy and unreliability). Also the power and cooling requirements for quantum computers today are cost prohibitive.

Increased carbon levels enhance the greenhouse effect, causing more extreme weather, rising sea levels, and disruptions to ecosystems and human societies. At 422 parts per million in the atmosphere (0.0422%), carbon presence is at the highest level in millions of years. Becoming carbon neutral without increasing the carbon footprint is a key to address the challenges arising from global warming.

How can quantum computing help?

- Quantum Computers can help improve the chemistry of batteries to improve energy density of batteries, to identify replacement materials for the composition of batteries and significantly reduce the cost of grid scale storage of solar plants. Imagine cost and power efficiencies for both the solar plants and the batteries through this improvisation
- Did you know that a lot of Carbon Dioxide emissions are from manufacturing of cement, a quintessential commodity in the construction business. Study of materials and process involved in the cement manufacturing is a great candidate for Quantum Computing leverage
- Green Hydrogen, great potential to replace fossil fuel, costs 60% more than natural gas. Quantum can help introduce pulsing to efficiently split water (H2O) into green Hydrogen and Oxygen.

There are many many more value propositions from the build out of a scaled Quantum machine. Are the definitions of Superpositions and entanglement as introduction of Quantum Computing too hard to process compared to all that is going on in the AI/ML/LLM worlds? Yes, Indeed this is hard for laymen to process.

Just know that Albert Einstein had difficulties accepting some aspects of quantum physics. Despite his role in its early development, he was troubled by its inherent randomness and lack of determinism, famously stating, "God does not play dice with the universe."

title: Maximum Cities
content:
Earlier last week, I was at an airport lounge in Bangalore and I ran into Victoria. She was helping with the catering and had been there for two years. To my surprise, she moved there from Andaman and Nicobar Islands, which is quite rare. She seemed to have some problems with her family situation, also possibly with the city and was planning to move back to the islands. This conversation happened in less than a minute. This made me wonder what it takes to be in a big city, especially a big Hi-Tech Metro in a country like India. I spent two days in the city of Bangalore, attending an event. Hotel was about 15 minutes walking distance from the convention center. When I was walking on the sidewalk, a motorcycle rider went past me on the sidewalk, at a very high speed of about 40 km/h, as the roads were busy. These riders often use the sidewalk to ride during the peak hours. I have seen this behavior in the cities of Mumbai and Pune as well.

Crossing the road in Bangalore was an adventure in itself. Nobody stopped for the pedestrians to cross the road. When you have about half a dozen other walking citizens, one of them musters the courage to start walking through the high speeding traffic and the other citizens join him or her. As crazy as it sounds, eventually life works out.

The Facade At the event, addressing the leaders of the high-tech technology companies a bureaucrat representing the state of Karnataka started selling all the greatness the geography has to offer. Great weather, access to existing successful companies, academia, talent and the great economic progress that the state has made.

The bureaucrat failed to address the massive challenges with traffic and the severe lack of infrastructure.

All of us try to present the opportunity while greatly hiding the underlying problems. This is not unique to one individual, a division, a company or a city/state. This is not a criticism for one state over the other but it makes me wonder how we as a bureaucrat or an employee of a multinational company change our mindset. For a moment, we are on the busy roads of the third world and quickly switch gears to the first world as soon as the air conditioning at the office kicks in.

Check Points in a Global Environment To build a corollary and to understand the blind spots, let’s examine a few data points: When I picked my current job at Snowflake, it did not have a well defined perspective on what the role entailed as we were in a formative state of the company and of the geography.

- What was expected? At first, it was imperative to understand the expectations. We built the geography as an arbitrage for talent availability and cost management. We had to have clarity in how we would build talent through academia and lateral hiring, while leveraging our personal network for referrals
- Workplace: Clear mandate was to ensure that we built a world class work environment that would be equal to or better than our global offices. So, as stated above, we brought in the first world air conditioning to the office (pun intended)
- Exposing the World here to the World there: In all the years, with Snowflake and prior to that, we have encouraged the visitors to take the bus with our employees so that they can see the speeding riders on the sidewalk (pun intended, again). In their latest visit our leadership agreed to cancel their plane travel and took the highway to travel from Mumbai to Pune and enjoy eating Vada Pav on the way. This brought them closer to the reality of our adventurous lives and left with them long lasting taste buds of sumptuous Vada Pav (Vada Pav is a popular Indian street food consisting of a deep-fried potato dumpling placed inside a bread bun).
- Reality Check: In the years, we also realized that owing to geographic vicinity and skill unavailability, we could not hire for all the functions and positions in India. This equation is changing as skills become generally available over time and with the organization gaining maturity. We constantly evaluate and communicate both ways with an honest assessment showcasing the art of possible.

While this is just a snapshot of a few aspects, it gives a material view of being honest and open in your engagement expressing both the opportunities and the challenges associated, in both directions.

What does your experience tell you? How do you communicate honestly? How do you maintain consistency to be in the same world inside and outside the workplace? Are there gotchas you would like to share?

title: Memory Lanes of Casualties
content:
Earlier in May, an underage inebriated driver ran over a young couple while driving his fancy vehicle at a very high speed in a residential pocket not too far from my residence in Pune. The young immigrant couple lost their lives causing a huge uproar in the city, social media and with the country at large. It is still reminiscent somewhat in our memories and we are still in the middle of the legal proceedings. Hopefully, justice will be served and people will not forget that easily.

Russia-Ukraine war estimates a tally of 500,000 deaths between soldiers and civilians. Many thousands of deaths resulted from the recent Palestinian-Israeli war. One thing that most of us have easily forgotten is the deaths from Covid in the 3 years since the epidemic began. It is estimated to have caused more than 10 Million deaths from the pandemic.

Covid - The Wuhan Virus: Two sides of the One big pandemic It is yet to be established if the pandemic originated from the lab in Wuhan or from the wet markets in the vicinity of Wuhan. As we stand in the middle of 2024, the US Officials are divided between the two theories. Palki Sharma Upadhyay, a bold journalist who moved from WION to Forbes, always referred to this pandemic as ‘The Wuhan Virus’ irrespective of the applicability of the theories. World was angry and it still somewhat remains both angered and puzzled without the conclusion or the accountability for the pandemic.

First Side: The Case for Natural Transmission Similar to the emergence of SARS and MERS from Asia and Middle East respectively, many point to Covid as an emergence from animal-to-human transmission. Two scientific white papers point to the Huanan Seafood Market in Wuhan. Second Side: The Case for a Lab Leak The Wuhan lab maintained “one of the world’s largest repositories of bat samples, which has enabled its coronavirus research,” U.S. intelligence officials have written. Let’s examine this one at a length and a few entities to understand before that: NIH (National Institute of Health): is the US government's leading medical research agency. It funds universities and hospitals, and conducts its own studies, to fight diseases and improve health.

Eco-Health: is a non-profit organization with a research focus on infectious diseases. WIV (Wuhan Institute of Virology) aka the Wuhan Lab: a Chinese research facility that studies the Coronavirus.

Gain-of-function research tweaks organisms, often viruses, in a lab to understand how diseases work and develop treatments Eco-Health facilitated research dollars from NIH to the Wuhan Lab for the gain of functions research in Wuhan. All evidence according to some of the divided scientists point that the virus emerged from the gain of functions research in the Wuhan Lab. The original intent was possibly to research and prepare for the next pandemic which led to the pandemic itself. So essentially the effort to try and solve the problem would very likely have caused the problem itself.

- The US Government since then has stopped all the fundings to Eco-Health
- A lot of interrogations, though inconclusive so far, are still work in progress
- Prof. Jay Bhattacharya of Stanford University is a prominent figure in the COVID-19 pandemic discussion. He argued against the lockdown and as a result he was banned from speaking on Social Media
- Series of email communications with NIH, Eco-Health and virus theory were deleted. In some instances the government business was conducted on private messages, which has happened in the past as well The Implications from the Pandemic are far fetched and still not over: 1. Macroeconomic consequences led to global massive subsidies, government fundings, kickbacks, economic slowdown and many other unnecessary expenses around the globe 2. Huge governmental debts for the next few generations to deal with 3. We shut down the economy and slowed down the growth 4. There were Immature medicinal responses for oxygen supply, medicines, injections, vaccines and other myths 5. At least 13 communicable diseases are surging past pre-pandemic levels 6. 10+ Millions of Deaths. Suicides 7. Our children lost 2 years of outside life 8. Supply Chain Crisis 9. Political upheaval around the globe 10.Bat Virus that was not transmissible was possibly reengineered to make it transmissible While the findings still remain inconclusive we must continue to make them conclusive.

### Additionally

Recommendations for the Future 1. Gain of Function Research must be discouraged 2. Countries must not build labs in and around population centers 3. If at all build them on an island 4. If there is leak don’t lie about it 5. Don’t become a doctor to the world when you are hiding the truth Credits https://www.nytimes.com/2024/06/14/briefing/two-covid-theories.html https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2024-06-14/why-is-everyone-getting-sick-beh ind-the-global-rise-in-rsv-flu-measles?utm_campaign=news&utm_medium=bd&utm_sour ce=applenews https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R6hJh-OwoZw

title: When Value Is Invalidated
content:
Recently we replaced a subscription of a CBT platform with pay per use methodology. Our apparent understanding of value created from a CBT platform was invalidated from low usage. Vendor did not care to track the usage as the commission from selling the software was seat based. Ultimately, we did not renew the contract and the supplier keeps chasing us.

### Market Sentiments leading to Volatility in Stock Prices

For having reported revenue lower by $40M ($9.13B reported vs. $9.17B analyst forecast), including 11% Year-over-Year growth, as an absolute number just equating to less than 0.5% of the forecast revenue, Salesforce.com dropped its valuation by a whopping $40B (20%) in just one day.

We have seen similar valuation movements similarly either as a big event or multiple events triggering big movement in the valuation of the company which directly correlates to the traded share price.

Why is it that Salesforce.com valuations and stock prices are beaten up despite growths in revenue, customer base and profitability?

- Market still expects continuous high growth from SaaS and high technology companies which is not sustainable. The projection is for Salesforce to grow at 7% Q-o-Q however this is not good enough
- With $9B in free cash flow for Salesforce the valuation is still at 20x. Revenue to Valuation (Share Price) is still over 6x. $237B of market cap today compared to annual revenue of $39B
- Lot of expenses are reduced and the profitability of the company is still high however the valuation is still beat up Critical Indicators of Interest While there isn’t a specific one or two things to point a finger at, here are some likely indicators. This theory will be applicable for all the SaaS and technology spendings not just limiting to the Salesforce.com situation
- Economy is cooling off and as a result the spending is a lot more tightened. Global GDP forecast is lower by 1.4%. US Federal Reserve moved the interest rate from almost 0% to a number close to 5% in a very short duration
- Enterprises are very cautious about making investments in multi-million multi-year contracts
- Software Development is getting more commoditized especially in the AI/LLM based world
- There are considerations for both Build vs. Buy and cheaper alternatives are evaluated
- Companies are getting more conscious of software and/or seats sitting on the shelf from under-usage. In some cases, a lot of the paid features of a seat remain unused
- AI capabilities offered by Enterprise scale SaaS software are lot more expensive than what comes from open source models or alternative AI platforms
- Similar to client-server data center based software getting replaced by SaaS offering, AI enabled stack will offer similar opportunities to displace SaaS
- To a large extent, 80% features are far more acceptable at less than 50% cost compared to having precise software solutions
- Somewhere customers are also envisioning pay per use (Consumption) as opposed to pay per seat (Subscription) licensing models. Seat/License as a proxy for value is no longer valid So What? Here are some key questions worth evaluating:
- Are we scratching the surface where machines are replacing humans?
- Is the economy continuing to push us to do more with less? We always have thoughts so however have we acted accordingly?
- To a lot of the leaders growth is a function of headcount in the org as opposed to the generated value or impact. Have you seen a drive for creating value without involving more heads?
- Trends show that founder run/involved companies do very well over the long run. Similar to Jensen @NVIDIA and Musk @Tesla, Marc Benihoff is still in-charge. Will he turn the game around for Salesforce?

title: Ripe Like an Alphonso, Age Like a Vintage
content:
As a young person, you aim to ripe like alphonso, the king of fruit, bursting with vibrant potential and energy, to make a mark on this planet. As a leader, you want to be a seasoned mariner, with your wisdom and unwavering resolve, to guide the teams through calm seas and stormy waters and a keen eye on the horizon. Does this really happen? How do you see the mirror to know when you don’t get it right with others? It demands authenticity and a natural self. When you are artificially nice to people in socially awkward moments, you may not know but under your hidden discomfort all of a sudden your heart pounds and occasionally raises your blood pressure.

So, why this mention and where are the awkward moments?

You may find this situation in new relationships, in unchartered territories with people and most usually when giving or receiving feedback, especially speaking in the corporate context with your manager or with your team member, at both ends of the equation.

Firstly, unknowingly, ‘sugar’ plays a key role in these. We most always find comfort in being nice, in compliments and patronizing in conversations with unnecessary positivity. Secondly, most usually as a recipient, we start fidgeting, avoiding eye contact with the presenter, we cross our arms, we may have an awkward eye movement, we occasionally pull the chair around and palms possibly get sweaty as the sucralose rubs on as a sweetener. With these under the skin responses and when you are artificially nice or someone else is artificially nice to you, you get awkward moments. Most importantly, we experience cardiovascular stress, unknown to our own physiology.

So, How should we actually deal when giving or receiving feedback?

- Firstly, DO NOT patronize with unnecessarily nice feedback as you are somewhere
- 
- 
- 
- ruining your reputation Ensure that you always have data and specificity to back your qualifying statements If I suggest that you do not use sweeteners, it also means that you need not be rude in your interactions When providing feedback to others start with small specific constructive data points in a couple of dimensions Stop giving generic feedback like you need to improve your communications or you need to be more visible. Stop accepting these as a recipient as well
- Provide specific actions/recommendations that are time bound, event bound, topic bound and support bound. An example could be - you will present a product demo at the next quarterly all hands meeting in August and I will help you prepare your pitch.
- When providing feedback ensure the recipient’s receiving antennas are on (for her to be present in the moment), avoid attacking weaknesses, identify how your feedback will help the individual, be honest, be objective and as specific as possible. Ensure that the ambience for the conversation is comfortable , perhaps even informal location like a coffee shop
- When you give a presentation and if you receive lukewarm ‘great job’ feedback with claps in emojis then don’t feel elated. Ask for real feedback. As an audience be authentic in providing feedback to the presenter As you take this back with you, think about both pleasant and unpleasant experiences, how you reacted as a giver or a receiver of the feedback. You can raise the bar for the next interaction with these thoughts.

Find a mentor in someone who can provide honest and objective perspectives. Where possible be one.

title: Back-Pocket Objects and Strategies
content:
As a rarity, many years ago, I was attending a friend’s baby shower. His father was entertaining us and to ensure an engaged audience, he announced a gift for anyone who rushed to him first with a photo print of her/his spouse. Another one for anyone who had a 1 Rupee currency note. I failed in both the attempts.

It made me think of another point in time, illusionary this time, if an executive asked for anyone to show a 3 year career roadmap, who would instantly show up. I always feel that I will pass this test. I take my career in perspective with large chunks, with at least 3 years on the horizon. So, what? Why does it matter?

Here is a short list of introspections for this thought:

- It allows me to look forward and keeps me away from gratification for what I have accomplished
- We get stuck with these expectations and largely relate our recent accomplishments to expected recognitions, such as remuneration, promotion or gratification
- A lot of us are left disappointed as the organization’s actuality of compensation planning is in line with inflationary adjustments and an additional payout relating to your performance. Most always (y)our expectations far exceed the actual recognitions How to fix and fast forward?
- Firstly, let’s understand that this is your career. Reduce, if not completely eliminate, your dependence on your leader
- To get to 2X compensation (100% increase) you need 10x thinking
- Thinking by itself does not help without a strong and impactful narrative
- You need to demonstrate continuous (past and present) ability to execute on your plans
- The plan has to strongly tie to the objectives of the larger organization spanning beyond (un)known objectives of your leader
- You need real time interventions to create this long term view. Ensure to fine tune this and provide details for the associated narratives over time Takeaways: How do you execute this?

I found these beautiful tweets that can help to strengthen our wiring:

1. 15 Things required to be mentally strong.

https://x.com/succeededmind/status/1807993691295883484?s=42&t=JIJCIIGaG6vQasP iHPp6BA 2. 60-70% most impactful work that helps acceleration are done in personal space/pockets. https://x.com/zebulgar/status/1810036671053967482 3. Over time, those who have broad understanding and impacts for life will thrive. https://x.com/gaganbiyani/status/1807778182365352147?s=46&t=ZnpTJCdDQBgrMGn p0viVgQ

title: The Assassination Attempt and Gun Access
content:
Shocking news to wake up with. A failed assassination attempt on Donald Trump, ex-president and a presidential nominee for the 2024 US elections, by a 20 year old. It is beyond me to see access/usage of guns in the powerhouse of the world and the security lapse that would have led to this situation.

I definitely intended to write about the state of American companies given the recovery post-Covid, following supply chain crisis, unending wars, the AI bubble, increasing interest rates, elections in India, Iran, Indonesia, Taiwan, UK and the upcoming presidential elections in the United States.

### Here are some capsules worth observing

Mag 7 and Top Companies on the planet Magnificent 7 include Amazon, Apple, Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Tesla leading to a total market capitalization of $18T+ comparable to the GDP of China, world’s second largest economy. The thought I had shared about valuation of companies (6 Aug 2023) far exceeding the GDP of many nations is getting compellingly stronger with time. As part of the top 10 companies on the planet Saudi Aramco (Oil & Gas), Eli Lilly (Pharma) and Berkshire Hathaway stand out as non-technology companies.

Tech has dominated the world in the past decade in both accelerating the growth of traditional companies and more importantly in displacing/adding businesses with technology as a key driver. This leads to another thought in terms of how a start-up starts with an aim to innovate and disrupt traditionality, how it raises funds, how it builds as a private company and at what point does it become public, or not.

Public or Private Mike Scarpelli, Snowflake CFO, one of my favorite leaders, has stated a few times that going public (IPO -Initial Public Offering) is much easier than fulfilling the norms of running a public company business, given the statutory and regulatory expectations. AI and the perceived/implied impact it will create on privacy, copyrights and employment shifts is adding a whole new dimension in the forward looking challenges and the opportunities that the world will face.

Jamie Dimon, chairman and CEO of JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM), states that the number of U.S. public companies is plummeting—and that’s bad news for the democratic component of the economy.

He states regarding the significant decline in the number of public companies that from their peak in 1996 at 7,300, U.S. public companies is now a total of 4,300 — the total should have grown dramatically, not shrunk.
