Meanwhile, the number of private U.S. companies backed by private equity firms — which does not include the rising number of companies owned by sovereign wealth funds and family offices — has grown from 1,900 to 11,200 over the last two decades.

### Here is why in a nutshell

- Being private lets you raise capital with far more flexibility
- Staying public necessitates intensified reporting requirements (including investors’ growing needs for environmental, social and governance information), higher litigation expenses, costly regulations, cookie-cutter board governance, shareholder activism, less compensation flexibility, less capital flexibility, heightened public scrutiny and the relentless pressure of quarterly earnings So, What?

Many of us as employees of publicly traded companies ask questions around revenue, valuations, growth and the share price, especially. It is important to understand the premise on which the companies are built, how they innovate, how they grow, what are the impediments in the growth and what role do I play to enable that 10x growth personally for me and for the larger organization. How do you (under)stand?

title: Happiness and the Illusion of Progress
content:
Happiness: Our deep rooted mind is happy if we are making progress with life. Salary raise, recognition, peer comparison, new house, new car or new assets are all implied elements of material progress and absence of these events is perceived as lack of progress, which make us unhappy.

Inflation: Inflation is the rate at which the general level of prices for goods and services rises, reducing purchasing power.

Annual Inflation: 2% annual inflation growth target was first adopted by New Zealand in 1990, as proposed by Reserve Bank Governor Don Brash. This has become relatively a global baseline in the last 40 years which needs revisiting as inflation largely has surpassed this assumption in the recent past.

Interest rates established by Federal Reserve Bank in the United States usually tend to be higher than inflation rate. As a result, the interest rates have been higher post the covid era following the inflationary economy.

Liquidity of Capital: In order for businesses to prosper, liquidity of money, which relates to access to capital, must be high. This largely relates to the growth orientation of the companies. So, Why all this?

Low inflation leads to lowe interest rates. Low interests will lead to easier access to capital and better opportuntiies for busiensses and economies to prosper. This will result in growth mindset especially for the technology sectors and tie to prosperity leading to increased happiness, which what human mind chases, largely speaking.

S&P 500 Index: The S&P 500 Index is a stock market index tracking the performance of 500 large publicly traded companies in the U.S. It is widely regarded as a key indicator of overall market health and economic performance.

Markets: Looking back in the last 12 months, S&P 500 Index experienced significant growth, reflecting strong market performance. From July 2023 to July 2024, the index rose from 4,450.38 to approximately 5,460.48, marking an increase of about 22.7%.

So why did the index grow despite the high interest rates?

The "Magnificent 7" stocks, comprising Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta Platforms, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Tesla, have played a substantial role in the S&P 500's performance over the past year. These seven companies, collectively known as the Mag 7, account for nearly a third of the S&P 500's market capitalization. Their combined influence has been significant, driving much of the index's growth.

In 2023, the Mag 7 stocks surged by approximately 75%, while the remaining 493 stocks in the S&P 500 rose by only 12%. This stark contrast highlights the outsized impact these tech giants have had on the overall market. Without the contribution of the Mag 7, the S&P 500's performance would have been markedly lower.

For example, Nvidia led the charge with a remarkable 239% increase in 2023, followed by Meta Platforms with a 191% gain.

Monopolies: This disproportionate growth also shows how monopolies will come into play. NVIDIA makes H100 GPUs worth $30k a piece. A lot of AI/ML infrastructure will have hard reference to their architecture.

We also experienced global outages in banks, airlines, finance, retail and other industries owing to issues related to Microsoft/Crowdstrike dominance.

In a Nutshell disproportionality of nations are now also reflected in companies. For now, just be aware, observe and appreciate these phenomenon

title: Incrementalism: Last Thoughts as a Snowflake
content:
Last set of thoughts with this phase of life as a ‘Snowflake’. Sundays are not going to stop and so are the thoughts not going to stop. This weekend has been a deeply introspective 48 hour window.

INCREMENTALISM: Writing this one with a perspective of an investment analogy. India runs a campaign called ‘Mutual Funds Sahi Hai’ and there is an attached strategy of a Systematic Investment Plan. Largely, unless you are an F&O (Futures and Options) player, you will invest with a long term in perspective. Essential difference between a Fixed Deposit and Long Term investments is a combination of fluid model, long term tenure and the (dis)proportionality of results, of course there is always an element of risk. Let’s take this game to our personal lives. Systematic Investment Plans involve regular periodic investment of a certain capacity to invest in the form of money or efforts. Similar to financial investments, effort investments need to be fixed and continuous. This is the magic of incrementalism.

You need a very predictable investment in learning a skill, bringing awareness of worldly realities, applying the skill/knowledge and driving for measurable results/outcomes. DANGERS OF INCREMENTALISM: When you chase for a certain increment (pun intended) or a promotion, you tend to put outcomes ahead of efforts and this leads to gratifying short term gains and long term disappointments, unless of course that brings happiness.

The key difference here is to state that investments have to be incremental. In order to have non-linear returns, outcomes or expectations need not be either short term or just incremental. Disruptions or Transformations happen only with a long lasting perseverance, whether it is to do with building an LLM or discovering a cure for cancer.

ACUMEN and TIMING : Acumen is a function of knowing where to invest, how much to invest and appreciating Return on Investments at the right time. You need returns to buy a house, for educational loans or to make bigger investments for the future.

Acumen to know where to invest money or efforts has to be very selective and to not follow someone’s lead blindly. This needs a solid awareness of goals first, navigational path to source knowledge, execution framework and of course a support structure to lean for guidance. Another thought that came my way, especially over the weekend as I pursue my next phase in the journey, is this one critical question. Whose Inbox do I exist in, e-mail or mental? Once you have made your investments, gained incremental knowledge over time, and generated returns/value, now is the time to look for compounding effects. With finance you need a new investment model and with professional drive, you need to know who you know or rather who knows you? Do you navigate to be present in their mental or electronic inbox (e-mail)? Compounding does not happen without this, your knowledge will stagnate with you without being able to navigate and communicate.

There are many ways to arrive in someone’s mental inbox which I will write about at a future point.

CONCLUDING: Are you looking for that F&O like iPhone perk from a new opportunity or building a long term corpus? Remember, in ROI there are no returns before the investments. So do you have an SIP? Whose Inbox are you in?

If you would like to continue receiving these thoughts just drop a note on my LinkedIn inbox with a preferred email address to receive these.

title: The Lesser-Known Musk Ventures
content:
By now we are aware of Tesla, SpaceX, The Boring Company, X (formerly Twitter) and StarLink as some of the Elon Musk businesses. He continues to work on what the future holds. Not so talked about and relatively new initiatives from Musk include Neuralink - built to alleviate human suffering while expanding capabilities of human mind, Optimus - the Humanoid Robot, desire to build a human colony on planet Mars and finally the buildout of ‘Gigafactory of Compute’, the world’s largest supercomputer.

Buildout of ‘Gigafactory of Compute’ is 750,000 sq ft in Memphis, state of Tennessee and is on an accelerated timeline to complete in 2025. This supercomputer involves 100,000 GPUs which are all connected to each such that a GPU can talk to any of the other GPUs in the cluster. This likely represents a human brain that humans can actually see, analogically speaking. Similar to the human brain, gray matter is represented by compute blocks and cables connecting the GPUs represent the tissues of the white matter. Human brain has 60% white matter which is equivalent to cables and these cables similar to the tissues are responsible for carrying signals inside the brain.

So, why compare the Supercomputer buildout with the brain?

To achieve AGI/ASI (Artificial General/Superhuman Intelligence), we will need access to extraordinary computing capacity.

Additionally, the AGI buildout supercomputer will also support Neuralink. Neuralink will improve AI-Human Symbiosis and solve neurological issues that humans have.

1. It can bypass damaged neural pathways and facilitate direct communication between the brain and external devices 2. It can cure blindness by directly triggering the neurons in the visual cortex 3. Eventually you can augment human brains to increase human output rate by increasing the number of electrodes/channels using the neuralink 4. Make human eye see different wavelengths beyond the ordinary to create infrared eagle vision and more Beyond Neuralink, AGI/ASI scale can be initiated on this Gigacomputer. On the podcast mentioned below, they joked saying, machine interacting with humans will be like humans asking the plant: I want to make it happy but it is not saying a lot.

How are GigaComputer and other major initiatives implemented?

Here are the basic Principles/Algorithms as narrated by Musk for an intense drive to improve the product process of the Supercomputer build out and others.

1. Firstly, make requirements less dumb. No matter how smart the person who gives requirements is, there is some dumbness to it. You don’t want a perfect answer to a wrong question. So, always question the requirements 2. Now, delete some part of the requirements. If you don’t delete at least 10% of what your process is then you are introducing inefficiencies. People feel successful at the start if they are not asked to remove things that they have put in their request 3. Once this is done, further simplify and simplify the process in the design. Most common mistake that smart engineers make is to optimize the process that should not exist in the first place. You may in your design and requirements eventually put things back that you may have deleted in the first place. Deleting the requirements or a process step introduces pain for the user or the designer respectively. You need to tell them that some of the things that we delete we may put them back in however without this first principles thinking your product will not be simplified 4. Any given thing can be sped up. There is always a faster way to get things done. You don’t get to speed up (improvise schedule) up until you have simplified and optimized, both the requirements and the design 5. Finally, automate. Now it is important to realize if you don’t follow this approach you may automate something, think of speeding up and then eventually realize that in order to optimize you may delete something that was not needed in the first place.

Critical Success Factors AI Computing infrastructure is a voracious user of water and power, and the environmental downsides of hosting these mega-machines is a source of growing concern. xAI facility in Memphis will need to use roughly 1 million gallons of water a day and up to 150 megawatts of electricity Globally, computer processing is adding a worrying burden on energy resources, and demand is anticipated to keep surging. In Ireland, for example, data centers consumed 21% of the nation’s electricity in 2023. with a huge amount of greenhouse gas emissions.

While there are planet changing initiatives that will be implemented on the modern AI technology stack, the modern stacks of Crypto, AI and Data Centers play a tug of war with greenhouse gas emissions.

Your job in this space is to keep an eye on these contradicting elements, remain engaged with the stack build out especially with the inference stack and be excited about the future as AI evolves and leads us to AGI/ASI (Artificial General/Superhuman Intelligence).

title: Questioning the Definitions
content:
Today, I am writing about how I questioned some definitions to understand a few recent concepts. Here we go.

Imagine you are doing a ‘Subject Matter’ talk or a presentation. In preparation you have a structure based on a flow or your knowledge based on which you have prepared your talk. What makes a Tech Deterministic or Non Deterministic?

Deterministic Tech produces the same output for a given input every time. Traditional algorithms and rule based software follow specific instructions and provide predictable results. LLMs are trained on a variety of data sets and the inference or responses of the model rely on probabilistic algorithms to generate responses. Given that token generation is sequential (100th word of the inference is not predicted till the 99th word is generated), LLMs generate varied and creative responses for each ‘ask’ just like a human brain would.

As a presenter: In the mock of being a presenter you have studied your subject for years and this makes your talk super predictable in the context of your preparation and material to be shared.

Why do the models hallucinate in that case?

Models respond to training data and not factual accuracy so it makes them predictive, leading to incorrect or nonsensical responses. They cannot make real-time verification and hence just like a human would (we all sometimes say nonsensical things) model would do the same. Now when you are reciting tables of mathematical numbers you are always referring to some verification or validation so you will not tend to hallucinate due to finite factual references, unless of course you are making a genuine mistake.

As a presenter: Now imagine that in this session someone asks you a question and you are not sure of the answer. You may answer it subjectively with some abstraction or defer the question saying that you will come back at a later point.

Fundamental nature of LLMs is poised to be an accurate specialist and as a result unlike human responses, language models may hallucinate and give an incorrect response. Imagine how many people will come back to your ‘specialist’ talk if you hallucinated and said ‘sorry’ let me try this again.

Similar to answering mathematical questions, how can models be made more accurate or more deterministic?

Models can be improved with techniques like Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) where the models retrieve relevant, up-to-date information from external sources before generating a response. This ensures fact-based responses from being trained on a specialized dataset. This will mitigate hallucination and provide far more trustworthy outputs.

As a presenter: In this situation, many times leaders take along specialists and use them similar to RAGs to provide qualified responses after providing initial directional thoughts. So What? In your pursuit of life, you need solid subject matters to be ‘out there’ that come from training and many instances of responsive inferences. You will often get stuck and you will need to better prepare to infer (imagine at meetings), to respond (show up), to reduce hallucinations (keep mum when you are not sure), to train yourself (find where you fell short) and finally use support (of experts and your colleagues) to make an impact. Do you?

title: Understanding the Recent Economic Turmoil
content:
Carry Trade: A carry trade involves borrowing money in a currency with a low-interest rate and investing it in a currency or asset with a higher interest rate, profiting from the difference (the "carry"). This was evident in the recent findings as many investors borrowed from almost 0% interest Japanese Yen and invested in high yield assets, especially ones attached with the US dollars.

Following chart shows how the median population has trended in 3 of the geographies, namely India, Japan and the United States that I considered for this write-up. As you can say, the current median age of the Japanese population is significantly higher than that of the United States and much higher than that of India.

Let’s also look at the ratio of national debt to GDP ratio for these countries In a nutshell, you see that the debt for the United States and Japan, as compared to the GDP, has significantly grown and remains unmanaged.

So, what? This essentially shows that between the aging population, cost of servicing the debt and inflationary impacts both Japan and the US have an uphill battle to run their economies. If not corrected, countries like India will have a similar outcome in 2 decades from now. What triggered the global economy in the last 2 weeks?

Two weeks ago, the Bank of Japan decided to raise its interest rates to 0.15-0.25% from 0%, for the first time in years to curb rising inflation and stabilize the yen.

Going back to carry trade, the borrowed capital in form of Japanese yen, now becomes expensive. As the governments would service the debt, your need to serve the loan payment for borrowing in Japanese Yen now has an interest payment and an additional economic uncertainty from any future rate increases.

If this was borrowed at 0% and invested in the US bonds at 4-5% you are making roughly an annual gain of $40,000-$50,0000 against a borrowing of 1 million dollar equivalent. The larger organizations who use this leverage are interested in a lot more than 4-5% gain. Their game is much bigger. How?

- Now imagine that borrowings are much larger in billions
- Borrowings are against collaterals like assets and equities
- Borrowed money is then further leveraged to invest the borrowings in F&O (Futures & Options). Simply stating, the borrowed capital is invested in proportionately much larger investments than the loans
- Now, all of a sudden when the Bank of Japan announced this raise in the interest rate, there is a rush to cover for making the loan payments both in actual and speculative modes
- This rush creates a pressure on all the asset classes including real estate and equities. As you can imagine, equities are much easier to exit from than other fixed asset classes
- F&O algorithms resulted in a very aggressive sell-off of equities worth billions $.
- Hence you see a mode where there are significantly more sellers than buyers (beautifully explained by Nilesh Shah at Kotak Bank -https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VY8sXX-QKME)
- You saw a major sell-off in the global markets as a result Seeing this major sell off and uncertainty, the Bank of Japan decides to hold on to increasing the raise in the interest rates. What were they thinking?

Markets have stabilized since then although the uncertainty of macroeconomic situations still holds and on a separate note, there is a looming danger of the United States entering in the recessionary mode.

What is in it for me/you?

Recession in the US will impact the world and the attached ripples will be experienced by all of us. This will create both opportunities and repositioning needs for your personal portfolios. Will the reversal of decision by the Bank of Japan lead back to the ‘same old same old’ borrowing. Keep your eyes and ears open. This will impact all of our lives where we live in this globally connected/impacting world.

title: What Does Your Week Look Like?
content:
Most of us ‘working professionals’ are trained to start the work week on Mondays, check on things to do for the day or the week and just jump start. For those who are super apprehensive about the food chain sentiment, our work emotions are driven by the boss’ mood swings, her agenda or sometimes we rejoice from them not being around for the week. We have a finite set of choices to begin the week or the day. Comfort leads to this Auto-pilot. A week may also have intersperses of workout, breaks, and family routines. Week ends somewhere between Thursday, Friday or Saturday and then there is some unwinding before the cycle resets for yet another Monday.

So, Why this?

Some people love the predictability of life. For me especially, ‘change’ is far more comforting than routine. Our routine life also needs discipline, however this will keep you cruising and just let you be. I want to address the requirement of intensity to discover the next version of us. The growth mindset.

Importance of ‘Change’ Environment around us goes through constant changes, some of them are significant life changing events. Think of how life has changed and challenged us through the covid pandemic, the following supply chain crisis, government intervention through stimulus, economic growth, major policy changes to manage inflationary and disinflationary impacts, the wars, the energy and the weather crisis and of course, the evolution of AI/LLMs. These events have forced changes in our work environments, lifestyle choices from being stuck at home and riding the associated ‘monetary’ tidal waves.

One part of me wants to learn and appreciate the changes and the other part is to change myself. You can be a change catalyst in two ways. Firstly, appreciate above events and make changes to your work and life models: we learnt how to work remotely to where we have reached hybrid work styles, we leverage the AI and new-age tech tools to accelerate our work journeys, we made investments to leverage major geopolitical or macroeconomic events and lastly but most importantly to recognize the importance of human life and health from the perils of the wars and the pandemic.

Secondly, in addition to leveraging these changes in our existing life, life itself can change through these. Disruption in the hospitality business with AirBnB, Full Service Autonomous vehicles, Media consumption through OTT streaming compared to traditional cable connections and dreaming to colonize Mars would not be possible without this mindset to change the world. Two thoughts in conclusion

- Effort is everything: World around us moves very fast and if you want to sit there thinking that you are better than man of the worst performers then you will keep cruising
- Wandering Mindset: I came across this Ted talk that shows how to ‘hack your brain for better focus’, you need not just workout or meditate but also let your mind wander from a thought to a thought.

It will bring a much needed breadth.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PBaFURjVrm0 So, are you ready for a change as the new Monday begins tomorrow?

title: Matters of Mind: Taking a Break
content:
Many of my wellwishers suggested that I take a break this time around. I have been working for over three decades and have never taken a break in between my jobs. So, for the first time, I am on a break. At any given point either, I am rejuvenating or anxious. Rejuvenating to recharge or anxious to think about what’s next? Should I be recharging or just stay busy looking for my next career opportunity? Honestly, I think this one needs a placebo.

Change your mindset, change your game I came across this ted talk by Dr Alia Crum where she shares how our thinking determines our body’s response. She shares four beautiful illustrations showing positive psychological and physiological responses from placebo’s effect on patients, love for their job by the housekeeping staff from exercise benefits of their work, ghrelin level drop from description of how the milkshake was made as low calorie sugar free recipe vs. high fat high calorie recipe, and lastly how dealing with stress leads to peak performance.

In case you are not aware, the placebo effect occurs when a person experiences a perceived improvement in their condition after receiving a treatment with no therapeutic value, driven by their belief in the treatment's efficacy.

Ghrelin is a hormone that stimulates appetite, increases food intake, and promotes fat storage, often called the "hunger hormone" because it signals the brain to induce feelings of hunger. So, Why this?

One of my readers asked me to write about the ‘perceived’ value of the work we do. Similar to Dr Crum, let’s think about four possible situations:

1. First, let’s imagine that there is no recognition or appreciation of what you do. Zero. Nada.

2. Manager and Management is deeply appreciative however you are unsure. So you think that you are not appreciated as there is no acknowledgement.

3. You get recognition and appreciation from the leadership, all the time. This keeps you elated and makes you really happy about the work you do, just like the housekeeping staff knowing that they exercise regularly by the virtue of doing their job. 4. In the last instance, even if you receive real or ‘sugar coated’ acknowledgement of the work you do, you are self-aware and self-critical of the work that you do. You believe and have strong awareness of your ability to move the needle.

Which situation would you rather be in, of the four of the above. Some of you may want situations 3 & 4 both and most evolved individuals would look for only #4.

So let’s conclude with the ‘Matters of Mind’

- While there is no suggestion to not exercise or to not follow the diet, our psychological and physiological states are definitely influenced by the mind.
- Should I be having fun in my sabbatical or be anxious for my work? Neither - just be cognitively aware of both the pockets
- Should I always be stressed as it gives me peak performance? Not quite, just be aware that stress is not always a bad thing
- Lastly, imagine you are (like me) looking for a new opportunity, would you rather be in #4 of the 4 situations or tell about your value to the outside world only based on the validation from your food chain.

title: Spend Spectrum and Purchasing Power Parity
content:
Spend Spectrum, Cost of Living Adjustments and Purchasing Price Parities A friend of mine from Mumbai met me today in Lonavala, which is a relative mid-point between my house in Pune and Mumbai. Since we were meeting with families after a very long time gap, we decided to go to a fancy wine & dine place. I had called for a driver to drive us so that we could enjoy our drink and drive home peacefully. Upon return, I asked my driver how much I owed him for his lunch as I wanted to pay him allowance for his meal and more. He said that was about 100 Rupees ($1.20).

I was humbled by two things: His honesty as a first thing. Secondly, how cost effective his meal was compared to the embarrassment it would cause for me to write how much we spent on our lunch.

In another situation, I wanted to buy a recliner for my TV room a few years ago. I went to a fancy European furniture store and the saleswoman tried to sell me this expensive recliner chair which was a little more expensive than a modest sedan car one could purchase. She told me even Kartik Aryan, a celebratory Indian film star, has this recliner. I had no idea of who Kartik Aryan was and irrespective of that I decided to purchase a cost effective option. The Danish option of the recliner was to be made to order and handmade in Denmark, which obviously would involve both meticulous quality and cost of European labor.

As you move from the Northern Europe to the Southern Europe to some Asian countries and eventually to the South Asian subcontinent you will experience the purchasing price parities, spend spectrums and cost of living adjustment with unit costs coming down as you pan through the chronology of these geographies, as described.

So, What? How does this similarly impact the rest of the world?

As I write this, Volkswagen company (VW), a parent auto-manufacturing company of brands including VW, Skoda, Audi and Porsche, is considering shutting its manufacturing plants in Germany. Between the pandemic, the supply chain crisis, and the Russia-Ukraine war the European and German economy especially have been hit hard. (Dis)parities exist between Europe and Asia as well as between Northern and Southern EU nations.

Between the rising costs of labor, factory ecosystem in Europe and threats from China based EV companies, VW’s business is threatened. VW can’t sell as much in China anymore, EVs made in China are imported to Europe and most importantly even the VW’s China made EVs are levied 38% import duty in Europe. All this has made the business harder for VW. So what does VW do? They decide to shut their German plants down and plan to reduce expenses by $11B in the next 2 years. This will have implications - firstly, it will directly impact the employment and GDP in Germany. VW is just a symptomatic representation of what is happening with other companies and EU countries as well. Possibly the negative growth movements will lead to a recessionary state of additional Western countries including the United States.

Governments and Federal Banks are under extreme pressures to reduce interest rates to keep their economies afloat.

Global Capability Centers (GCCs) in India These situations of shifting workloads out of Europe creates opportunities for the world to draw some balance. GCCs, which many of you are part of, represent 1%+ of India’s GDP and this contribution from the GCCs continues to grow. There will be opportunities to build more technology, design, and manufacturing units in cost effective geographies. This will need the governments to offer increasing ease for doing businesses, gain political stability, industries to create talent, assure quality and get ready?

Your takeaway from this is to firstly acknowledge the global recessionary sentiments, impacts and opportunities this will create and plunge in when the opportunity knocks your door. Of course you will need to dress up in overalls, roll up your sleeves and be ready. Are you?

title: How Inflation Is Actually Measured
content:
Have you ever thought of how inflation is measured? Of course, in a way it is a measure of affordability of goods and services. I was curious so I asked who measures, what it measures and how?

Inflation is measured using Consumer Price Index (CPI) and the Producer Price Index (PPI) as a measure to know the rise or fall in the price of goods and services over time. This measure will play a key role in soon to be announced interest rate adjustments in the United States by the Federal Reserve Bank.

It is a measure of affordability for housing, food, transportation, healthcare and education. Federal Agents (Humans) measure the costs of these items in 75 urban areas for what the consumers actually pay after adjusting for discounts and promotions. The percentage change from the base price is determined as increase/decrease in the current price determining if the movement is inflationary (costs increase) or deflationary (costs decrease). Similarly, PPI is tracked by interacting with producers for what are the trends in the selling price of housing, transportation, food, healthcare and education.

Two questions then come to mind. In the age of AI and LLMs why do we need humans to measure this and what other indexes do exist and how they are measured. Here are the few notable ones.

Volatility Index: This is a forward-looking index that reflects market participants' expectations of volatility over the next 30 days. The more investors expect significant price swings in the near future, the higher the volatility index will be. In simple terms, it’s a barometer of the market's anxiety level. It measures the ‘fear factor’ from the real financial data and is far more automated than the CPI/Inflation measure with human involvement.

Global Temperature and Climate Indices track the planet’s health. They are far more accurate and tracked over a long period, even in the century years.

Arctic Sea Ice Extent Index tracks the changes in the size of the Arctic ice cap, which is a crucial indicator of global warming.

The Air Quality Index (AQI) which is observed on a daily basis even by common (wo)man is critical for understanding the health impacts of air pollution in cities worldwide. The AQI is calculated based on measurements of pollutants such as particulate matter (PM2.5, PM10), ozone (O3), nitrogen dioxide (NO2), sulfur dioxide (SO2), and carbon monoxide (CO). Of course, we are all aware of indexes like NASDAQ, DJIA (in the US) and SENSEX (BSE in India) as a measure of stock market’s performance.

While most of these indices are **highly accurate**, they can still face challenges related to data collection in less-developed or remote areas, timeliness, and the complexity of the phenomena they measure (e.g., biodiversity or water stress).

So, What is in it for me? Now imagine a ‘Personal Index’ that would measure you by you. What if you were to measure yourself? What would the performance basis be - would it be others around you or just you in comparison how much better you are this year compared to what you were last year?

I imagine life similar to a game of golf - how better is your current game to the past game as I just compete with myself to be a better me. I would rather be a racehorse with blinders on the sides to not be distracted by the world around me.

Do you know of other critical indexes that I may have missed? Do you think of other imaginary indexes that can exist? Are you a racehorse or a fiercely competitive individual that always looks around?

title: A Note After the Outage
content:
Firstly, my sincere apology for the miss last weekend. I came from Mumbai late Sunday evening last week and for the 24 hours following 7 days up until right now, I went through a long dengue outage. Never been through a phase like this which I may write about in the near future. Here are the thoughts for today in the meantime.

During the India-Bangladesh first cricket test match, one of the commentators mentioned how the Indian cricket team must have been on a high after the world cup win to come and play this series now. I am like did we not lose the world cup, what is he talking about? I had the pleasure of seeing a few matches of the ODI world cup and a strong recollection of our loss to Australia in the finals.

Let me ask another question here. Do you know who Liz Truss is? Most of you may not - Liz Truss served as the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom for just 49 days, making her the shortest-serving PM in British history. Liz Truss's brief tenure was characterized by a failed economic experiment, market instability, and a rapid loss of political authority and support. The speed at which these events unfolded left her unable to recover or effectively govern, leading to her short-lived term as Prime Minister. This happened in the month of October ‘22. So, what? Why do these events matter?

Very early in my career I was made aware of one thing. You are as good as your worst number or your weakest link. For me India’s loss of ODI world cup finals resonated much easier than the relatively recent T20 world cup victory. Nobody remembered all the great work Liz Truss would have done to become the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom. She could barely make it to 49 days in her job before her nation became unforgiving. They did not let her survive on the account of the grave misses.

Earlier in June I wrote about this Salesforce.com mishap. For having reported revenue lower by an absolute number just equating to less than 0.5% of the forecast revenue, Salesforce.com dropped its valuation by a whopping $40B (20%) in just one day.

Assuming the loss in a sporting event is not an error, misses in revenue, project’s deadline, political blunders or product security lapses can be of grave danger to the brands of both, individuals and organizations. A response process needs to be imbibed in the culture of the organization or the fabric of an individual.

Of course now, ‘"To err is human; to forgive, divine." Professional world certainly does not experience as much divinity for forgiveness. So let’s intervene and ask some questions. Do I/We Err and if so What do I/We do?

In the information age, news and words travel at rocket speed. So when you err, here is what

### one must do

When errors happen, good leaders treat errors as opportunities for learning and organizational improvement, sharing lessons with their teams to foster a culture of growth. They

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- Communicate transparently and promptly Assess the impact and prioritize mitigation Implement a robust fix and learn from the incident Hold people accountable while fostering a no-blame culture.

Rebuild trust through consistent actions Show empathy and offer support

### Asking yourself

Do you recall a time when you made a blunder? Did you find someone else to point a finger at or could take charge of what transpired? Do you recall such a situation that happened around you and how a given leader played her part?

Is your organization or you as an individual prepared for unforeseen times? Do you have a plan or even a mindset to respond with calm?

title: Yet Another Wave of AI Displacement
content:
Back to AI thoughts after a certain gap. Uber and other equivalent platforms replaced traditional taxi companies, AirBnB displaced the hospitality businesses, and cloud technologies displaced traditional hardware (Compute, Network & Storage) businesses. We are facing yet another displacement of technology platforms with the advent of AI, especially with the Language Models.

Many hundreds of millions have already accessed LLMs around the planet and close to a billion users have directly or indirectly benefited from them.

It started with GPTs used for inferencing, for writing emails, leading to a series of co-pilots, langchain agents to today where the world is moving towards AI agents working under certain orchestration. What does this mean? Where is it going? How will it evolve? Let’s examine some predictions while asking more questions.

Generative AI Investments and Expectations Companies continue investing in their Generative AI journeys. Although a great opportunity lies ahead, the budgets for GenAI are not specialized or specific today. From everything that I have seen, GenAI budgets are created by re-allocation of dollars from Data Analytics, Business Applications, spends on Productivity Applications, reduction of Outsourced Services, and shift of Robotic Process Automation and Marketing budgets to GenAI. GenAI is still seen as generic in the broader technology approach and as a result it lacks specificity in defining roadmaps or objectives. Similarly organizations are still unsure about the ROI, both in terms of timing and magnitude of the returns. In a nutshell, the hype of GenAI has gotten a little ahead of reality today while I do not dismiss the terrific potential that lies ahead.

Evolution of On-Prem and SaaS Applications These began with two distinct divisions. One part covered data models that were either custom designed for a specific solution or a packaged data model that came with the application, on-prem or SaaS based. Second part covered the application logic which was built based on certain expected business process behaviors. Millions of application developers today are employed to manage these comprehensive business systems. The application logic got modularized for performance and efficiencies even to the extent of creating microservices based solution architecture.

Modern Data Stacks from companies like Snowflake, Databricks and hyperscaler’s Data Platforms provide access to the data sourced/ingested from a variety of business systems. Traditional data analytics and insights were drawn using visualization and analytics engines sitting on top of these platforms.

Language models are changing the traditional game here. Let’s examine how?

New-Age and largely ‘to be’ AI/LLM and Data Stack Continuing with the narrative of the Datastack, now imagine a data harmonization layer that sits above the data stack from providers like Snowflake.

1. At the bottom most layer of this stack, imagine your Data & AI fabric with LLMs built and integrated into the datasets. LLMs can be self sourced, open sourced models that are customized or closed sourced models accessed using the APIs. Heart and soul with cross company data exists in this datastack layer 2. Imagine the next layer of specialized Small Language Models which are derivatives of Large Language Models and provide a continuous feedback learning loop back into the LLMs. These models interact with each other, create knowledge graphs and report back causal (Cause and Effect) outcomes to the underlying LLMs 3. Lastly, imagine a multi-agent network sitting on top of these language models along with a master orchestrator Let me give an example here. Customer interacts with say Customer Support AI agent for a service request. This Customer Support AI agent works with the master orchestrator to interact with Order Management or Supply Chain AI agents to provide a specific service to the end customer. Organization’s data is managed with LLMs, SLMs provide specialized function specific learning based on specialized training, while continuously learning from interactions. What will change? When will it change? Will it?

- AI Agents will potentially replace SaaS/On-Prem application logic and microservices such that the logic moves to AI agents leveraging specialized Small Language Models
- Learning and improvements of SLMs/LLMs will happen through data and behaviors, just like a human being would
- Agents will have complication from the fact that SaaS companies, LLM providers and Data stack companies are going to introduce their version of agents
- Application logic will move from SaaS to the AI agents that are working with each other
- LLMs, SLMs, knowledge graphs and causal models will have to play together such that they replace pre-programmed deterministic SaaS behaviors with model behaviors
- Will this displace SaaS companies? Will SaaS companies be duplicative with both, the application logic and AI agents
- Will we move from high maintenance white box programmed SaaS logic to model based self-learning with a potential non-deterministic mindset
- Will there be deterrents to slow this growth? Many companies that I have worked with do not even enable co-pilots owing to security risks I personally feel, following my earlier prediction of SaaS, AI and Business processes to be in a single melting pot, that SaaS is due for some displacement. Although, when and how is for the time to tell. What do you think?

title: Moglis of the AI Jungle
content:
Following my writeup last week, I had the opportunity to spend a lot more time understanding the AI landscape. Some more findings with the hope that I will keep it simple for quite a few of my non-tech subscribers to appreciate the depth here.

Most, if not all, of you have used OpenAI/ChatGPT for something or the other. OpenAI released quite a few models of its own assuming that each increment captured more knowledge of the world and a greater ability to provide information to its consumers. Assuming that OpenAI would have published half a dozen of their models, HuggingFace which is a repository for models published surpassed a count of Million models in the month of September.

Similar to language models like OpenAI’s language models there are models to interpret/generate texts, images, voice and other aspects. Majority of these are open source and free to use however there is still a way for them to generate revenues through the use of compute resources by consumers and through other partnerships.

Alongside the jungle of models, I saw many coding co-pilots evolve.

What are Coding Co-pilots?

In the Airlines business, between being a pilot and the point where we get to complete auto-pilot operations, Co-Pilots are assistants to the pilots, which we have seen on the airplanes. Let’s assume that a programmer or a coder is a pilot in this case and the AI models/software work as co-pilots to increase the assurance of coder/programmer’s (pilot) capabilities. Existing companies like Github and Microsoft/OpenAI have their co-pilots namely Github co-pliot and Codex from Microsoft. Figma, a design company which helped imagine state-of-the-art User Interfaces, is also building capabilities to convert design templates into ready to use code in a programming language of your choice.

A bunch of startups, many with very young co-founders have started companies like Cursor, Codeium, QoDo (Codium earlier), Poolside, and Augment have built similar co-pilots. They are early stage private companies and have raised anywhere from $120M to $600M. This market has many players now and will reach a point of saturation between the speed at which they are evolving and the rate at which existing and new companies are also adding these kinds of capabilities.

So what do these companies do

- If you have used (or heard of) VS Code Editor then consider these are forks of VS Code
- Coding co-pilots are like really nice autocompletion coding capabilities on steroids
- They unify developer communities within an organization
- Many of these companies actually use their own co-pilots to further mature their co-pilot capabilities
- They look over your shoulder as a programmer and are a really great virtual colleague to have
- Co-pilot models can validate and help code multiple sections of multiple files at the same time
- They can provide AI aided code reviews and unlike manual reviews of code where we reviewed codes chronologically these editors review codes logically, keeping in mind the flow of the algorithms What are the challenges and opportunities?

A big challenge exists from the point that input prompts which usually are large programming files and the outputs are small sets of recommendations making the models respond with a very low latency (or high speed). This makes them very responsive however expensive as the size of input tokens into the models need a lot more computation. These companies that have built co-pilots need to build a lot of caching ability so that knowledge from previous input prompts can be retained and effectively managed.

Models usually respond with an edited version of the code recommending changes that you can remove as codelines from your code (marked in red usually) and the codelines which you can add (marked in green usually). To a naked eye of the programmer you can accept or reject these changes and upon acceptance the changes are automatically made in the code. I personally experienced the danger that with all the changes, not being a regular coder, I tended to accept the changes quickly and changed my code base. Not maintaining a code repository led to a lot of knowledge/code loss and I realized that while using these you need to be super attentive and engrossed in your codebase. It was quite frustrating for me. The opportunity lies in the fact that these tools can provide better code review abilities, help maintain version control and also perhaps at some stage show flowchart/graphical/algorithmic representation of the recommended changes.

In a Nutshell The space is moving rapidly, getting congested, a lot of investor money is flowing in and it will be hard to keep up. Naturally, like many other phenomena there will be consolidation opportunities through success, mergers and gentle fading away of the few companies. Are you aware of the space? Did I miss anything that you can share? Are you using the co-pilots? Are they making you efficient and smarter or may be efficient and dumber? I am enjoying keeping myself up-to-date.

Let me know if I should write about any other topic of your interest.

title: Who We Really Are (and a SpaceX Catch)
content:
Past week has been eventful in two ways. One was for me to think about who we really are and second was to appreciate SapceX returning a booster back at the launch pad from the Starship rocket on Sunday. The launch tower which was dubbed as chopsticks caught the descending 232-foot stainless steel booster which would be as tall as a 15-storeyed building. Why does this matter?

The reason it matters is because imagine the acceleration this will provide to significantly reduce the cost of shipping rockets which is a measure of cost per kilogram of rocketship.

- When it all started the returning rockets or components came back as debris so there was no reusability ever
- Earlier SpaceX launches in the last few years led to successful recovery however not at the base, that means there was a lot of cost of moving these heavy objects at the base for repurposing
- The cost of launching spaceships and satellites have come down from $10,000 to $1,000 per kilogram
- Missions to Mars can only be accomplished if the cost can be brought down significantly more. Imagine colonization will require sending so much more in the form of constructing a colony wherever in the outer space Importance of Focus Mars missions will not be accomplished without the ‘focus’ and for that you need to know both: what your equivalent Mars colonization mission is and how focused are you in accomplishing your mission. Let’s go back to the first of the two things I mentioned and think about who we really are? We often tend to sway our objectives for our greed as humans for certain designation or a certain remuneration. In the process we lose sight of the core objectives so some thoughts

### on that note

- Being artificial and adjusting to suit a culture, organization or a leader will often set you up for a failure
- When working for an organization between where you start and to when you eventually become successful is a long stretched messy middle. The messy middle can make you impatient and uncomfortable
- Being comfortable with this discomfort will be a big key for your success. It takes many years for the organizations/teams to appreciate what you bring to the table
- While dealing with the discomfort you will have to build long lasting relationships and identify sponsors/mentors that will help you navigate
- For your mission, how you utilize your time will help you get to Mars sooner. You will find cross-roads to decide whether you will do something or delegate a given task. If you are not a leader and cannot delegate then you will need colleagues to assist you. You will need to decide which tasks/events will you be a part of or skip the ones that are not part of your mission. Delegation and Assistance should only be facilitated with ones who have the chemistry and relevance to the given tasks/events. We have seen many figureheads who are just out there so be very careful to not become one or spend your cycles with figureheads who don’t add value
- Sometimes we get stuck with the finite nature of outcomes. For example one might address the goal to get a specific share in a specific market. True winners build opportunities and measures with Blue Ocean strategies. A Blue Ocean Strategy is a business approach that focuses on creating new, uncontested market spaces or goals ("blue oceans") rather than competing in overcrowded, existing markets ("red oceans"). It emphasizes innovation, value creation, and differentiation to make competition irrelevant

### So then let’s look at some thought provocations

Are you an outlier? Are your objectives or goals when accomplished will make you one? Do you know your goals and are they going to make you an outlier?

title: How to Be a Schmoozer
content:
Let’s define a ‘Schmoozer’ first: This is a person who is a sweet talker and can very easily persuade others to do what she wants. This skill is especially important to manage your food chain.

I was with some friends and like how we talk about teaching life skills at schools and colleges, a colleague of mine, of course with great intentions, suggested that one thing that people need to be taught is to be a good ‘schmoozer’. I immediately responded and reacted to say, this is not required. I do not support schmoozing or ‘kissing up’, crudely speaking.

Let the Truth prevail In the long run schmoozing can get you somewhere and may also give you all the wealth and success. You need to ask the question to know what matters to you. I often speak about authentic communication that involves honesty, openness and objectivity, applicable at all levels. Whether you are speaking with your team, your peers or your leadership, authenticity matters.

When you are dealing with a dilemma of communicating via ‘schmooze’ or ‘truth’, especially with people who hold greater influence, ensure you take care of a few things. Firstly know the path of communicating the facts, know the choices and the impact or outcomes from each of the choices. Also, it is important to find the right setup and place to communicate, ideal 1:1, so that you have minimized the points of complexity when being courageous when sharing thoughts and ideas.

- Ensure that you have thoroughly thought about all options and that you are thinking outside the box. Thinking inside the box to schmooze is easy
- Ensure that you have moved from comfort of conformity inside the box to outside the box
- You will risk your reputation outside the box however the long term rewards are far superior
- Discomforted mental state in these situations is very normal
- To be divergent you will need to cross the border of your mind. This needs long thinking which is different from fast, deep or brilliant thinking. What does this mean? It necessitates exploration of options and the attached value of each of the options Imagine you are innovating for your organization. You need many inputs from multiple entities inside and outside your organization, you reach out to people who have the strong ability to express and then create a platform for these disparate inputs to converge.

In a nutshell, you know if you are a schmoozer or an impact leader. If you believe that you are selective or balanced about being both then think hard. Of course, communication requires safety for both the sender and the recipient of the information. What are your experiences and thoughts? Happy to hear back.

title: More on Schmoozing and Thought Leadership
content:
Somehow magically, the last few weeks have been focused on thought leadership and following my thoughts on ‘Schmoozing’, I got 20% of readers writing back to me with their perspectives. Two more perspectives on that note for today: A reader asked me to write about Competing vs. Collaborating. Separately and secondly, I met a bunch of highly accomplished families in California who were discussing their thoughts behind education and making investments for the next generation. Perhaps there is a convergence on both the topics at the tail of my message today.

Competitive vs. Collaborative Colleagues We often get impacted by competitive colleagues. Aggressive competitive mindset can be demonstrated by a combination of schmoozing, blowing your trumpet, ‘I did this’ stories, undermining others’ ideas, withholding information, dominating conversations and exaggerating mistakes of others. Largely extroverted and bombastic behaviors.

On the other hand, individuals who are collaborators and wanting to work with others will have the ‘we did it’ mindset, recognize efforts as a team, allow people to speak, help others to prepare and cover up for others’ mistakes.

Investing for the Next Generation We often worry about rising costs of academia irrespective of whether you have young children, ‘ready to attend college’ children or for some of you who are trying to get additional specialized education. One of the colleagues at the dinner also mentioned that we need to prepare for the cost of weddings of our children and perhaps even a down payment for their new home. I was flabbergasted.

Often as a parent or for self, we will aim to stretch and spend handsomely on education. Many of the undergrad programs in the United States cost $400,000 over 4 years. You have seen similar costs in your geography or for your programs. Given that financial abilities vary, I would suggest to not stretch beyond your means to educate yourself or your dependents and to not make them dependent on your financial position for a fancy wedding or a house payment. In all situations competence is what matters. These individuals that shared commitments for education, marriage or properties all had converged to technology, of course we are in the Silicon Valley for this instance. They came from diverse backgrounds like being a nutritionist, aerospace engineer or an MBA.

Formal education certainly provides the baseline. Navigation in the real world, unknown to many of us, is purely through building competence.

Competence: Superseding ‘Formal’ Academia or Competitive vs. Collaborative Mindset Competence is largely built as a function of cognizance (awareness) and perseverance. Here is a checklist

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- Identify the problems and opportunities that exist You identify the most challenging initiatives that bring you outside of your comfort zone Learn and develop skills to solve these difficult problems Look for cost effective relevant and alternate training mechanisms to develop skills in adjacency. Basic formal education is required that should not create a long term debt Stay ahead with industry insights Be an effective listener and make decisive contributions Voice assertive opinions with facts Outpace deadlines and targets. Seek help from influential team members to collaborate Be open to receiving and providing constructive criticism Communicate accomplishments with facts and celebrate often Obviously there is more to this list, so I am always looking forward to hearing your thoughts and ideas.

title: 'Internal' Neuralink: Connecting With Your Mind
content:
Neuralink, an Elon Musk company, is building a brain-computer interface that can bypass damaged neural pathways and facilitate direct communication between the brain and external devices.

I came across two really great talks and picked a third from my past life to connect a few dots. What if there was a linkage mechanism like Neuralink that can super-accelerate your connection and engagement with the external world. I am sure that there are thousands of such talks, however my intention at this point is just to provoke thoughts in order to achieve this acceleration.

1. Your body language may shape who you are (24 Minutes) Amy in this talk mentions "power posing," where adopting expansive, confident postures for two minutes can boost feelings of power and reduce stress. It raises your testosterone and reduces your cortisol. You fake and infuse confidence in your posing and thinking not just to make it but to become it. This authentic change will foster personal growth and empowerment. This internal wiring and preparedness can then leverage your body posture and expressions to further make seamless engagement with the external world. How?

2. You are (or can be) Contagious When you meet a person first, what do you look at? Face, Shoes or Body. Not quite, they look at your hands first. Your hands speak a lot - a hawk, a defender or someone who welcomes. Now I figured what it meant to welcome with open arms. They look at whether you are holding a rock, scissor or welcome/warmth. If you can’t see someone’s hands you can’t see her intentions. Greatest of the Ted speakers use 465 hand gestures in 18 minutes. Your brain gives 12.5x weightage to other people's hand gestures. Our implied and expressed emotions, fears and intentions are contagious. When you smile artificially with just your mouth open and naturally with your mouth open stretching your cheeks all the way to your eyes, you look more welcoming. Try it. Lastly when you speak what matters is the deep rooted tone of your intentions. All in all to accelerate your messaging to the outside world, from the thoughts so far, your power posing matters and then you exponentiate that with your niceness. The magic continues.

3. The Problem With Being “Too Nice” Now when people speak, you can know easily when people are being nice artificially or naturally. When someone speaks about your shoes or your attire, you can most always tell how much of that is natural or artificial. What does one want to hear?

The spoken feedback loop is also a very difficult layer to lay over your posture and expressive niceness. Imagine an interpersonal dance when we give and receive feedback. Tough feedback without honesty and nicety creates discomfort in the recipient’s body language. Shrunk body or pulled back chair. It is a lot easier to give general feedback: You did great, keep doing what you are doing, I love your sense of humor and a lot more while actually not giving honest feedback. Imagine, someone at your work tells you to take more initiative. Whatever that means - lord save me at that point.

Try to be honest and tough at first. Start with the positive part of the content (you did great, the topic was awesome, your confidence was very high), move to the net neutral aspects next (you could have changed the order, used a different font or a more impactful set of words) and finally lean into the constructive part. Recommend replacement behaviors: You talked about a great topic although you could have provided a course of action. They did not walk away with the understanding that you had intended to convey…. I am sure there is more.

Take away from here: You see that the whole process of impactfully engaging with the external world is and can be very cyclical. Your inner wiring, your body language, your warmth, your message and the construct of it. The feedback loop too. I am amazed at how we exist. Take feedback from people you work with and find out if they see you as a package or if you see a package somewhere in someone.

As always, do write back and share your feedback. Let me know what else I can think about.

title: The Magic of 90 (Days of Transition)
content:
Following the US election drama in the last few weeks, I wondered what it must feel like to be in the transition phase of about 2.5 months between the announcement of the result and the actual point in time when the new president-elect moves into office. When both the leader and the ruling party change, there will be many opportunity windows.

- For the departing leader, to implement the last set of desirable actions
- For the arriving leader, to start defining the canvas of the new term roadmap and
- Most importantly, for the arriving leader to understand the impact of the departing leader’s actions on the new term roadmap Stakes are very high in such transitions. It made me realize that all of our lives, personal and professional, can be managed as chunks keeping similar perspectives. Before I get to labelling these 90-day units, a few plain basics.

Plain Basics John Thompson, one of my CEOs from the past, got us a T-shirt labelled ‘This is the most important quarter of the year’ with no specific date. It made me realize for the first time that personal growth is non-negotiable and must be continuous.

Earlier in March 2024 I wrote about your personalized ‘Network Development Plan’ as a comparable analogy to ‘Gross Domestic Product’. This is what it looked like definitionally NDP = C + R + I + (X - A) where

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- NDP C R I X A => Network Development Plan => Content (Largely the Accomplishments) => Relationships (People in your Life) => Investments (Intellectual Growth & Support) => X Factor (Accelerators) => Abscondment (De-Accelerators) Now the labels.

First 90 Days If you are in the first 90 days of a job, a business, a relationship or any kind of personal journey, you focus on the first impressions made, learning about your environment, first set of goals, defining a structure for success, coalition/befriending, making an impactful presence and most importantly avoiding any boo-boos.

Previous 90 Days & Next 90 Days At the beginning of the new 90-day window, you look back at two important aspects of the ‘Network Development Plan’ components: The X Factor and Abscondments. X Factors will allow you to ensure continuous growth with accelerators and awareness of abscondment will keep you cognitively aware.

As you plan for your next 90 days, know of your big chunks (Content), Coalition/Befriending (Relationship) and Investments (Intellectual Growth). Set aside time to think of breakthroughs you will accomplish as part of your X factor.

As humans, we tend to gratify ourselves from efforts in the past compared to the outcomes from the past. This is a trap which can slow you down. Of course, you must always celebrate success.

Last (Ending) 90 Days When you begin to end something, you complete your assignments, do a very effective and honest handover and know that you are not burning a bridge of the past. Sometimes you need these bridges as part of your future journeys. You are leaving a brand and a legacy behind. Imagine as a departing leader of a nation, what your last actions can constitute. Someone else’s first 90 Days This is where your X-Factor can come into play to help others in arriving gracefully. This is where you can build relationships and trust. You will enable both the individual and the organization. Schmooze respectfully.

Takeaways Similar to a nation’s GDP, your Personal or Network Development Plan is a function of 90-day chunks. You will have X Factors that will provide accelerators and growth, always seek for them. Remember, two-quarters of negative growth leads to a recession: repetitive abscondments will make you recessionary.

Do you know your 90-day chunks? Are you growing enough or you are recessionary? Do you have a recovery plan? Lastly, growth has to adjust for inflation and goals need to grow larger. Make it fun.

title: Fixing a Nation in Hyperinflation
content:
guidance offered to large institutions: Increase revenues and decrease expenses. Now imagine you are a nation suffering from hyperinflation (144%), corruption, debt, unemployment and poverty (45%) and get this advice. How do you fix that? How long does it take? How do you deal with adversaries in the process? This is the story of Argentina in the last 12 months and the greatest of the economic reforms that took place under the leadership of Javier Milei, president of Argentina. In November ‘23, Milei won decisively with 56% of the vote, marking a seismic shift in Argentina’s political landscape in favor of Milei’s unconventional style and promises of sweeping reform. What evolved in the year from thereon will be etched in the history books.

In a capitalistic society, you aim to provide good quality goods for your citizens at a good price point. According to Milei, you are either a socialist economy or one that is capitalist, everything that is in between is a neoclassical trap.

Drivers that led to Reformation of Captilasitc Argentinian Economy

- Milei approached to eliminate monopolies and concentrated economic structures. Any economy that encourages competition thrives in the long run
- In a big way, his belief in being a minarchist built a very aggressive approach to removing and reducing governmental regulations. As a minarchist, you support minimal governance limiting to essential functions like defence, law, and order. This reduces costs, reduces regulations and interventions, leading to very effective abilities to conduct business inside a nation
- He created a ministry of deregulation that reduced between 1 to 5 regulations daily assuming that there are still 3,200 regulations pending reviews. Regulations with energy subsidies, import/export barriers, currency exchange controls for foreign currencies, and rental property laws were repealed to encourage competition, economic prosperity and with reduced governmental intervention
- Elimination of intermediaries in the form of poverty managers, who asked for counter service (bribes) led to not only reduced expenses but also improved fiscal balances and better service for the citizens
- The number of cabinet-level ministries was reduced from 18 to 9. Among those dissolved were the Ministries of Culture, Education, Health, Labor, Social Development, Public Works, Housing, Transportation, Energy, and Telecommunications. These were merged into new entities like the Ministry of Human Capital and the Ministry of Infrastructure. This led to elimination of thousands of redundant jobs
- Inflation subsequently reduced from 54% annually to a much desirable number of close to 2% How do you fight with the forces within the nation?

To have been in a leadership position meant fighting with the adversaries whether they are the opposing politicians, corruption or the lopsided media houses. Here are some highlights

- Before Millei, Argentina faced 900 roadblocks a year. Its citizens were paid to participate in the roadblocks and attacks. By the virtue of elimination of the poverty managers and other bureaucrats, many intermediaries were eliminated at first
- Citizens participating in roadblocks were barred from receiving poverty benefits and some were jailed
- Aggressive reduction and removal of public works agencies reduced obscured accounting and uncontrollable spends
- Corrupt officials were jailed and their pensions were taken away
- The judiciary was further empowered to make decisions
- Regulatory powers were taken away from politicians, companies and power groups to create a true capitalist and competitive landscape
- Social Media and other media platforms were leveraged to reduce the influence of corrupt journalists Governance in Capitalistic Societies Some things that came to my mind to draw corollaries
- For nations to become successful and growth-oriented, leadership is the key. The economic brilliance of Javier Milei helped the way. Leadership teams matter a lot
- Picking a cue from Argentina and the large debt of $35T+ in perspective, the United States has initiated a brand new initiative to set the foundation effective January 25 in the form of the creation of DOGE, Department of Government Efficiency. They will do a similar review to reduce and eliminate governmental inefficiencies
- The U.S. has recently proposed imposing tariffs on Canada and Mexico, particularly as part of measures to combat illegal immigration and the smuggling of drugs like fentanyl
- It takes almost 3 days to transfer funds across financial institutions in the United States. India (UPI). Brazil (Pix), Singapore (PayNow), Malaysia (Duitnow), and some of the EU nations like Norway, Sweden and Denmark emphasize real-time payment efficiency and financial inclusion. The US and other advanced economies will need to learn and leverage these techniques I am sure there are more aspects that will help nations prosper like Argentina has done in a year. Looking forward to hearing from you all on what other insights you may have. I was inspired to write from this Lex Friedman podcast with Javier Millei, which was a truly intriguing experience for me.

title: Play the Ball, Not the Bowler
content:
I was on a trip to Rajasthan last week, and after a very long time, I went away without my computer, so I missed writing last week.

Watching the India-Australia cricket match preview today, I came across an important message, ‘Play the ball and not the man’, in context with how you need not worry about the larger perceived beast that might not even exist and focus on what is in your control. Today’s thoughts are a trigger of this basic understanding.

Tug of War between Approach and Outcomes Imagine a project where you have invested a fair, if not significant, amount of time and resources. It could be about learning a comprehensive skill like becoming a financial advisor, working for a cause like a not-for-profit initiative to provide education for underprivileged society or building your own business or a company.

With most of these, you would have explicit or implicit end goals in mind: It can be about outcomes like revenue, profits, sales, a new job, promotion, number of lives enriched or number of trees planted. It can be anything. For the sake of simplicity, let’s call these outputs. To achieve your goals, you need resources in any combination of funds, skills, processes, people and institutions. You leverage these resources and navigate through circumstances that involve bureaucracy, chemistry and other real-life challenges. For the sake of simplicity, let’s call these as inputs.

Time goes by and you reach a point where you have made progress with your stated goals or outputs. Through the process, you may realize some surprising findings about your environment: your inputs in terms of your beliefs or values are conflicted. You are on the course to achieve your goals (outputs) and there are fundamental questions that remain unanswered about the methodology employed. Possibly to be even more clearer that the methodology employed is not aligned with how you think.

With inputs, methodology or the process employed, there are three possibilities: 1. In an ideal world, with a fairy tale perspective, methodologies are perfect (inputs) and they are in turn yielding desirable outcomes (outputs) 2. Many a times, you may realize that outputs are not yielded as the methodologies for inputs are compromised. Easy to fix - press Reset here 3. Often you get the stated results however the methodology employed is somewhat or significantly compromised In scenarios 2 & 3 above you have some decisions to make. Obviously, in scenario #2 above, you need to realize quickly and decide if you need to fail fast. In a situation like #3 above, you need a deeper examination. What does that look like?

Fail Fast or Deliberate This is a game of your conscience: Milk the cow (proverbially) or Fix the game

- If your fundamental beliefs are compromised then it is a lot easier to fail than to deliberate
- If there is an opportunity to navigate and fix the methodology and the associated process assuming you want to capitalize on the progress and fix the methodology then certainly stay the course a. Confront with the reality and have honest conversations with people involved b. Identify all the red tapes that exist, talk openly with involved constituencies and people c. Make time-bound recommendations and a very effective tracking mechanism
- Prolonged situations in scenario #3 can have deep impacts on your personal (mental and physical well-being) a. Silent and looking ‘composed’ individuals can have serious personal impacts b. Individuals with outbursts to confront loudly may benefit from being expressive. This can cause certain immediate short-term impacts on others involved however you are better off with this quick failure than a dreaded deliberation A big example of significant changes was experienced with major shifts in Meta’s (Facebook)

### strategies

- They decommissioned their biggest crypto initiative named ‘Libra’ in early 2022
- They right-sized (headcount reduction) their organization and significantly changed their AR investment strategies in early 2023
- They invested efficiently in Open Source AI framework by building a series of LLM (Llama series) models for their business’ forward-looking strategy
- Enviably, their share is valued at 6x from where it bottomed below $100 not too long ago As Meta would have faced the ball and not the man behind in the cricketing analogy, have you come across such situations? Is there a monster behind your goals that intimidates you in terms of the man with the ball as opposed to the ball itself? What if the monster is the one that pays your bills and more? What would you do?

title: Look Back, Introspect, Repeat
content:
When you look back at life, you can think of major milestones and setbacks. Rights and Wrongs. Perhaps there is a method to repeating the rights: Look Back, Introspect, Improvise, Rinse and Repeat.

Satya Nadella, in this conversation, looked back to identify things that worked for Microsoft. It is a long list: starting from Windows 3.1, Windows NT, Internet Explorer, Dynamics, Gaming, Office 365, Azure, LinkedIn, and now their AI journey with the investment in OpenAI. There were some bumps on the way, like mobile (remember Nokia), search, Skype, and their attempts to play in the larger SaaS and Cybersecurity business.

Failing at something is perfectly alright Last week I touched upon ‘Failing Fast’ and this time around with Satya’s narrative and a few other bits, here is an attempt for a broader correlation.

- Failure of Microsoft Search or Skype is an indication of differenting between not doing something compared to failing at doing something. There are more things Microsoft was successful from doing compared to the failure from doing thing
- When Microsoft started the Azure (Cloud) journey, AWS was already ahead of the game. Similarly, Google had a lot of advancements in Deepmind and Microsoft could still capitalize on their AI game through their investment in OpenAI. These are never one-player games whether it is AWS vs. Azure, Gemini vs. OpenAi or Databricks vs. Snowflake
- Having lost their ability to capitalize from their leadership with PCs and Windows to a shift in cloud computing wasn’t bad. If you keep playing, you can win it all again and they did with Azure over time
- In another example when Apple had to design the Macintosh improvements they were competing with a $30B IBM giant compared to their about a $1B business value. As Steve Jobs said, when companies grow bigger they want to replicate success by repeating the institutionalized processes or frameworks. Process wrongly becomes a strategic driver. In reality, your best people and the teams are the ones who understand the content for innovation far more than the process itself. These high achievers may be a pain in the back to manage however their content and creations can be second to none
- Of course, there are companies like Amazon who have a fascinating process of no PowerPoint slides and they have their ‘infamous notes readout’ mechanism prior to meetings. These powerful processes do exist, however, they are deemed to be useless without outstanding content created by top talent When the world speaks about Microsoft, they speak of Satya equally as much as they would have of Bill Gates. Imagine someone like Satya who is a consummate insider promoted to the CEO of the company and then getting the company to be the most valued business ($3T+) in the history of time. At one point, Satya’s wife presented to him a book titled Growth Mindset (Carol Dweck) and this mindset is instituted in every aspect of Microsoft’s growth over the last decade and more.

In a nutshell, to always keep doing something is imperative, failure is part of the process, and the process itself cannot determine success.

- 
- 
- 
- Right people on the job, the right content/creation from them, the growth mindset and their relentless perseverance will get you to win, always Do you have a growth mindset, do you have a high degree of perseverance and are you in the right job without the overheads of process or expectations complexity?
