What a great time of the year to ponder these questions… Happy holidays.

title: Agents (AI) and Agency (Human)
content:
As we end the year, let's think about Agents (AI) and Agency (Human). Most of the AI based approaches are to mimic and exemplify human behaviors in machines. Conversely, for this time, let’s personify this ‘Agentic Mindset’ inside a Human Being.

A Recap: AI Agents Earlier in October (Oct 6), I wrote about AI agents.

Specialized AI agents are typically Small Language Models which are derivatives of Large Language Models and provide a continuous feedback learning loop back into the LLMs. These models interact with each other, create knowledge graphs and report back causal (Cause and Effect) outcomes to the underlying LLMs. Specialized agents offer artificial intelligence in a specific scenario.

In simple words, compared to a Large Language Model like ChatGPT responding to any kind of questions a specialized model is trained for a specific purpose like writing SQL statements or in simpler terms a fitness tracking app using TinyGPT will suggest workout routines based on user history and goals.

Just remember that in the Agentic AI architecture, orchestrating layer can invoke calls to multiple specialized agents.

High Agency Mindset Orchestration High agency mind (of a human) is trained to take full control of her actions and destiny and not wait for perfect conditions or blame the external environment. If you were a ‘high agency’ individual, your orchestration layer would be a very determined mindset that will always avoid hallucination. You will invoke a variety of agents in your personal space to persuade, to navigate, to not be distracted and to constantly be a mechanic of reinforced learning through any kind (human or machine) feedback. Your mind here is strengthened to activate variety of personal agents

- One set of agents will keep your focus on pursuing goals with determination. This agent conceives ideas and turns them into concrete actions
- An agent in you will take responsibility of your life and focus on what you can control
- An agent that will evaluate situations based on your knowledge repository and wisdom more than accepting external opinions
- Another agent to push through challenges: this one will especially look for how other individuals that do not have high agency are not distracting you. Distractions can come from external agencies, largely humans, who can be a combination of nay sayers or are conservative thinkers in nature As a high-agility individual, you are always on your ‘treadmill’, energizing others, seeking growth, and not giving up on constraints.

This is a great time of the year to reflect on your grit. Ask yourself: Am I trained to stay focused? Do I realize when distractions slow me down? Do I find an excuse or blame in the outside world? Are my goals big enough?

title: For Your Ears Only: AI and Communication
content:
As we kick into the new year, I wonder how we define some James Bond-like experiences and how we achieve them. Two thoughts on that note: A corollary between new-age AI-enabled smart devices and communicating effectively.

For Your ‘Ears’ Only: If there was a James Bond movie starring currently available smart devices then it would certainly be titled as ‘For Your Ears Only’, just for the fact that they always have their ears ON. You have already experienced how you are talking about something with your friends or family and the next thing you see is a promotional advertisement of products related to your conversations on your social and professional media feeds.

As soon as Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) or Artificial Super Intelligence (ASI) will kick in, you will see an AGI/ASI starrer representation of Matrix (Keanu Reeves Sci-Fi from 1999).

This eavesdropping of AI in our lives made me think about what it means to keep our ears open as a human or do we behave like Mahatma Gandhi’s three monkeys: See No Evil, Hear No Evil and Speak No Evil. Unfortunately, modern-day organizations are not wired to sustain this thought. Let’s devise how one can behave in an organizational setup.

Redefining ‘See No Evil, Hear No Evil, Speak No Evil’: To do the best in your profession you need to keep your ears open. This to me is not about eavesdropping but to build a super cognitive spatial awareness. If you see something or hear something that needs attention then be very critically aware of what transpires. After all, modern-day organizations (for profit or otherwise) are designed to drive certain outcomes and we often hear the other corporate jargon: If you see something then say something. Here is a quick look at what modern-day organizational monkeys would look like (courtesy DALL.lE openAI) and this was the prompt: ‘Can you create a visual representation that shows Gandhi’s 3 monkeys with a slight modification that shows one monkey with hear no evil selectively listening with one ear open, second one with appreciating the evil with one eye open and the third one speaking wisely… perhaps sitting in a corporate setup inside a conference room‘ How does one execute being an effective communicator?

- ‘Seeing/Hearing’ things require effective reading of the room. Knowing who is in charge (the authority), knowing participants, body language, responses from yes (wo)men, the silence of the quiet ones, the hollow loudspeakers and authentic participants is important. Scan the room to know participants in all the segments and their (un)intended messages
- Now that you have some cognition of the players, process the most important stuff, stuff that matters
- Build an active filter to know who is listening and who is not listening. Who in the authority is around to hear and understand what is going on
- Once you identify the critical stuff that matters, identify who needs to hear this
- Know what needs to be told. More importantly, identify the mechanics: How will you tell and When will you tell In a nutshell: Understand the redefined ‘organizational’ monkeys
- Keep your eyes and ears open
- Know what is going on and if you see/hear things that impact our being then certainly do something about it. You will know that something is going on, so don’t translate this approach to an equivalent of eavesdropping
- Be direct in your communication after identifying the message, the people that need to be communicated, the packaging of the message, timing and the location
- When something matters, certainly do something about it and don’t be a silent observer on the sideline
- Make a dent and think of 10x value disruptions Do you recall any situations where you personified this modern-day monkey business? Did it help or backfire? Will you try and become so, if you did not voice out your part in the past? Do you think differently?

Happy New Year

title: Rome Is Burning: The LA Fires
content:
Rome is Burning: It is estimated that ongoing Los Angeles (California) fires have caused damages equivalent to $100B to $150B in losses. Today, there is no unmanned surveillance or preventive defense mechanism in place to detect and prevent these outrages. There are some companies that provide aerial intelligence to help with the defense mechanism for the responding agencies however that is not enough. Significant progress in AI and Quantum Computing is required to defend the planet from these calamities.

As the progressive changes evolve in the AI space, it will expose us to, both, the curses and the boons. Geoffery Hinton, the Godfather of AI, quotes that there is a 10% to 20% chance of AI leading to human extinction in three decades. Well the outlook is fairly positive with that low probability, however there is a possibility of extinction as well from the low probability. To the best of my understanding, the impact will not be probabilistic, but of some proportionate representation of change in our lives. In a way Hinton actually increased the chances from 10 to 10-20% range and in response he stated that we have never had to deal with things (AI) more intelligent than ourselves before. The only thing that came close was an example of an infant baby that controlled her mother, who is the more intelligent of the two.

What it takes to prevent Rome from burning: What does it then take to positively engage with this fast changing world and what do you focus on? Two weeks back I brought up this ‘high agency’ mindset and here are some thoughts that purely focus on the input process and value focus on the quality of work done as opposed to the quantitative outcomes.

The Process Outlook

- While not demeaning ‘(y)our’ formal education, you will not learn anything deeply in your basic undergrad programs. Of course, if you are doing deep and narrow vertical research as part of a doctorate program then it is a completely different story. No matter what each of us studied, to use and apply AI for example in our professional life, we will need to go back to some AI training.
- Our learning and knowledge application process needs to be continuous. This involves broad reading, researching, continuous hunger for information, talking to really smart people and the ability to connect information from unrelated fields.
- Critical thinking and Problem solving mindsets are far more important skills. Now imagine that as part of your work assignment, you have a unique problem, unrelated to your academic knowledge, to solve. For example, when you went to college a decade ago at a Marketing school and you were building a social media campaign when a lot of the influencer based techniques were not documented. Obviously, you will need to acquire knowledge and skills to understand platform-specific algorithms, content creation for different social media formats, and build relationships with influencers.
- You need to leverage the tools, leverage a lot of easily accessible doctorate-like online research and work with a high agency mindset. Don’t use one off answers from these tools but build skills to master a given topic with a lot of foundational details and then apply the tools of learning. It does not mean that you walk into your physician’s clinic, and post doing some cursory research on the internet, tell her what is wrong with you. Being Obsessed: In addition to having a great process outlook you need to obsessed with: 1. Simplicity for what you build 2. Apply Design Thinking 3. Speaking like how your Customer would like to hear from you 4. Communicating to the world with a Marketing mindset 5. Giving them an experience, not a product After all Larry Ellison (Oracle), Bill Gates (Microsoft), Steve Jobs (Apple), Mark Zuckerberg (Meta) and Elon Musk (X, SpaceX, Tesla, Neuralink) obsessively built the greatest of the companies focusing on process outlook. They all also have the commonality of dropping out of their college programs at some point.

So coming back to the latest controversy on absolute 90 hours a week effort, do you believe if all of these leaders worked hard? They all did and they still do - the good thing is nobody told them to. My take away for that is to remain net neutral of what these 90 hours a week opinions and pushes are - make your choices, build your process, be obsessed and do what is right for you.

title: Eight Million Jobs, Unfilled
content:
December 2024 reported 8 million open jobs compared to 6.4 million unemployed individuals in the U.S. This has largely been the trend for a while and that there is still either a lack of desire or lack of skills for employment. We completed more than 2 years of significant advances in AI/LLMs, post OpenAI’s ChatGPT announcement in Nov ‘22.

In the early part of the 20th century, tractors were introduced with the mindset of efficiently feeding the humankind and not to create disharmony in the agricultural economy. AI has to operate similarly; else, with all our jobs eliminated, we will have machines working with the machines and make humans extinct in the process. It is very unlikely that humans will destroy her race. This necessitates the AI and the machines to coexist in harmony with humans.

What has changed in the last 2 years?

Obviously, the first thing we got access to was a very simple ability to prompt the models with our questions. Advances in the AI/LLMs improvised, both, the professionals and the people consuming their

### services. Here are some examples

- Content Creators and Copywriters: Nowadays, a lot of the blogs and content are seamlessly created with the use of LLMs. Grammarly.com and Writer.com provide powerful AI based writing and editing tools
- Administrative Tools: AI tools now help to automate email correspondence, meeting scheduling, travel management and data trend identification. Microsoft 365 Copilot is one such provider and Athena Executive Services provide virtual assistants to perform all these tasks
- Customer Support Representatives: AirBnB has for over years not provided human based customer support and has relied significantly on their knowledge tree. Several AI based technical support frameworks now partially replace the human based support
- Legal Analysts and Paralegals: Harvey.Ai and Casetext significantly expedite processing and analyzing of legal documents, dramatically lowering the workload on junior staff What can additionally change in the next 2 years?

Financial Analysts, Marketeers, Medical Transcribers, Educators, Architects, Designers, Software Developers, Radiologists and Recruitment efforts will go through significant acceleration and improvisation with the help of AI based frameworks.

What AI/LLM Opportunities do exist and will evolve?

Prompt Engineers, Agentic AI Developers, AI Administrators, Synthetic Data Creators, AI Ethics Managers, AI Policy Writers and users of the above mentioned and evolving AI frameworks will find new modes of employment. If you are a professional in a non-AI field, your knowhow and use of these evolving frameworks will keep you relevant, make you hyper productive and make you a successful leader in your journeys.

Additionally, AI will also expose us to new age vulnerabilities and will necessitate more growth in building specialized skills, information security, and intelligent data management.

Convergence of AI, Robotics and the Physical World We will see this convergence in healthcare to conduct remote and robotic surgeries, as humanoids in factory setup, autonomous full service driving, ai-powered robots for companionship, monitoring for the elderly, in warfare and in securing the physical world from calamities.

As I wrap this topic, let’s not forget that AI based executive assistant will support human business leaders, AI based content will be read by humans, AI in Healthcare will enable physicians and benefit the humans after all, full service driving or autonomous vehicles will move humans or goods consumed by humans, humanoids will help produce goods for the use of humans and care for elder humans (not just machines) and AI will support customers who are humans…. The real question is what role do you want to play, which side of the equation do you want to be on and how would you capitalize these advancements in your being?

title: An AI Update
content:
It's time for an AI update, once again. Most of you are familiar with ChatGPT (OpenAI’s Large Language Model) which enables tasks for text generation, translation and natural language processing based on the large source of text datasets, which are widely available on the Internet or otherwise. ChatGPT and other models evolved to become multi-modal in nature to process modalities of texts, voice, images and videos to generate output in form of texts, voices and images, largely speaking.

Outlook for the year 2025: I have already mentioned about AI agents and here is the recap to

### level set baseline understanding for today’s write-up

Specialized AI agents are typically Small Language Models, as derivatives of Large Language Models. They provide a continuous feedback learning loop back into the LLMs. These models interact with each other, create knowledge graphs and report back causal (Cause and Effect) outcomes to the underlying LLMs. Specialized agents offer artificial intelligence in a specific scenario.

In simple words, compared to a Large Language Model like ChatGPT responding to any kind of questions a specialized model is trained for a specific purpose like writing SQL statements or in simpler terms a fitness tracking app can be created from using TinyGPT, which can suggest workout routines based on user history and goals.

OpenAI announced the ‘O’ series (the letter O and not the number 0) of models starting with O1 and recently released model chatGPT O3. Both these models respond with well-reasoned explanations. To understand with an example, for a prompt like “If Sarah is older than Tom, and Tom is older than Mike, who is the youngest?” ChatGPT O1 will respond after reasoning to understand that Sarah is older than Tom, Tom is older than Mike and therefore, the age hierarchy is: Sarah > Tom > Mike to conclude that Mike is the youngest. Comparatively, version O3 provides deeper and complex reasoning making it more capable, however it becomes slower due to increased ‘think time’.

In addition, OpenAI announced the operator mode which is available for the advanced users who pay $200 a month for their subscription. Operator mode works like a classic human operator to solve complex problems with AI: for example if you provide a recipe, image of available ingredients in your refrigerator or pantry then the operator mode can identify the missing ingredients. It will go and prep your order for the online grocery provider and wait for you to submit the order.

The reasoning, operator mode and the agentic AI capabilities will be greatly leveraged in 2025. Chinese Goods have arrived: DeepSeek is a Chinese AI startup that has developed advanced language models, notably the DeepSeek-R1, the opensource AI model that is as powerful as OpenAI’s O1. Notably, it was released within only 2 months of OpenAI’s O3 release. They also released 6 additional small scale models ranging from 1.5B to 70B parameters. Remember that a parameter is a weight assigned as a connection between 2 neurons of a model, which adjusts during the training of the model and improves predictions. Smaller models provide ease of use, low cost and better efficiency for well defined problems.

- Like many classic Chinese goods, cost of million input tokens is only $0.55 and million output tokens is $2.19. OpenAI is 7x more expensive in comparison
- While reasoning DeepSeek-R1 shares the underlying chain of thoughts similar to explanation provided above to determine Mike as the youngest of the 3 people
- DeepSeek-R1 Model was built cost-effectively as, unlike OpenAI, not much consideration was put on safety and was built with very high IQ Chinese engineers, of course at a much lower cost. Sounds apt for Chinese product
- Although this low cost model will allow many startups to compete easily in the grander scale Announcing Stargate, a $500B US led AI Initiative: On another note, last week, Donald Trump led the announcement of $500 billion AI infrastructure project in the United States as Joint venture between OpenAI, SoftBank, Oracle, and MGX, an Emirati investment firm.
- $500B will be spent between now and 2029 with $100B already committed for deployment in 2025 with the first data center deployment in Abilene, Texas
- This AI infrastructure aims to enable early cancer detection using AI-powered blood tests, develop personalized cancer vaccines using AI and mRNA
- It will also support various AI applications across industries including healthcare and national security
- With this large investment plan, it will create significant entry barriers for desiring bad actors
- Of course, China and other leading economies will bounce back with their investments and probably even a low cost alternative This $500B investment is in addition to
- Colossus, Musk’s 100,000 Nvidia H100 GPUs supercomputer built already to expand to 200,000 GPUs and eventual scale of over 1 million GPUs. This was built in Memphis, Tennessee just within 3 months as a collaboration between xAI (Musk), Dell, Nvidia and SuperMicro
- Microsoft, Meta and Amazon plan to invest additional $80B, $75B and $65B respectively in 2025 to expand their AI infrastructure Power games to win in the AI space have begun. Time to keep our eyes and ears open

title: Circles: Family, Friends and Profession
content:
All of us have a family, a trusted circle of friends and a professional environment. Some of you are leaders or matured practitioners in your profession, some are aspiring to be the leader tomorrow and some in early phase of their careers. Let’s assume different roles to dig deeper.

Circle of Life: As a parent or as a child, you have experienced situations where perceivingly one sibling gets a lot of attention and many of her shortcomings were tolerated. You may have been a sibling who was benefitted or in a given situation you were impacted by privileges the other ones received. As a parent, you may have unintentionally favored one child over the other. Similarly, in a professional setup you have seen colleagues who have been favorites in an organization and as a leader you may have favored one bunch over the others, yet again unintentionally. Friends also share similar behaviours or feelings with each other.

In all these interactions you are impacted from other people’s behaviors: some demonstrate anger, some share complaints, some become seemingly or relatively dysfunctional or nonperformant in their jobs. The Lion King: Whether family, friends or professional setup, you have relationships with other people and their relative (in)abilities to be expressive. In my professional leadership development workshops, I spend a lot of time on cognizance (awareness of world around us): ability to understand the workplace, the environment, the economy, the culture and most importantly the people at all levels. A key aspect of cognizance requires extraordinary communication skills.

- When someone is complaining, whining or showing unruly behavhiors, many a times, they don’t say what they really want to say
- These behaviors can distort you and frustrate you as you don’t have time for all this. At the workplace, you just want to finish your day and go back to family. At home, you just want to finish your dinner and get ready for the workday. With friends, you just want to finish drinking your coffee (or other brown liquids) and get back to whatever is next
- Most always these obvious unruly behaviors or interactions happen because you actually don’t hear what they actually mean to say
- They see you in a position of power, a caregiver, a solution provider or just somebody who needs to lend their ears Most always, we deal with a very difficult cocktail of minds and we feel burdened. Why am I holding myself responsible for these people? They are adult co-workers, friends or family so get your game together guys. Unfortunately, life does not work like this. As a result be the king, the Lion King:
- 
- 
- 
- Firstly, enjoy being in the position of power that someone is trusting you to be the confidante Lend them your ears. Try and understand what they actually want to say Don’t judge people in a very hard way as it it detrimental to both the parties On January 5th, I wrote about reading the room and similarly today the message is to focus on the other person and try to understand why they came to you When someone asks for your time irrespective of what the issue is give it to them right away. Don’t setup a meeting to decide when the actual meeting will happen. I have seen many conversations, that actually only need 5-10 of hallway or drive-time conversations, take weeks to be setup. So move at the pace of the progress AI is making and be out there. Always check-in on people you care about. This is why: unusual part of thoughts this week as I need all of you to spend 2 minutes to watch this video. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tX8TgVR33KM

title: Corporate Enters Politics
content:
Corporate enters Politics: Earlier this year, Donald Trump initiated his second term as the president of the United States. Prior to joining politics, he had a diverse business career spanning several decades from Real Estate to Hotels, Casinos, Television and more. His transition from business to politics at the highest levels of government is one of the firsts, largely uncommon at this point in time.

### Wholly owned Corporations by the Governments

- Within the United States amongst a few United States Postal Service (USPS) and Amtrak - National Railroad Passenger Corporation are largely loss making entities
- BBC, Channel Four Television Corporation, and Network Rail are owned by the government of United Kingdom and are generally loss-making or reliant on subsidies. There are profitable entities like Royal Mint (Coin and Bullion makers) and UK's Export Finance
- Japanese government owned public corporations and infrastructure entities are generally profitable due to a strong industrial base in Japan
- The Government of India profitably runs public sector enterprises like ONGC, NTPC, Coal India, and Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL). Whereas the Telco arms like BSNL, MTNL and steel sectors are loss making entities

### Pension Fund Investments allocations in Corporations

- In the United States pension funds are invested in a diverse mix of equities, private equity and government bonds with increasing allocations to private markets
- In the United Kingdom pension funds are primarily invested in bonds and some in equities along with some real estate holdings
- In India, National Pension System invests in a mix of government bonds, corporate debt and equities with a focus on safe returns
- Japan and Germany divide pension funds investments in equities and fixed incomes It is imperative that with advances in technology, introduction of corporate experienced industry leaders in the government agencies and ever increasing national debts, all the loss making government run enterprises/agencies across the planet and their investments will be scrutinized.

The Big Players: If we look at an interesting mix of countries by their projected GDPs for the year 2025 and companies by their current valuation, here is a list of the top 15: 1. United States (GDP): $30.34 trillion 2. China (GDP): $19.79 trillion 3. Germany (GDP): $4.92 trillion 4. Japan (GDP): $4.39 trillion 5. India (GDP): $4.27 trillion 6. United Kingdom (GDP): $3.73 trillion 7. Apple (Market Cap): $3.42 trillion 8. Nvidia (Market Cap): $3.27 trillion 9. France (GDP): $3.22 trillion1 10.Microsoft (Market Cap): $3.08 trillion 11.Amazon (Market Cap): $2.4 trillion 12.Alphabet/Google (Market Cap): $2.3 trillion 13.Saudi Aramco (Market Cap): $1.8 trillion 14.Meta/Facebook (Market Cap): $1.8 trillion 15.Tesla (Market Cap): $1.12 trillion This is roughly a 50:50 list with company valuations displacing many of the countries in the recent years.

So, what makes us think about this interspersion of the corporations and countries

- With the recent announcement of DeepSeek (referring to my earlier mention of the Chinese goods 2 weeks back) AI technology in addition to other cloud based premises, easily cross geographic international boundaries
- It is evident that the starting point for this Chinese made model was based on hardware (GPUs) and AI models invented in the United States
- If you access the Chinese AI model in a remote capacity from anywhere on the planet, you are providing China with access to your data
- Donald Trump and the United States as a result is starting to impose tariffs based on a combination of source nation and sourced goods. Such tariffs already exist for many other nations
- Sanctions are already being put in place across the planet for certain export orientations of chipsets and on fossil fuel. Some of them like ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) have existed for years In a nutshell, we are seeing a large cocktail in the making. Corporations are experiencing a monumental growth in their valuations and Countries are regulating multinational configurations of the Corporates, including applying sanctions, tariffs and government participation. Remember the ban on TikTok for the proposed US ownership for a 50% share of the organization and protecting the associated data.

International Alliances (Peace) and Wars in the future will have big boundary-less digital components in the play, weapons including. Keep your eyes and ears open.

title: Two Decades of Logos
content:
In 2 Decades: Prior to plotting this chart, I could not have recognized half of these plotted logos. If you put your birth year in perspective you can think of how much of your life has overlapped ever since 2004 that the not so smart devices gave you the pleasure of solo-time in the fantasy islands created by these platforms. We have moved away from ‘sharing the feelings’ to ‘seeing/showing fantasies’. Beyond the social fantasies is the world of assumptive entitlements. Perks in the social or professional environments are often taken for granted. Fitness centers in the housing or commercial complexes remain greatly underutilized as you have paid for them indirectly. Free coffee at the office (some of the luckier ones get a lot more) is taken for granted. So, why all this?

The ‘Giver’ Mindset: One big thing besides the perks that remains underutilized is in the contagion you can offer. Earlier, I had mentioned about the fact that the first thing people notice when they see you, are your hands, not your outfits or your shoes.

This greatly underutilized contagion of being and feeling good, which is easily available to you remains largely unnoticed. Here is a quick checklist to be able to share and communicate with others:

- Assuming that others around you, especially the quiet ones, are okay is dangerous
- Your team, at home and at the workplace, need assurances more often than you can imagine
- Communicating often and giving occasional praises go a long way, especially in intense workplaces
- Communicating failures, bumps or challenges in timely manner matters a lot
- Timely expressiveness of joy and connection creates a safety net for others
- These investments build credibility for to you to later share tough messages easily
- If you are one of those who can’t express with words then you can bring in cookies to share One more thing that I learnt lately is that we make a lot of reforms with fitness, learning or wake up schedule. Most of us suck at keeping up to them. Above all of these reforms, sits the determination to do what you decide to do. If there is one thing that you can fix then this is it: the sheer determination to keep up to your own commitments to self. So what you are waiting for, go ahead and bake those cookies for your buds.

title: From the 8086 to the AI Architect
content:
My first introduction to a computing processor (CPU) was to an Intel chip number 8086. A simple program to add two numbers ‘3’ and ‘5’ looked like this.

- 
- 
- 
- 3E 05; C6 03; 76 This was machine language code to say, load number 05 in a register (3E) Add immediately the number 03 to the register (C6) Halt the program (76) Assembly language was the next improvement to the above machine language representation, which was primarily a program in terms of hexadecimal numbers (16 digits in the form of 0 to 9 followed by A to F compared to decimals ranging only from 0 to 9). The same program was written in Assembly language

### with a slight improvement for human understanding

- MVI 03, A (Move 3 to A)
- ADI 05 (Add 05 to A so A = A + 05)
- HLT (Halt the program) Programming languages like FORTRAN, C, C++, and Java brought in many advances. We use the algorithmic representation in understanding the problem and solution which is a lot more English-like along with increased sophistication and functionality. We write modules that were invoked as reusable components of programs. We write algorithms and associated code which become reusable as well. So, why talk about this somewhat antiquated representation of life?

With continuous progression and scientific growth, we can solve many problems without needing to know what lies beneath the engine. I have been writing ‘Sunday Thoughts’ for a while now and wished to create a portal. Sandeep Upadhyay, my colleague and our PMO head in India, offered to help during his weekend to get this out, however it continued to stay in the backburner of my priorities up until now.

- Most of the people I associate with now have the ability to use AI and the large language models to ask questions, compose emails and brainstorm thoughts. Remember that 40% of the world still does not have access to the Internet or the SmartPhones
- The next thing after LLMs that we have is a workbench to write code using some of the available code accelerators or co-pilots. So I decided to use the co-pilots and LLMs to publish all my writeups. Firstly, I asked LLM to parse my long singular file of 2 years worth of writing into individual pdfs by using the authored date as a delimiter. Bingo - 1 minute later I had my individual pdfs. Essentially, without any prior experience of programming I could create a lot of code using these co-pilots. I used ‘Cursor’ and OpenAI’s co-pilot
- Another co-pilot mode is also available in the algorithmic nature of the language models. The ‘Operator mode’ is one such recent development. It enables you to instruct the language model to perform repetitive tasks
- Operator mode makes ChatGPT not just a text generator but a gateway to a suite of tools:
- It receives and parses your request. In my example, I told the operator to pick each of my writeup files and give it a title.
- It decides which external tool to call (e.g., DALL·E for images, Python for code execution). I asked OpenAI in operator mode to create a prompt for the image and image subsequently to store the image in the database. I asked it to create entries for categories, content relevance (dated or not) and the creation date
- It sent the requests to the operator mindset and retrieved the output.
- It integrates the external output into its final, cohesive response. It organized my postgres database with all the intricate details for each of the articles, individual pdf files and the ai generated images that represent the article.

This seamless integration allows you to generate multimedia, code, and dynamic information in one interactive conversation. So essentially my process last week became a write-up for the thoughts this week. Here is the representation of the first cut and also access to the past write-ups. Hope you all can leverage on this automated operator assistant similarly or better.

title: What Coaching Leaders Taught Me
content:
I have been coaching a bunch of leaders for the past 2 months. When I asked them to look back and identify major accomplishments from the past year, they could easily do so. Although the narratives turned out to be impressive, they came back with caveats, which may not be uncommon for a lot of us.

- Outcomes were not tied to specific pre-written objectives, they just happened
- There was a mindset of gratification, of course in a good way, largely for the effort based inputs that provided qualitative outcomes. I led, I improved, I implemented, I trained… all good stuff however without a connection to the predetermined objectives
- Largely individuals expect guidance for what they need to do and may not easily be able to determine the expected measures at the gate. She may have improved something or implemented something from efforts however it does not tie to either to the larger objective or does not quite meaningfully add to the sum total game
- Most of us need a BHAG (Big Hairy Audacious Goal), a compelling long term intriguing goal that keeps us inspired to keep pushing. Of course, I need someone to tell me what this should be - it is a natural tendency Either I need someone to tell me what the larger purpose of the institution or the organization should be or if I am an independent one person entity then I will keep pushing to do what I do in the best possible manner without defining this BHAG (Be-HAG).

What do you do if this does not exist in your definition of life? I say one of 3:

- Ask for the larger purpose which the institution or the organization is driving towards
- If you cannot get this purpose clearly from them then recommend for what it should be and use it as a reference to create your purpose for navigation
- If you are an independent one person entity then create your Be-HAG, something that you think is largely unachievable Now tie your life relentlessly to achieve this. It does not mean you don’t take the time to drink your poison, smell the roses or have a good time. Intersperse life and the purpose of it. As a human being, you can also be slightly multi-purposed. I know of this ambitious person who wants to ensure that no one sleeps hungry, at least in her vicinity. She can certainly do so however unless you are sitting on a gold mine, you are not going to easily be able to feed the world. In this instance, she can have a professional goal to excel in her work and generate a lot of wealth. She can incrementally then work towards the larger goal of feeding the world. She can certainly have 2 BHAGs. This is your life and you have choices to make.

With that said, ask yourself if you know of your BHAG, your organization’s BHAG, are you going to ask for it in absence of one and create one if you do not gain that clarity. I want to follow up on this thought to write about how we can remain undeterred with our BHAGs and stay focused. Next week on that aspect.

As a starter, I heard this somewhere. Define your BHAG and start working on this, the path will appear. Clarity does not come before action, it comes from action.

title: Knowing Your Personal Chatter (I)
content:
Knowing your personal chatter (1): Do you talk to yourself? Answer is a big yes, even if you responded with a ‘no’ right now while reading this. We all do. When we do speak with self, we drift away from the current moment of time and create chatter. Think about, as mentioned last week, having a defined BHAG (Big Hairy Audacious Goal) and then constantly drifting our mind away from objective(s).

Some examples of how it happens. You are driving and all of a sudden you arrived at your destination without even remembering how you got there, not remembering your list of groceries at the grocery store you had decided to purchase when you left home or not remembering what you read through the chapter of a book by the time you reach at the end of the chapter. Chatter is normal and not all chatter is bad.

When in a social context, we use the reflection from chatter to impress others from our conversations, which is quite natural. At the same time, we can use this inner voice as an advantage to achieve our goals. We also need to recognize that while we were formally taught to learn maths, learn to paint, or even learn to swim, no one taught us to concentrate as a skill. Teachers always told us to pay attention however never to show ‘how’?

Technology Traps: An easy example of how we easily get distracted is in the infinite loop of social media reels. They work our mind in orchestration with our implied expectations of distraction. You don’t even know what the next reel would be, however ‘they’ have figured out what will make you stick. Our ability to pay attention or stay focused on the task at hand is very low so we go in this endless loop. In another example, imagine you are sitting on your couch and you get called to help by your spouse or your parent. Your ability to respond to them is much lower than a passive beep from your phone’s messages. Technology can be good as long as we are incharge of it.

Everything written on this page so far is about ‘knowing’ what causes distractions. Now a little bit on ‘Focusing’ or ‘Being’.

Focusing or Being (2): Being ‘meditative objectively’ is an oxymoronic thought. To meditate with a purpose will not happen. Although married to one, I am nowhere close to being a practitioner, let alone writing about it.

Imagine you are watching a movie, the director has written the script to navigate in your mind to make you feel angry, joyful, hungry, sad or even lusty, all within a span of a short window. In one of the learning videos, I saw that in order to increase focus and not experience all these emotions in our chatter, we need to focus. Focus momentarily closing your eyes on the chair you might be sitting on, close your eyes to bring awareness to the room you are sitting in, remember the event like a wedding or a birthday party you may have attended recently or your recent holidays. One of the things we started following recently in my family is to remember in the morning all the things we ate or drank in the previous 24 hours, everything. This is hard and you will tend to forget many things you ate including a small candy. If practiced well, it will allow you to identify moments to focus, bring awareness of what you ate and take you back to your guilty pleasures from the day before.

Expecting Rightly (3): I personally realize that knowing and then ignoring distractions is easier, being objectively meditative is a lot harder and as a result it will be much easier for me to

### manage expectations. Here are some for me

- Be cognitively aware of distractions, dissolve chatter and focus as much as possible
- Avoid getting supplements or being drugged in order to induce sleep or bringing peace to mind
- Sleep, Exercise and eat healthy (hard, hard, hard)
- Distance yourself from remunerations or sitting in the expectations of glory
- Build long term goals and not expect short term returns So ask yourself a question in this very materialistic and dystopian world, where you do want to

### always to be

- Knowing your distractions in real-time (1),
- Being meditative (2) or
- Setting manageable expectations (3) Truth is always somewhere in between.

title: A Modern-Day Fantasy Land
content:
A write-up of a modern day Fantasy Land.

Then and Now When I first saw a Tesla car at a showroom in San Jose around 2011, I could see that there was no engine in the car and the front of the vehicle had empty space which was referred to as ‘Frunk - as a Frontside Trunk’. It was my ‘what the frunk?’ moment. Electric Vehicles actually were invented a bit prior to the combustion engine vehicles however rechargeable batteries were not invented then. After a very long history of more than 125 years of commercial use of combustion engine vehicles, hybrid technology was employed in Battery/Gasoline versions of cars like Nissan Leaf, Toyota Prius and Chevy Volt. It was followed by a massive revolution in Electric Vehicles through large scale production of EVs by Tesla. Many followed this trait. Today, we are at the cusp of FSD (Full Self Driving) in its nascent phase.

Another pot where life is melting similar to automobiles, is software engineering. Coding co-pilots are enhancing (or disrupting) the lives of software engineers. Your mastery from expertise and experience of programming languages is getting replaced by co-pilots, which accelerate your throughputs by giving 3x productivity on average. You summon the co-pilot with the best of the commands (pun intended) and it responds exceedingly well.

With another perspective, Modern telescopes like the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) can observe light emitted billions of years ago due to a combination of advanced engineering, infrared technology, and the fundamental nature of light itself. This part has fascinated me for years. We can see the past because of the ‘time machine’ effect captured by these telescopes from the light that takes billions of years to reach earth. Increase in the mirror size (2.4m to 6.5m) and increase in the wavelength capture range (0.1 to 2.5 microns to 0.6 to 28.5 microns) has made JSWT far more capable then the preceding Hubble Space Telescope.

Now and Then Human Gut Microbiome or the Gut Microbiota is composed of microorganisms including bacteria, viruses, and fungi. Functions of diet, lifestyle and age can have an adverse or positive effect on the health of the gut to enable immunity, vitamin creation, immune system modulation and maintaining the barrier to keep the gut health intact. Damaged gut can impact heart health, liver health, and mental health resulting in diseases/dysfunctionality like cholesterol, diabetes, fatty liver, deteriorated circadian rhythm and more. Supplements like Super gut and procedures like Fecal Microbial Transplant (replacing your Gut Microbiome entirely from a healthy gut) can attack and fix the underlying gut surface area which actually is a cause for many of the named diseases.

In another example, introduction of ‘Neuralink’ in experimental stages will help to manage significant dysfunctions in the human back problems and even the vision. This is giving a huge hope in fixing a lot of physical impairments by leveraging healthcare and technology improvements together.

So why this and that with ‘Now/Then and Then/Now’?

A lot will change in the field of automobiles, software, healthcare, transportation and more.

- You will see flying cars as potential improvement to Full Service Driving (FSD)
- By combining capabilities of images, audio visuals, write-ups, and ‘sora’ (OpenAI’s video creation tool) like tools, your great grandchildren will be able to see your past life similar to James Webb Space Telescope seeing the space (this is scary - they are going to find out either way so this is the best point in time to start behaving)
- Human body parts and diseases will be easily replaced and cured respectively with a lot more ease
- As an auto mechanic in less than 2 decades from now if/when all the combustion engine vehicles have been retired, you will need a new set of skills. The new problems & defects will largely be autocorrected & auto-repaired. We will need specialized technicians to repair flying cars
- As a software engineer your imagination and analytical skills will play a far bigger role than your technical knowhow
- Neuralink can possibly be used by ordinary humans to see (visualize) Northern Lights from a distance as an example. I am sure since I have not heard of things like national parks or forestation in interplanetary setup the extra terrestrial lifeform already has an equivalent technology (They never showed up with clothes in movies so they are already thinking like the Emperor wearing his new robe) I am sure there are many more examples. This is a great point in time to imagine how life will change in the next 2 decades. You can remain excited or live in fear. Choose your play.

title: Fan, Not Fanatic: Personal Expectations
content:
‘Personal’ Expectations: Being a fan should not turn you into a fanatic, which can lead to building extreme or dangerous perspectives. As a result, it was an easy choice to make this weekend to follow through my personal study over watching a fantasy like cricket game, in spite of being a big fan of the game.

One of the leaders, that I worked for, dedicated time every Saturday morning on a book she planned to write. It took a few years of this perseverance and she eventually published her book about being a working mom. My lead to dedicatedly use part of my weekend to study about the topics of my interest was an instigation from her approach. I knew that to make this drastic change for my own expectations needed both, the discipline and a measurable outcome. I kept the process as simple as possible. Every weekend I look for significant changes in the world around us: Technology, Geopolitics, Economics and their interdependence on each other.

- Technology has been an instrumental foundation for the economic growth at least in the last decade
- Keeping aside some religious fanatics, Geopolitical tugs of war have been around ‘energy’ and ‘natural’ resources
- Transportation, Manufacturing, Technology, Warfare and more: Economic prosperity of nations from these mentioned activities is significantly dependent on ‘energy’ and ‘resources’, in addition to their unwavering focus on education and political stability. Hence the interdependence of the three on each other Our navigation to exist in this interdependent world requires very high acumen and cognizance and hence a lot of my write-ups also focus on ‘Thought Leadership’. This defines the quad of my categories of writing: AI/Technology, Geopolitics, Macroeconomics and Thought Leadership. Perseverance: The need for measuring what I learnt, selfishly speaking, created the ‘Sunday Thoughts’ series. In the two year that I have been writing these, I had to make significant changes in my engagement with this varied group of people across age groups and professions. It involved moving the narrative from tech-centric write-ups to a simplified language and also increased my focus on navigational skills in this ever changing world. A lot of write-ups on ‘Thought Leadership’ as a result.

Outcomes: The process gives me many things: discipline, commitment, staying connected with my network, drive to learn new things, write code, using the AI tools of the new era and a stronger ability to have greater conversations in this ever changing landscape. I take pride in marking this milestone with my 100th write-up today with a deep sense of gratitude for all your readership, feedback, your introspections and sharing more ideas to navigate in this complex landscape of life.

title: The Crowded Space of LLMs
content:
The Crowded Space (Refresher): Many of you have used chatGPT, which is one the most popular Large Language Models. HuggingFace.com, the marketplace for chatGPT and other LLMs hosts hundreds of thousands of such models. Including Small and Medium models, the site hosts over 1.5M models. Here are some popular LLMs:

Following OpenAI ChatGPT release in late 2020, Llama (by Meta in Feb ‘23), Claude (by Anthropic in Mar ‘23), Bard which got rebranded as Gemini (by Google in Mar ‘23), Perplexity (by Perplexity AI in Oct ‘23), Grok (by Musk in Nov ‘23) and DeepSeek the newest, cheapest and latest Chinese entrant in Jan ‘25.

Model Progression: The models have progressed to build multi-modality to input text, voice, images and to generate text, voice, images, and videos in response. Remember the two dimensions here of the models: multi-modality and the generative nature of these models making them very powerful over time.

- The operator mode of the LLMs can execute task like grocery ordering or travel booking autonomously by handing complex multistep processes without any human intervention
- The operator mode can also generate powerful graphical user interfaces and integration of third-party tools like Google Workspace, Slack or Microsoft Office
- In addition to the operator mode, the models have also developed reasoning abilities to decompose a problem into multiple steps with chain of thoughts or tree of thoughts logic. With this approach a problem is solved with a little more time in the play however it has a more realistic and methodical approach in solving the problem. This behavior provides a lot more meaningful responses in solving problems related to healthcare, education and technical support interactions
- The latest capability introduced in chatGPT is to generate powerful visuals with far more accurate textual elements and real life look and feel in the images So, why would I share this refresher? One element is to understand the comprehension and the competition in the space and the other is to simplify integration of these models in real-life scenarios.

### Orchestration

- Imagine as a doctor, if you would like to leverage your specific patient’s history, or anonymously leveraging all your patients’ interactions and combining them with powerful knowledge from research books. You can selectively generative capabilities of LLMs to leverage this knowledge and get many more perspectives invisible to the human eye/brain
- In other examples, marketer can build the same leverage with a campaign, repository of all campaigns or industry knowledge to generate more intelligence leveraging LLMs
- Same theory applies to Software Developer’s knowledgebase, a Tutor and her notes, a Storyteller and her stories, and many more use cases The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard introduced by Anthropic in November 2024 to enable seamless integration between AI systems and external tools or data sources. MCP (no males involved here - just the model) eliminates the need for custom integrations by providing a universal framework, allowing large language models (LLMs) to securely access real-time context and interact with diverse systems like databases, APIs, and content repositories as mentioned in the examples above.

Here is the depiction of such a process where you right hand side could be any form of knowledge and tools that can feed that knowledge into the LLMs The Future: This landscape is getting far more comprehensive and competitive. Not many of the LLM companies are making proportionate revenue for the cost of infrastructure in deploying these models. The hope for the future still remains bullish and perhaps AGI (Artificial General Intelligence) will change the game.

What is in it for me? In discussing these opportunities, I come across a handful of naysayers. A technology leader refuses to use these capabilities as she wants her employees to understand the underlying layers of programming before the employees can use these answer sheets or co-pilot coders.

It took me back to my engineering years and possibly some of you can relate to the same. High-schools are teaching a lot of programming languages and excel workings that we (and many of you) learnt late in your learning cycle.

Students and Employees need to learn both methodical workings to know what is under the hood and also exploit tools in a far more proportion that is going to propel the journey. A reminder once again, your job or profession will not be replaced by a machine but it is certainly vulnerable to the ones that know how to use AI in the same context. So, are you ready?

title: Traditional TV and the Western Screen
content:
Television sets at most of the hotels in California are not upgraded to the smart ones yet, which gave me some exposure to local TV channels in the recent weeks. Compared to the Eastern hemisphere, the West still follows a lot of traditional television, especially the news channels. Theme of the advertisements on the channels were three fold, largely speaking.

- Pharmaceutical/Medicinal advertisements for skin diseases, weight management, depression and diabetes are the ones that I can remember
- There were quite a few advertisements from life, health and property insurance companies
- Lastly, all the channels consistently showed legal/law firms that would fight for you, mostly to make money from accidents based lawsuits All the pharma advertisements had fast voiceover of disclaimers to defend from any future lawsuits. Insurance companies promoted defense from life and health events. Law firms showed you all possible ways how accident victims could make money from so-called offenders. In a good way, I know of two family friends who did not file a lawsuit for a handful of accidents they met with and let go of the individuals that erred. So, why this preface? I want to write about two things based on this narrative: Assurance from Insurance and secondly about how we should make decisions.

Assurance from Insurance: Common forms of insurance include health coverage, life insurance policies, automobiles and travel insurances. Most employers provide coverage for health and life insurances. Automobile insurance is somewhat mandatory across the developed world. We get a sense of assurance from insurances for personal and family’s well being to defend from untimely events in life.

Working (or not working) for an employer, fighting for a promotion in a given job, to relocate for work, and to make a significant shift in your professional life involve a certain amount of risk taking, as we constantly seek assurances. These risk avoiding behaviors reflect on the need for insurance to make decisions. Most always we make decisions in favor of materialistic outcomes and as such there is nothing wrong with it.

At this point, let’s think about our cognitive (in)abilities that lead us to making certain choices. Decision Making: We often have these deja vu moments and possible regrets regarding new products (it was my idea), investments (I knew this company isn’t going to do well), relationships (I knew from the beginning they were not meant for each other) and a long list of things. We come across a lot of information and decision crossroads to pick an option from many. We decide for

- 
- 
- 
- 
- Academic path to follow (specific stream and levels to get the Master’s or Doctorate) Mode of employment and the associated employer(s) Rent or Buy a property Choosing a life partner Investments we make and a lot more Here is a perspective to not have a long list of regrettable things. I was advising a founder CTO last week regarding how to introduce AI in their product and business operations. The leader of this company (the CEO) is in hyper growth mode primarily focusing on selling their high in demand product to new customers. Being a start-up there is not much appetite to make huge investments for introducing AI/LLM capabilities.

I suggested that the CTO employ a simple model with her leadership team (the details in the parenthesis which is specific guidance from me to her and may be slightly technical in nature so

### ignore them based on your readership)

- Build a model that shows options or choices (4 Choices: Radical or Incremental introduction of AI in operational and product capabilities in this instance)
- Make a recommendation (Recommending a feature set introduction that leveraged AI/LLM, specifically Agentic RAG, capability in one of their product lines and introducing AI enablement in the operational world, in this instance - this would give a jump start)
- Recommend investments and advantages from making this choice (Upkeep within the industry, Competitive landscape and definitive product advantages in this instance)
- Outcomes from not making this choice (dilution in competitive rigor, in this instance)
- Risks associated (A slightly longer product cycle to begin with, in this instance) If at first this model or the approach is rejected, go back in 45 days clearly indicating what we lost from inaction. The rationale for recommendations can include some or all of the following in the descending order of priority: What we do will positively disrupt our world, increase revenue/profitability, save resources (money, headcount, and time), and significantly enhance life experiences.

In utilizing this model, don’t apply this to make small decisions. Imagine if you had such a comprehensive workflow to decide on which flavor of ice cream to eat.

Some more ‘To Dos’ and ‘Don'ts’ Do

- 
- 
- 
- 
- Be cognitively super-aware of size and impact of the decisions Read and research similar historical data points Predict what the future holds for the world around us Make sure the time is right and remember it is never too late Ensure your basics are protected (Capital protection, Family wellbeing etc.)
- Have a sense of gratitude towards what you already have accomplished Do not
- Follow the crowd
- Let someone decide for you
- Do not sweat on small decisions - eat your ice cream and make sure to burn it down
- Accept your surrounding and live with mediocrity
- buy Insurance for everything at the cost of not experimenting There is a lot more however I am breaking here to grab my cappuccino, let’s add cocoa powder as well.

title: The World by GDP
content:
Country GDP (USD Trillions) Population (Millions) GDP per Capita (USD) GDP as % of whole 1 United States $30.34 347.3 $87,400 26.3 2 China $19.53 1,416.10 $13,800 16.9 3 Germany $4.92 85.9 $57,300 4.3 4 Japan $4.39 123.5 $35,500 3.8 5 India $4.27 1,463.90 $2,900 3.7 6 United Kingdom $3.73 68.9 $54,100 3.2 7 France $3.28 68.6 $47,800 2.8 8 Italy $2.46 58.7 $41,900 2.1 9 Canada $2.33 39.3 $59,300 2.0 10 Brazil $2.31 217 $10,600 2.0 11 Russia $2.20 142.8 $15,400 1.9 12 South Korea $1.95 52 $37,500 1.7 13 Australia $1.88 26.9 $69,900 1.6 14 Spain $1.83 48.1 $38,000 1.6 15 Indonesia $1.49 285.7 $5,200 1.3 Remaining 180 $28.59 3,755.30 $7,613 24.8 Global $115.50 8,200.00 $14,085 I created the table above to understand projected macroeconomic standing of the top 15 powerful nations for the year 2025. Here are some observations

- More than 75% of the Global GDP (Gross Domestic Product) is represented by 15 of the 195 member countries of United Nations
- 55% of the global GDP is represented by top 5 nations
- If you take into consideration the massive populations of India and China then 5 nations also represent 42% of the global population
- In a nutshell 55% of the global GDP is represented by 42% of the population
- Remaining 180 countries represent 1/4th of the global GDP with 46% of the world’s population Now the Tariff Wars As you can imagine, early announcements of tariffs have economically crippled the world. We faced covid from 2020-2022, unending wars in the last 3 years and now this. Here is yet another bird’s eye view of tariffs
- To manage ever increasing debt ($38T), headwinds from inflation, possibly to bring production domestically and to boost the economic growth (GDP), the US wants to impose reciprocatory tariffs on the supplying world, especially the likes of China or just China, primarily
- Tariff impositions will actually increase the cost of goods, say iPhone for example. This will largely impact the consumer as the manufacturers will simply pass on the cost to the consumers
- Manufacturing domestically in the US will necessitate many things starting with power (energy) demands, infrastructure, attached technology in manufacturing, associated labor and most importantly the will power of the population to do these high intensity jobs. This will not happen overnight and will take a long term of 5 to 10 years, if at all
- Outsourcing and offshoring have evolved for a reason to provide the economy of scale. Just look at how the disproportionately high populations in India and China have enabled this for years

### A few things to ponder having said this

- In this trade war, there will be marginal winners and losers with zero net gains
- Nothing seemingly will make any of the nations more productive right away and additionally causing a lot of near and mid term derailments
- Somewhere to manage the manufacturing price and the process, the manufacturing countries will have to introduce substandard mechanics which will negatively impacting their population and the environment
- If the manufacturing did move to the US in some cases, the cost of production will still be high or even higher. This will force some form of domestic subsidies to increase affordability of expensive goods Fortunately there are some early signs of negotiations and considerations. Many considerations are required beyond economic growth. Clear skies and clean air in some geographies will not prevent natural calamities around the planet.

The planet does not benefit from the big becoming bigger. It does not benefit from the increased gap between number 1 and number 2, number 2 and number 3. Citizens of the world need to survive from wars and pandemics. We do not need this price (tariff) war right now on top of everything.

title: Making Peace With AI: Conflicts and the Human Touch
content:
Before I get into the depth of this topic, there is a correlation which some have laughed about: The over-referenced Characterization of powerful words in my life that begin with a “C”. My first strategic technology framework from 2010 was based on 4 themes: Cloud, Consolidation, Compliance and Cost. The Management workshop that I Conceived as “Leadership Laboratory” is based on 4 Cs: Customers, Communication, Cognizance and Charisma. A few more “Cs” to add today.

Technology: Conflict of Creativity vs. Cementation/Comfort: I saw a podcast yesterday where a presenter was sharing concerns about how AI will possibly Cement the Creativity of the Human Mind. In another incident, a technology leader was hesitant to introduce ‘AI’ tools in his company with the fear that his employees would not understand the foundational concepts of technology due to ‘AI’ driven automation. This fear exists with parents whose children are leveraging AI to do homework, all kinds of artists who feel their art will not be appreciated due to AI-generated art, and more. This Conflict—technology and AI Cementing processes and killing creativity—has induced a lot of reluctance and fear in humans. There is also a fear that AI will make humans more Comfortable and lonely as a result. Fear arising from excessive use of (Ro)bots in our lives leading to Comfort in loneliness.

Distinguishing Internet, Cloud and AI: They certainly evolved in the order of their mention. We have been using the internet from the early 1990s to search and to access a lot of information from the WWW (World Wide Web). Similarly the Cloud became popular from 2008 onwards. A simple example of Cloud is in accessing your emails. You may not have been a creator in the cloud era, however all of us are definite consumers of the cloud. AI is different. Imagine these three vectors: Individualism, Industrialism and Information Technology stack.

- As an Individual AI is far more accessible for both creation and consumption. You can become far more productive and competitive as an AI user in your personal capacity
- Industrial growth will accelerate from AI: Automobile, Agriculture, Manufacturing, Healthcare and all you can name. Combination of Data, Robotics, AI and other technologies will provide hyper acceleration in social and economic reforms built in these industries
- Leveraging AI in the Information Technology stack to build disruptive applications is inevitable This narrative necessitates confrontation with AI, and in the absence of that, you will lose your edge as an individual, industrialist or technologist without this embracement. Are you Conflicted with this AI intimidation?

If yes then read on. If not then ask why not? Dealing with any conflict needs dissection.

- Firstly, build Courage to define your conflict. Know what has led you to avoid this, both as acknowledgement and awareness
- Second phase is to build Curiosity after Courageously Confronting. Ask questions to know why the conflict began in the first place. What stopped you from addressing this so far? If you personally do not have this conflict, and you are courageous enough, then determine how you will create Curiosity for others to embrace AI
- Lastly, build Clarity for action. What are the next steps? What tools do I employ and how will I bring awareness, education and utilization in perspective? Are you building awareness of tools outside of your natural choices? ChatGPT is just the beginning of this journey. Feel free to write to me if you need help with starting points. Even if I may not know personally, I will have enough resources to guide us This model of Courage, Curiosity and Clarity can be used with any kind of organizational, interpersonal, or even personal Conflicts.

title: Taking a Pause From the Ordinary
content:
This write-up though delayed is an instigator and a bit longer. It is a process to take a pause from the ordinary and see how you will change a few ways of working with AI or if you are a newbie to AI then how this can all start in a very refreshing way.

AI: ‘in Action’ or ‘Inaction’: Following my rant on AI conflicts and human touch last week, here is some more. Imagine if AI can become powerful to understand your context, your intent and very particularly the tone of your voice. How can you better collaborate with AI? This is with an assurance that it will not kill your creativity, unlike the obvious fears, but make you more creative.

If you are a non-technical professional and want to collaborate better with Generative AI (ChatGPT and her cousins) then this is for you. By the way, we now give this recognition to chatGPT so she can now have identity (She/Her) on her LinkedIn account. If you are a technical professional then you can use this point of view for yourself and/or to inspire other non-technical associates, professionals or not. This process can dramatically augment and amplify your creativity.

- Instead of asking direct questions to LLMs, we should ask LLMs first to say, ‘What is the best way for me to ask this question to AI for it to answer it well?’ You see the point and if you do not read this again
- With this you can now ask ‘AI to use AI’ as opposed to how we could not use Excel to use Excel, which would have sounded oxymoronic in the years
- Ask AI (ChatGPT or any of her cousin) to find how I can best leverage AI: Ask AI to ask you enough questions till it understands your responsibilities, workflows, and KPIs (Key Performance Indicators)
- After she has learnt about you, now ask her to make two obvious and two not-so obvious recommendations
- Given the neuronic being of LLMs/AI, it will continuously assess her own work. If you watch the blog below you will see a beauty in an example where a non-technical professional was able to build a very highly effective tool using AI, for many many professionals
- Many of us are not able to fully utilize AI’s true potential (90% of us to be specific)
- Outperformers treat AI like a teammate compared to a tool. As a teammate, when you get mediocre performance you give him/her the feedback - why would you not do the same with AI. Give feedback, coach AI, and improvise her context in your engagement
- In an example in the same podcast here, you will see how you can use AI to simulate an individual who you might be having a confrontational conversation with
- Without the interactive and iterative process with AI/LLMs, you will fixate on the first response as an obvious solution. I am sure there is a lot more however here is the link to the podcast The AI Monolog (13 minutes): https://youtu.be/wv779vmyPVY?si=yp1xVwOABRbp7Izd

### My journey and the Attached Narrative

- All the tools in my work product below use builtin LLM capabilities indirectly
- Although technical by profession, I have very low familiarity with intricacy of the tools used so you can consider my engagement with a non-professional orientation
- Leveraging the tools, I learnt about a lot of the product creation platforms, boosted my productivity and created a lot more room for the creativity
- If the framework sounds very technical, just appreciate the gist of the process and build inference in your personal technical or non-technical capacity Automating the Thoughts Engine with Generative AI: As you all know, I write every week and have a linear document that tracks my ‘Sunday Thoughts’ which are separated by date of authoring. I have a distribution list of people that I send these thoughts to by email. I decided to improvise the initially created ‘Sunday Thoughts’ portal using AI tools to accelerate and simplify the distribution process. In addition, preceding the distribution a lot goes in organizing the content intricately, again using AI tools. Here are some more details on how the AI engine helps me to do my job. Some parts are repeated here from my past update however without them it will not give a holistic view of the process Content Organization
- I created an API (Application Programming Interface - an automated way to summon chatGPT from my software code) to access ChatGPT in its operator mode
- I asked chatGPT to write a python script (python is a popular programming/scripting language) by first taking the large monolithic file of 100+ articles and dividing it into individual pdf files, using author date as a delimiter. 30 seconds later I had the individual pdf files
- I created a repository on Supabase/Postgres database portal (similar to Google drive but with specialization for accessing data in a specific format) to store my data and the content files (Images, Writeups, and more)
- Database stores information of my subscribers with basic user profile and a separate table (just like an excel sheet) for my write ups to track article title, creation date, document name, image file for the article, article pdf file name, formatted write up for web rendering, category of the writeup as mentioned in the four categories (Technology/AI, Macroeconomics, Thought Leadership and Geopolitics), determining if the content is dated in terms of time relevance or ageless and lastly tracking comments of my readers
- I created another file store on Supabase to save images for the write ups, pdf file of the writeups, and audio version of the AI generated podcast
- I asked chatGPT to write another python script to take each of the generated pdfs read them using the operator mode and process each of them in the following way:
- Give an AI generated title for the document
- Identify category combination based on the content in the writeup
- Asked chatGPT to generate an image that can best represent this article
- Save the created date
- Take all the generated and known data for each of the files subsequently to create an entry in my database to use it for my distribution portal Content Distribution
- This part is for my portal for content distribution. I used Vercel to host my portal site
- I used an image from my imagination to ask Vercel to give me basic shell of software code to render my article content
- I used Cursor.ai to develop the website using Next.js and React.js based framework.
- React.js is a library for building user interfaces, mainly handling the view layer
- Next.js is a framework built on React that adds server-side rendering, routing, and full-stack capabilities out of the box.
- In short: React builds components; Next.js builds entire web apps faster, with SEO, routing, and API support included.
- I kept interacting using Cursor.Ai to iteratively write the code to render my articles: like showing my latest article on the top, showing a scrollable grid and the search bar on the top
- Cursor.AI essentially connects to my git repository to retrieve and push my codebase to the website. It reads and writes content and data into my Supabase data and content repository
- When you click on the ‘Read this Article’ button, I instruct openAI again in operator mode to render the article as opposed to just displaying the pdf file. So far, this only shows the text mode and I will add rendering of the attached images in the near future
- Besides using OpenAI in operator mode, I also tried something else. Using Eleven-Labs.com I could save a representation of my voice (tone and accent) for AI’s use. Eleven-labs.com converts the pdf files (Text to Speech ) to podcasts of my articles using my voice. It then has the ability to read my article and narrate the article using my voice as a podcast link on the website. This feature though used and tried is cost prohibitive to introduce at this point in time.

All this was done with zero programming skills and it is just the beginning.

### Here are the tools I used

- Cursor.AI - generative AI capabilities to write my python and javascript code ($20/month subscription)
- Supabase/Postgres Data Store - generative AI capabilities to create/update tables, store data and manage the API for connecting to the Supabase environment through my codebase (Currently using trial subscription due to the nature and scale of my work)
- Vercel helps to host my website and write baseline code to convert graphical design into starter set code (Currently using trial subscription due to the nature and scale of my work)
- Github to store my code repository for version and release management ((Currently using trial subscription due to the nature and scale of my work)
- OpenAI - I have had this subscription for leveraging LLMs for a while now ($20/month subscription)
- OpenAI - to use the API and operator mode, I had to purchase API basic credits which I will renew once they are exhausted Here is a link to show how this magic worked for me. This is just the beginning and I will add more features overtime. Oh no, if you got this far please write to me and I will send you the link as I am fixing a defect as I write this.

Do you have recommendations for improvisations? Are there other tools that are worthy of reviewing and mentioning in the future?

title: Technology, Wealth and Redistribution
content:
Technological Explosion and its impact on Wealth: Growth, Disparity and Redistribution “Unicorn” is a privately held company with a valuation of $1B+ and “Decacorn” is similarly valued at $10B+. This is a prediction that, very soon, we will likely have Unicorns run by a single individual. Let’s say that, at least in today’s times, I have seen half a dozen founders running enterprises valued over $1B+. Yes, just 6 employees in the company.

Cutting-edge technology has empowered a selective, small group of people to generate enormous amounts of wealth or valuation. This has, and will continue to, introduce greater disparities in the wealth distribution. Here is a quick view of the global wealth and GDP evolution across the past four Industrial Revolutions.

I.

II.

III.

IV.

Invention of the Steam Engine enabled a lot of factories and automation in the second half of the 18th century and first half of the 19th. The global GDP was tracked at roughly $500B at that point in time. 90% of the national wealth was held by 10% of the British population, with Britain being at the forefront of the growth curve Electrification, Railroads and more led to the second revolution in the late second half of the 19th century. The global GDP was tracked at $2.7T at that point in time, roughly 5.5 times that of the first revolution. The top 2% of the nation owned 50% of the national wealth in the United States, as it started to dominate the world order The Tech boom in the second half of the 20th century provided a much larger growth narrative. The US’ dominance continued in the 3rd Industrial Revolution as the global GDP grew 13x over 100 years from $2.7T to $41T In the 4th revolution, technology continues to dominate, and the global GDP grew to $110T+ as projected for this year. The world order remains somewhat challenged - the US still leads the pack however, the future will most likely have the world order leadership somewhat divided

### Democracy and Communism

In a nutshell, Democracy is a political system, and Communism is built around economic and political ideologies. Here are some key differences between the two:

1. In a democracy, multiple political parties compete, compared to a single party communist ruling 2. Democratic nations have largely capitalistic approach, and Communism is built around collective or state based ownership, largely controlled by few individuals over a long span of time 3. Communism aims to create a classless society and largely suppresses freedom (speech) to retain the state’s ideology 4. You can compare democratic nations like the US, India, Japan and Germany to countries based on Communist ideologies like North Korea, Cuba, Russia and China Now, imagine a triangle with Government as one vertex, Industries as the second and People as the third.
