Wealth is largely generated through industrial revolutions, which is accrued and consumed by people, and somehow regularized by the Government through policies and taxes. Each vertex helps maintain balance in this triangle by pulling or pushing to make an ideal equilateral triangle. However, between the industries, individuals, and geographies, the representations of these triangles can often become scalene.

Democratic nations are largely capitalist in nature. Sometimes, Communist countries try to simulate ‘Capitalist’ behaviors to create a people and industry led growth mindset. This is actually a dangerous approach. Look at what happened to Alibaba and Jack Ma following their respective growth in PRC (People’s Republic of China).

Socialism: Collective ownership leading to equality, welfare and regulated markets By definition, socialism can exist in both Democratic and Communist nations. They use policies to bring parity in well-being through progressive taxation, universal healthcare, education and pensions.

How should the employment and wealth imbalance from AI disruption be managed? Is UBI (Universal Basic Income) the answer?

Just go back to the triangle of Industries, People and the Government. When we have excessive wealth and power imbalances, the world will become more aggressive and revolutionary.

To prevent that, there should/will be governmental intervention. Although desirable, ‘Universal Basic Income’ is a very hard state to achieve. This is why?

- “Universal” applies to all humans, and given the unique powers, resources, and technological advances of each of the countries, we will continue to live in the imbalances
- People have wealth inherited and generated over centuries and decades. This cannot be disrupted
- Giving free money has never been a good sign. In the COVID years, a lot of the stimulus funding was abused for luxuries or substances This only means that the third vertex of the triangle, in the form of government, will have to create an amenable social structure through education, healthcare, subsidies and taxation. The disruption from the new age tech revolution is inevitable, and only time will determine the level of impact it will create in inequitable wealth distribution. Hopefully, common sense will prevail as it has for the centuries.

title: Gut Feelings, Explained
content:
“Gut feelings” are instincts arising from the brain-gut connection. Gut microbiome influences our emotions and decisions, triggering subconscious brain responses, mostly irrational. Gut itself is as big as the brain of a dog or a cat and hosts 500 million neurons which send signals to the brain.

So, why talk about the Gut Feelings to start with?

Firstly, to understand what we mean by gut feelings, and secondly to build awareness in order to manage our anxiety from the subconsciousness that gut feelings create. Most mental uncertainties are from the fear that originates from anxieties, largely initiated by the gut. In reality, when we are in fear, we tend to give up what we really want. What do we give up and what are some of the actions attached to not giving up? This writeup was inspired by this short ted talk so here is the quick reference for credit. https://youtu.be/bRtBHF-WPpM?si=dZHXDMpdaZhr9-mp Unfolding the Process of Wants We often find ourselves in a wrong job, uncertain relationships, and avoidance of medical diagnosis, all of which result in a prolonged procrastination. Something that holds us captive in the avoidance mode. Of all the things that I could gather from the podcast, doing a ‘Self-Audit’ stayed with me the strongest. What does the audit entail? Many dimensions expectations, my excuses and a long list of things. mental state, health, relationships, materialistic We often tend to use excuses to defend our avoidance. Excuses of impact on children from a toxic marriage, excuses for financial needs to be fulfilled while working for an incompetent leader and I am sure you have your reasons for specific avoidances in your pocket. The underlying thought of, ‘I am in the wrong place’, does not go away.

How do you conduct a self-audit?

1. In the list of your wants (what do I want part), identify the pivot of the decision - decision of your career, a given job, relationship, or health perspectives, whatever it might be?

2. Do an exercise just like you would in a fish bone analysis. If you are unfamiliar with a fish bone map then just look it up or perhaps I can write about it in future 3. As you identify dependencies around your want, determine if these dependencies are not your additional wants as well. Sometimes working for an incompetent leader or being in a toxic relationship for non-negotiable monetary expectations, play a tug of war 4. In the above parallel wants or needs scenario, appreciate that implicit material needs might be a bigger aspect that you may have been oblivious to 5. As you do the exercise, your mind expands or contracts with choices, you sense fear or joy with choices, you find excitement or dread with choices and some choices still keep you suffocated. Observe the impact on your mental state from these audit talks 6. Involve your gut again with the audit and ask, ‘Am I really in that bad a place?’ 7. A lot must be done with self before you seek help - do a deep assessment in isolation with that said 8. You can feed all these aspects into an LLM and ask questions. You will amazed with the mirror you see here however ultimately you are the one who should decide what you want 9. In lieu of an LLM or in addition, validating with your mentor helps as well. If you have a mentor go to him/her and if not find a mentor As you conduct the audit, keep in mind that you should ask a lot of questions, be honest to yourself, seek help and actively appreciate possible outcomes of action or inaction for what you want in life. This is painful however it will also provide you a launching pad for a quantum leap.

Lastly, ensure that the audit does not get you into daily analysis-paralysis syndrome. Engage with the outcomes of the audit and cool down until the next audit, which cannot happen until another significant milestone in your life.

title: The Question I'd Have Paid to Be Asked
content:
I was speaking at one of our company events and during the Q&A session somebody asked me a specific question on the future with AI. My immediate response was that I would have paid him a handsome amount to ask the question, not getting into the specificity of the questions for today’s topic. I was excited as I had conspired some questions that were off-topic. One of the hardest things is in prepping for a big presentation to the executives or for a talk to a large group. “Watch your thoughts, they become words; watch your words, they become actions” and simply to put in action instead of just watching, conspiring with these can actually help you accelerate.

Conspiring in the “Magical Kingdom” of Mind Breadth and Depth in knowledge are equally important. However, before big events like above, you will need to conspire deeply in parts.

The Thinking/Applying Part

- Magic of ‘Framework’ thinking: First principles always work the best. Identify the fundamental elements of a topic at hand, all the parts. For example, to present an effective marketing campaign, think about the product, the customer, the value proposition as it would appeal to her, and most importantly the interdependence of each part on the other as it would stitch up the story. If you have an existing template that worked in the past deconstruct it and then reconstruct with your applied thinking, while conspiring with the current situation
- Secondly, also think of an inversion mindset based on what you want to avoid. What do they not want to hear and what will not give you the sense of immense satisfaction in the post-presentation review. As an example, you know the job title you aspire for, number of followers you are looking for on social media, the compensation you want, the industry and the kind of employer you want to work for, the people that you want to associate with: use all these elements to determine what you do not want to see happen
- Do not use the baseline pre-existing model as-is, do not follow the guidance blindly, think independently and think all the time, of course not getting into a depressive state but to remain ecstatic about the opportunity. Do not worry about ‘what if it fails’ like negativity Penning it down At this stage, you will dictate your mind on a piece of paper, or a digital document. Writing helps you to organize your thoughts, it is also like reading your material twenty times, and to unmuddle the mind. Once you start writing it will help you to standardize, deduplicate, organize and most importantly discard the material you do not find relevant. Just to write these thoughts, I plug all my research material and then start discarding elements that are relatively duplicative or irrelevant as I progress through the page(s).

Rehearsing and Presenting the Narrative A little bit on how you can build confidence with your articulation. In one of the podcasts I heard this weekend it was said: Those who can articulate are dangerous people, positively speaking. Our body joints articulate and similarly if you can get your tongue to do the dance then your

### magic will influence the others

- Many are lucky in their upbringing. My father was a school teacher and taught English language and Mathematics. He taught us grammar mathematically and he had many impromptu conversations and quizzes for us. This is the first time I have ever mentioned him in my writings. We were brought up in a very modest setup although fortunate enough to have these intellectually enriching conversations at dinner time or otherwise
- Most academia does not provide any formal structure on articulation. This will need a continuous personal investment and the earlier you can make it happen, the better it is
- In order to practice, speak in front of a mirror and record on your phone. You should dry run with those who can observe and help control your body language, observe the fillers and the general flow that will help you grab your audience’s attention. Get into intelligent debates on the topic with your colleagues and friends
- When practicing remember that this has to be an intense integration of language and articulation. It will help you manifest your ingenuity and trustworthiness When articulating on the D-Day
- Pay close attention to what you say
- You are the only one who knows what is on the next-slide, next-para and the next-minute
- Expect the unexpected: Tech not working, Slides not showing up, Demo fails, you fumble and all other possibilities
- Only if you expect that an unexpected outcome will come your way, you can respond with the presence of your mind
- When responding to questions or unexpected timings, learn to pause. Ask in your mind what you think about it. Listen and respond to yourself first, quickly, and then tell the world All this work will eventually shift your mind from being vague and useless to dangerously articulate.

Imagine you are a military authority leading an army on a battlefield. You are continuously explaining your situation to the superiors, commanding the army, making life critical decisions and winning the war. Post war, you are remembering, recognizing and recommending promotions. All this needs great articulation skills so get ready to fire on all cylinders

### Credits

https://youtu.be/Z_z-QOagXZU?si=lpdPofFXzdc_zE2d https://youtu.be/2FTx7DV7sv8?si=kslO9Bz9hkR6LztE https://youtu.be/osd4XPpcWK8?si=GIrI3ZYaVFaV3clN

title: When Your Rating Gets Downgraded
content:
assessment? How did that happen and what were the impacts? This has exactly happened similarly to the US credit rating recently. Ray Dalio, a great economist of our times, beautifully explains this in 90 seconds and the write-up below drills a little more.

Downgrade of US’ Credit Sovereign Rating: With Moody’s downgrade of credit sovereign rating from AAA to AA1, as of May 2025, the United States no longer holds a top-tier sovereign

### credit rating. This happened largely on three counts

- The U.S. national debt has reached approximately $36 trillion and is projected to increase further in the coming years. For definition, this debt is the amount the US government owes from borrowing to cover deficits. Debt is largely due to funds raised from the public, foreign and institutional investors in the form of Bonds. Bonds incur regular interest payments and return of the principal at maturity
- Secondly, as you continue to spend more than the revenue you generate as a government, the deficits increase, raising concerns about fiscal sustainability. Naturally, when you spend more than how much you earn, it will have several implications
- You may have heard about the ‘One Big Beautiful Bill Act’, which is expected to be enacted by July 4, is currently under review of the US senate, and will significantly change the fiscal game. Besides changes in Energy, Technology and Healthcare policy changes, the bill incorporates increased spending and reduced revenues for the US

### government in the form of

- Provisions for additional Income Tax cuts
- Increase the debt ceiling from $36 Trillion by another $4 Trillion
- Increased spend on Defense and Border Security by more than $200B Downstream impacts of the Downgrade: Given the downgrade, this could reduce the demand for these bonds. This will necessitate tjat government to increase the yields (interest payments). When the yield on the bonds increases, it will have two effects:
- You are paying a higher interest payment for the bonds, so you will pay more to service the debt. This explains the push to raise the debt ceiling by $4T
- This will also push the central banks (Federal Reserve) to increase or maintain the interest rate for general borrowings Additional downstream impact, which is not significantly evident yet, can happen on the

### following sectors

- Equity Markets: Corporate borrowing will be discouraged and risk appetite to invest in equity markets can further dilute, especially in the interest rate sensitive segments like Real-Estate, Utilities and Growth Tech
- Currency: This can weaken the exchange rate of the US dollar, however there is also a counter that due to its nature as a standard reserve currency of the US dollar, it might rise to manage global risks. This can also lead to printing of more dollars domestically to fulfill the demand side from the increased supply of bonds
- Gold: This may increase investor optimism for gold and other hard assets, leading to an increase in price of these commodities While the downgrades reflect concerns about the U.S. fiscal position, we have seen limited immediate impact on financial markets. U.S. Treasury securities continue to be viewed as safe assets due to the country's strong economic fundamentals and the dollar's role as the global reserve currency. The US’ GDP is still $10T+ greater than its next rival in China. The downgrades serve as a warning signal, highlighting the need for policymakers to address the nation's fiscal challenges proactively.

So coming back to where we started, when you get a small ding in your performance review or your grades in academia, just look at it as a warning cycle and build a path to recovery. This points me to yet another great remark by Adam Grant stating, “The most underrated career skill is the ability to receive tough love.

Acquiring knowledge is easy. Obtaining constructive criticism is hard. If you can't handle the truth, people stop telling you the truth.

The people who grow the most are the ones who take feedback the best.”

title: A Note to the Curious and the Gen Z
content:
Readers of this post are a combination of talented AI and technology enthusiasts, Gen Z (Born in 1997–2012 phase), non-tech but 'curious about technology' business professionals and corporate employees. Today’s topic may relate to you directly or will certainly impact many younger family members who are navigating academically or are in the defining moments of their professional being. Let me start with an oxymoronic state.

Technological Oxymoron: Over the last two decades, I have been a big supporter and a sponsor of employing Early in Career Talent (EiCT). I could work with my teams and organizations to create hundreds of starter set jobs.

Over the years, a lot of experimentation, research and adoption of technologies was enabled by Early in Career Talent (EiCT). First time in the history of time, as we look at the generative nature of the AI tools, these tools perform the exact tasks that college kids did right out of college. As a result, these tools have created the largest 'net negative' impact on entry level white collar jobs, especially for those with 1 to 5 years of experience.

This situation will need a very highly committed operating model that will necessitate that corporates, governments, and academia define the forward looking work model for the new generation — otherwise the doomsday will actually begin right after the college graduation ceremony.

Recent Commentary from Leaders: Here are some relevant notable remarks from many of the technology and political leaders.

- President Barack Obama recently acknowledged the shorter work week, necessity for Universal Basic Income (more like subsidies to me) or need for building supplemental incomes. He also emphasized on upskilling to exist in the automating world or prepare to be left behind (Ref: Obama Foundation Democracy Forum)
- Sam Altman by the virtue of AI Co-pilots mentions that an effective software developer in 2025 is significantly different than what she was in 2023
- Dario Amodei, CEO of Anthropic, recently commented that more than half of entry level jobs will disappear, leading to 10 to 20% Unemployment — further strengthening the challenge for the Early in Career Talent (EiCT)
- Satya Nadella, Microsoft CEO, suggests that the level of impact that AI should aim for must lead to 10% GDP growth. For me, getting to GDP growth at 10% along with AI enabled unemployment sounds either oxymoronic or that the wealth disparities will increase significantly necessitating governmental interventions
- Micha Kaufman, CEO of Fiverr, strongly mentions that from AI, easy tasks will cease to exist for humans and hard tasks will become easier. AI is coming to take everyone’s jobs including his own What should I do?
- Firstly, if you look back in time, the ancestral work mode of 7 days a week changed to 5 days a week. Some jobs barely need 40 hours a week and will get to a 30 hour work week. I am not suggesting that one works less now, however keeping everything at the same baseline, if you smartly employ the right tools, your job can now be done in 30 hours or even less
- Anything that a remote worker does today will be simplified by AI. Instead of looking at AI as a threat, divide your job in parts as opposed to a complete job and determine which parts of your job can leverage AI and automation tools
- Look for additional functions/roles in adjacency that you can start learning and supporting. As a recruiter, teacher, technologist, marketeer, photographer, product manager, copywriter, sales person, service engineer, designer, data scientist or whatever you do today, you should be able to do this and the step above
- Encourage and be encouraged to build affinity towards physical and non-technology based industries
- AI change cannot be disruptive overnight and it will be gradual. If you look for it, you will have ample opportunities to intersperse AI in your life
- Complexity of human interactions, creative problem solving, contextual understanding, need for comprehensive skills set, regulatory expectations and physical dexterity still offer moat for AI to intersperse easily World Economic Forum – Future of Jobs Report (2025) nicely articulates the growing and declining jobs on page 21. Use this as a reference to understand the future especially for those navigating to become EiCT. Advise next gen to make sensible academic investments. Let’s hope that AI regulation, Academic reforms, Guidance to the Corporations in advanced economies is prioritized in addition to becoming the AI masterchef — else the domestic chaos will continue to significantly impact unrest.

title: Unmanning in the Real World
content:
Following the lead on ‘Generative’ nature of Large Language Models significantly impacting entry level jobs, here is yet another point of view on what other functions are being disrupted and where this disruption is happening.

Technology leading to ‘Unmanning’ aspects Following are some example disruptions created by the AI-driven robotics enablement in warfare and

### physical spaces

- ‘Renovate Robotics’ is an AI-based Robotics Automation company that automates roof installation as a co-contractor, promising to boost productivity, ensure safety, speedy implementation and precise installation of shingles on the roof
- Another company, Covariant, has built a Robotics Foundation Model (RFM) where robots are enabled with human-like reasoning ability. RFM is trained with text, image and video data to create robotic actions in the real-world to automate warehouse operations, for example
- Modern day defense technologies are leading to humanless warfare where companies like Anduril & Shield.AI promise to protect civilians and enable armed forces to carry out complex missions using autonomous unmanned aerial vehicles
- In another instance, a company named Teledyne Flir has built military microdrones for defense and covert reconnaissance Unmanning of the Intellectual Workspace Beyond the entry level jobs and skilled workforce (military and factory automation) is the impact on the intellectual workforce. This impact is coming through the advent of AI agents. An AI agent leverages reasoning ability, remembrance/memory and workflow automation tools to conduct human-like business as an advanced employee would. The ability to reason, plan and take action based on information it’s given, this AI agent simulating a digital employee thinks, remembers and gets things done. There are

### three parts to this

- Presence of brain is demonstrated through the effective use of LLMs which are used for reasoning, planning and language generation
- Memory in the form of datasets and history of past interactions allows tracking of continuity and successful results to take future action
- Finally, the AI agent will use tools like Email, Workflow Tools and other new age technologies to interact with the outside world A simple example is a Customer Service Agent. Compared to Generative AI-based internship-like behaviours, an AI agent is trained with customers, products, and several scenarios to support customers just like a human-based agent would work with the customers. There is also the ability to remember past interactions for reference and for training as any human agent would have. At some point in the near future, I want to create an example AI agent which I will look forward to sharing with a real-life example. I don’t have time to understand and use these AI Agents? On average, as a working professional we attend 15 meetings every week and additionally spend 1/3rd of our time responding to emails or chat messages. Also account for time spent on distractions and social media. This busy bee mindset numbs us out and prevents us from finding real freedom — creating space to take a break, to think, and to innovate. This perceived burnout leads to 4 million people quitting their rat-race jobs every month in the United States.

The good thing is between Digitization, AI and pandemic, we have gotten better. Our productivity has increased with many organizations moving towards a 4-day work week, explicitly or implicitly. This has been experimented with reduced time at work in Spain, Scotland, Norway, Denmark, US, Ireland, France and Germany. Certain jobs that are low paying in nature or certain cultures where a long working hours mindset is a dominant aspect are finding it hard to change.

It is imperative that you figure out ways to make yourself more efficient through effective meeting management, time management and investment in using the new age tools.

### Thoughts courtesy of these podcasts

https://youtu.be/dr1-fPkTxZM?si=dOS4i3-tUa9470Uj https://youtu.be/MyVfUaXyyqo?si=ou1zNRAn2ut3X3Xx https://youtu.be/uPZs_gAxfjM?si=hsIg8q6BDsXGRDPH

title: Spending, Upbringing and the Value of Money
content:
As a young person, are your parents critical about your excessive spending habits? If you are a parent, do you feel that your children do not understand the value of money? Are our spending and money management habits a function of upbringing, culture around us, geography, parental influences, next gen or just unique to us? Possibly, some combination of these. However, in addition, our spending behaviours, wealth creation, and money management are intrinsically determined by many factors unknown to us. Here is a deep dive Savings, Consumption and Wealth creation abilities around the world:

- Here is a simple list representing average savings rate from earned 100 currency units for various countries: ~5% for an American, ~35% for a Chinese, ~18% for an Indian, 6% in the UK and 10-15% in Japan & Germany
- Consumption & Spendings by the individuals determine growth of GDP (Gross Domestic Product) by definition. Higher the spend, higher the GDP
- China, structured as a communist nation, conspires via a state directed capitalist economy. In a nutshell, Chinese government determines year over year directional spends to determine sectors for economic growth
- Earlier mention of high savings rate in China reduces consumption within the economy. This forces the government to make investments in sectors like factories, infrastructure, automation (EVs) and real estate to push for GDP growth
- Top 4 Banks in the world are based in China and they hold $20T+ in assets more than what the country makes in a year. China’s property market is about $130T in value
- Comparatively, an average American saves only 5% for what she earns. With less than 4% of the global population, Americans spend 1 out of 3 dollars across the planet making it the largest consuming economy.

What encourages us to Spend & Consume vs. Save & Invest?

- China, for example, has bigger challenges from poverty, famines and crisis management. Citizens don’t trust the government and as a result they are pushed to save
- Owning real estate in some countries like India and China is a status symbol. Owning a home can be a prerequisite to marriage
- Social security (Post retirement care) in these countries is not reliable and healthcare is not accessible with ease
- China manufactures 20 billion pieces of clothing with 1.4 billion people citizens, has housing to accommodate 3 billion citizens, has over built road and rail network, manufactures 4.5 billion appliances annually, produces 1.4 billion cell phones annually, manufactures steel enough to fulfill half the world’s demand, produces EVs in large quantities and manufactures with an export orientation. This as such does not help its citizens as such but tries to drive growth with potential economic downturns
- Comparatively, consumer confidence for larger nations in the Western hemisphere is high. US leads the way in consumer spending, as mentioned earlier
- 60% of Americans own stocks. The UK and Japan have 30%+ investors in the stock market. Similarly, India is moving in this direction. Investments in China are a bit more conservative focusing more on real-estate and savings, away from its stock market
- Countries printing of currency for citizens based consumption are far more effective compared to countries like China that print currencies to drive controlled/politicized economic growth At large, spending habits of most Eastern hemisphere countries are relatively conservative. Additionally, State run economies like China reduce faith of the citizens in the nation’s intent on individual level wealth creation, forcing their citizens to save more. A lot of these countries and economies acknowledge prosperity but do not make use of it as they prosper. In Conclusion: When citizens feel protected, educated, insured, and with the convenience of infrastructure, they are encouraged to spend & consume more. In another example, Berkshire Hathaway a U.S.-based multinational conglomerate holding company led by Warren Buffett, holds assets in excess of $900B with only 5-7% of $900B invested outside of the US. They feel very confident about being protected with how the country is governed at large. As individuals, our consumption and spending at large is driven by economic comfort and less by habits or culture. Incremental policy reforms in growing economies lead to increased incomes and spending as a result, which remain invisible to us in a way. Countries with stagnated/rigid policy framework and corrupted state will have an impact on both, the wellbeing of the citizens and the economic growth. These nations will have to relinquish control to private companies and improve their policy reforms significantly in order to prosper.

If you have incrementally grown your incomes, built the ability to invest and feel more confident about the future then be assured that you are in the right place. Be thankful! https://youtu.be/X06ynCq0Pcg?si=cLMhi7yftMbWbyEi https://www.costco.com/franklin-floor-lamp.product.100719786.html $64M per day YouTube royalties paid to people; YouTube ate TV;

title: The Equation of Adversaries
content:
Equation of Adversaries: In our life, we don’t grow up with all the resources at our discretion Big City Decathlon As a young person growing up in a big city like Mumbai, it felt like living through a real-life, recurring Olympic decathlon — except our events were not on the track, but in the streets, staircases, and trains of daily life. The disciplines? Noise Pollution, Commuting by Crowded Mumbai Local Trains, Space Management (my 140 Sq Ft Mansion — yes, you read it right), Social Inequality (answering a phone meant a five-storey climb and descent), Identity Crisis (Can I bring friends over to my big fancy house?), and Social (In)equality on Display, just to name a few.

Of course, those who came from small towns had their own decathlon — perhaps with issues of infrastructure, power supply, access to high-speed internet, and other obstacles specific to their environment.

So What? How Does This Matter?

Among other things, your resiliency as a human is defined by your upbringing. Adversity forces you to play life’s games like Tug-of-Work and Survival-of-the-Fittest, sharpening your

### navigational abilities. There are two parts to this

1. Building upon who you are 2. Defining your continuous being As the strength of a skyscraper lies in its foundation, your ability to withstand professional uncertainty and stand tall through circumstantial storms is a direct function of how your early foundation was built.

Over time, you accumulate layers of experience — learning to deal with failure, to stand out, and most importantly, to stand tall.

What’s Your Modus Operandi? Invisibility, In Visibility, or In Visibility for Your Convenience This is where you pause and reflect on your own operating model.

Just two hours ago, I completed a three-hour mandatory harassment prevention course. As I reached step 99 out of 100, my browser crashed — everything reset. It felt like being bitten by the snake at square 93 in a childhood game of Snakes & Ladders. I will restart the training. But what does this have to do with your modus operandi?

There are these three Modes We Operate In 🥷 ‘Invisibility’ Mode In this mode, I become oblivious to my potential.

"I’m a doctor — I have enough patients."

"I run an online market research firm — I have paying customers."

"I have a job — I meet expectations."

This gives me just enough growth to keep pace with inflation. I walk with my head high, but I’m playing it safe.

🎭 ‘In Visibility for My Convenience’ Mode I become seasonally relevant. I build marketing campaigns, push throughput, launch a side project. This allows non-standard growth — maybe 2–3x inflation. I tap into my early-life Olympic resilience but only when I feel like it. I use compounding, and it feels good… but it’s not my best game.

🏅 Pureplay ‘In Visibility’ Mode This is where the game changes. Your goal is no longer just money. You push boundaries, step out of your comfort zone, and run a decathlon every day.

Fans of Better Call Saul will remember when Saul Goodman offered 50% discounts to drug offenders — low margins, high volume. That’s pure hustle.

### This is where

- You create new solutions for new markets
- You test unique ideas with confidants before launching big
- You operate in full visibility, not just for your personal convenience Like Rishabh Pant, who failed miserably in the IPL but scored back-to-back test centuries — it’s not about the wins; it’s about the attitude to go again.

Go for the GOLD: Your resilience and shaping didn’t happen overnight — they came from years of silent perseverance.

Now ask yourself: “Am I truly playing in full visibility mode, or am I just conveniently visible?” Search your soul.

title: You Just Need to Keep in Touch
content:
You just need to Keep-in-Touch: Although this write-up will sound technical in nature, at the end of it, you will walk with solid awareness and confidence to know how you as an individual and the ones you care about new entrants in professional lives can successfully practice AI. Retrospective State of Software Engineering: There was a very very high demand for software engineers and technology practitioners in the early to mid-90s. I know of 100s of my colleagues who could advent into the world of software without much of a formal education. They successfully have made a mark in the software and the SaaS space today leveraging some basic training and through experiential learning. This era is repeating and worth exploring how.

A big difference between the 90s and today is in the fact that the demand for software professionals was higher then than compared to the ease with which software can be written now. Let’s examine how.

Software 1.0: In this era, from client-server to very sophisticated SaaS evolution, you wrote code in English like software languages to instruct the computer(s) in the form of software programs. Easy enough.

Software 2.0: Imagine a Computer based AI chess player that is playing a game against a grandmaster chess champion. Obviously, when the computer is playing the game it is not programmed to remember all possible combinations for moves of chess pieces. It uses a technique called unsupervised learning where your computer is managed in the form of a neural network. Neural network has something called weights which are programmed to respond with chess moves. Evolution of self-driving cars is based similarly where the neural capacity of a Full Self-Driving mode uses the objects (other vehicles, traffic signals, people), the road dividing lines and the lanes to create a neurological response to drive the car.

Software 3.0: I believe Software 2.0 is a slightly complicated representation that gets simplified here. Imagine that your computer is now replaced by Large Language Models. You provide instructions in the form of prompts to program and fine tune the model to get answers. This, as you all have experienced by now, is in the form of English language interactions. As a new entrant in the AI space or as a graduating student, you need to know how Software 1.0 existed and how you can fully exploit the power of Software 3.0. Soham Parekh, a young and slightly misguided software enthusiast, did exactly this without keeping focus on one-thing and got distracted in the process.

Andrej Karpathy, ex-Director of AI and Auto Pilot vision at Tesla, has beautifully explained these and some more AI concepts in this referenced talk while addressing students who are at the verge of entering professional life.

In his words LLMs/AI are like power that is needed for our existence. This is a utility that needs a large Capital Expenditure investment (build LLMs equivalent to building a power plant), its consumers needing Operational Expenditure (Consume Power or Pay for tokens in the LLMs context), need stability (power to be highly available), with no latency (Light bulb glows as soon as turn the switch on), and with multiple LLMs (Llama, OpenAI, Claude) just like getting power from your power grid, battery, solar or oil based generators. In a nutshell LLMs are equivalent to electricity.

In conclusion: With LLMs, everyone is a programmer, as most of us speak English - a natural language. You don’t need to study software engineering to be able to program. He tweeted with a meme themed as ‘Vibe Coding’. You blabber to the LLM, ignore long wielded assessment, ignore a lot of meaning behind the code, keep talking the machine just like a 5th grader till it produces code for your purpose. Virality of the meme actually created a wikipedia entry on “Vibe Coding”. Here are the snapshots of his tweet and the Wiki page.

### Vibe Coding Tweet

### The Wiki reference it deserved

title: The Bullshit Served on a Platter
content:
You call your best friend for her advice on a situation. She tells you to be true to your inner self and follow your path. The Bullshit served on a platter — and she lost a friend. This bullshit exists everywhere, and this write-up will build a certain level of cognizance and appetite to deal with it.

Toxic Culture that is Devoid of Meaning Have you come across situations where leaders often say:

- “Let’s circle back” when they have no idea what to say at a given point
- “We need to be agile” so you can get $#!+ done without asking for guidance, budget, or more time
- “Think outside the box” and tell me what I would like to hear
- “We need to empower our teams” so that you are responsible for a task without any authority on budgets or resources
- “We are on a transformation journey” and I have no idea as a leader what we will achieve — however, we paid McKinsey a lot of money to draw a pretty picture and a lot more
- We are “Data Driven” or “Customer Obsessed” Probably there are a dozen more examples — and your favorites that you can add to this list. Well, at first, believe me: you are not alone looking to breathe in this kind of a world. AI-led Human Hallucination AI has given yet another flavor to this story. When we started the AI journey, we quickly realized that early versions of LLMs hallucinated 1 out of 6 times — saying things like 9.11 is actually greater than 9.9.

I have seen many podcasts and presentations where corporations are now cluelessly aiming to be an “AI First” company. They speak about using AI Agents, Co-Pilots, Model Context Protocols, and AI Applications — without having foundational AI strategies.

Compared to the Large Language Model hallucinating 1 out of 6 times, corporations are running like chicken without a head. Leaders here are hallucinating perhaps more than 60% of the time — compared to 16% by the early LLMs.

So, Why does this happen?

Naturally, everyone wants to be relevant and be strategic in their thought leadership. Nothing wrong with that.

This leads to “Feel Good Fairy Dusting” — that complicates your thought process in this abstracted world. These complicated words, thoughts, and messages are far from reality. They hear some bits in presentations, from service providers, peer groups, and some events. They bring half-hearted guesswork into the context of a professional setup.

How do you build cognizance, respond, and navigate in such situations?

- Culture of the entity where you are an employee, participant, contributor, or a creator matters a lot. Know the past history and understand your ability to find the right people and ask good questions. If the organization encourages a high-performing and participative culture, then life will eventually balance out these anomalies
- After finding the right people, ask for relevant examples to the first introduced fancy jargons in this write-up — or for the ambitious plans to navigate in the new-age AI-like transformation
- If your environment and culture permit, bring courage in perspective. Identify contradicting behaviors to what gets stated in leadership messages and call out the underlying “BS” — obviously without explicitly using the BS word
- This behavior trickles, and as a leader, you will start reflecting it. Remove it from your mindset and from your language
- Aim for simplicity in your personal language and ask your teams and colleagues to aim for clarity. Stop creating and accepting the fog of fancy words. Share knowledge with simple words and do not pretend to become a social/professional media influencer
- Most importantly, become a student of life and simply focus on strengthening your intellect In the last two decades, many organizations were born on the technology cloud to build cloud-relevant solutions. These companies — and companies prior to them or newer non-technological organizations — are all trying to stay relevant in the new AI world. If you are part of these organizations or an independent professional, then build your own awareness through use of these tools. Recently, I met many individuals who use the LLMs to do prep accounting work, legal groundwork, or financial research. Their engagement with tax, legal, and financial professionals after the use of these tools is simplified, accelerated, and becomes cost-efficient. They leverage the power of AI/LLMs for a lot of foundation work, remain savvy, and avoid becoming overly dependent on specialist professionals.

Jargons and generalized inspiration are often used by leaders in their conversation. As long as these thoughts are used as conversational seasonings, things are okay. But if they are part of your daily main course or buffet, then it is time to look for a change.

title: Opinionated Inner Circles
content:
In housing complexes, religious communities, large companies, families, and groups of friends, there are inner circles — and then there are others. Inner circles are groups of people that find conformity and comfort with each other. They think alike and rub their beliefs and energies off one another. In the process, they build opinions about those who are not part of the circle. A large, complex organization may have many such inner circles.

Unfortunately, the broader organization sees these circles too — and develops opinions about them in return. When such groups become entrenched over time, they often breed bitterness among people. Hatred and lack of trust build over the years, and eventually, this impacts the wellbeing of the entire setup. Major projects experience delays, red tape, and disharmony from bureaucracies. Organizations suffer financial losses.

At the same time, I’ve also seen that in times of crisis — barring extremist mindsets — people pull together to face challenges. The success of such organizations depends on leadership. Let me put a situation in perspective.

You are (or can be) Contagious This excerpt is from a previous write-up and is relevant here.

When you meet a person for the first time, what do you look at? Face, shoes, or body? Not quite — they look at your hands first. Your hands speak volumes: are you a hawk, a defender, or someone who welcomes?

Now I understand what it means to welcome with open arms. People look at whether you are holding a rock, a scissor, or an offering of warmth. If they can’t see your hands, they can’t see your intentions.

Celebrating Good Leaders It’s 5 pm on Friday. My team decides to go have a drink. On the way out of the office, Amit Kumar — one of my most celebrated leaders — takes 10 minutes to greet and wish a good weekend to every colleague he passes, including the front desk and security staff. Everyone waiting is irritated by this uncanny but predictable habit of Amit. As irritating as it got for us, Amit is that contagion that passes the positivity of life around. I’ve seen more leaders — but he always stood out.

Some good habits that he (and other good leaders) demonstrate:

- They make people feel comfortable
- They check on others: on their wellbeing
- They decouple time-lapsed or recent biases and focus on the person in front of them
- They don’t build toxins within — and this could be a much longer list Wrong Leaders are Celebrated Often As much as good leaders deserve to be celebrated, we too often see mediocre, incompetent, authoritative leaders idolized. Why?
- Sometimes they are celebrated due to racism or sexism biases
- There is the Action Fallacy — we mistake noise for leadership
- They speak more, so we hear them more — regardless of content
- They demonstrate confidence, regardless of competence
- They pretend to be perpetually busy, no matter the substance
- They blow their own trumpets and love to recover from the mistakes they caused
- They are the self-proclaimed captains of crisis The best way to discover good leaders is to introspect deeply on the traits of both good and bad

### leadership. Look for leaders who

- Don’t promote drama
- Quietly accomplish big missions
- Show empathy
- And most importantly, don’t create inner circles Your Tenancy Keeping myself in perspective, I can relate to many of the positive traits above. I am rarely in an inner circle. I demonstrate empathy. I avoid the action fallacy and resist creating drama.

### In reality, your tenancy exists everywhere

- Inside the inner circles
- As an observer of those circles you’re not part of
- As someone who appreciates your good traits
- As someone who strives to avoid contributing to toxic culture

### But the real question is

Where do you pay the most rent?

In seeking attention — or in driving results?

In action fallacy — or in building a strong premise of integrity?

The best way to find out where you're living as a tenant?

Seek honest feedback from people you trust — the ones who will help you see the mirror.

title: Chasing the Next Best Thing
content:
school, getting to a top college, getting that job, getting that promotion after the new job, building a successful business or looking for the next best opportunity. Next holiday, a fancy car, bigger home and the list goes on.

The race is always on and often supplemented by therapy, antidepressants, displeasure and anger experienced by loved ones, and sometimes futile efforts to calm yourself with meditation, no offense to yoga teachers at home or otherwise.

We become addicts of achievement and constantly look for validation and approval from others. Likes on Social Media, Appreciation from Leadership, Praises from your workplace. In search of this pursuit when people ask us how we are doing, the first word that comes to mind is ‘Busy’. At what cost? Invariably, our mental and physical being are impacted. I have been a victim of this mode. So I have come to terms with some of this and in all honesty, not entirely.

### Tug of War between Efforts and Outcomes

🧑‍💼📈✨ 💸📈🤑 💰💎🚀 As humans we like to fit in, not create chaos, build tolerance as our goal is to make notional achievements in kind (Promotion , Raise , New Job , Social Status ). In the context of achieving these notional achievements, we make a lot of upstream efforts where the daily rut far exceeds your tolerance, necessitating a headache pill at the end of each workday.

👑🏆 What if contrary to artificially fitting into achieving notional achievements, we focus more on doing things that bring joy like playing a sport, spending more time with friends, painting, cooking, fitness regime or traveling. For me personally, I focus on intellectual upliftment through my weekend research.

Once I/we do this, your psychological flow gets really smooth. When you are working on a problem (work related or not), your mind will be unburdened from the chaos that your expectations create. This seamlessness will let you focus on the most productive ways of working. Don’t expect for what you do in your personal space to create magic in your

### professional setup as there is more to do

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- Don’t be a Yes (wo)man to begin with Identify the value that your presence brings to a task or a meeting Escape from activities and conversations where you are not adding value Disconnect Actions from Expected Outcomes Expect outcomes to be byproduct of your actions and not as foundational goals Avoid doing things that don’t matter to your fabric and I am sure there is more If the expected activities compromise your fundamental beliefs then choose a path to decouple from the situation (people, organizations or initiatives) Expiry of Tolerance - Let’s Move On: In more than 3 decades of my career, I have had a few bumpy days. A day when I walked into the office unshaven from forgetting to shave, the time when my health parameters took a detour… and ended up at a pizza parlour or when I realized cultural toxicity. In all instances, I walked out, including relocating to a new geography. While you cannot quit or walk out unreasonably, make conscious choices of how you operate on an everyday basis. It is hard to stay away from attention addiction so keep assessing to decouple consciously from being in that mode.

Inspiration credits https://youtu.be/S8yWLA--S8Q?si=zuoJ1tO_UM6gsAwG

title: The Consultant Who Served Two Masters
content:
In my distant past, I had a colleague (Let’s call him Sam) who was working as a consultant at one of the client locations. This (over)smart young man was working for two clients simultaneously. No big deal with that however this is what happened. He used to claim for his hotel accommodation from both the employers and once from sending his invoice of client B to my food chain, he was caught. Embarrassed.

Multi-tasking, Multi-skilling and Parallelization When you multi-task to your implied oversmartness or from aiming to be very productive, you will encounter slippery slopes. It happens to all of us with small things and big things. I had a messy kitchen this weekend as hot tea spilled out from over boiling, owing to a small distraction from another task. Multiple lessons to learn: switch over to coffee, get my house in order before she gets here and realize that ordinary humans can only perform one task at a time. Sometimes these multi-tasking desires can lead to Sam-like embarrassments, let alone the lack of integrity. Multi-skilling is entirely and at the other end of the spectrum compared to multi-tasking. Here you work to prevent obsolescence, simply speaking to become resilient in this ever changing world and especially with potential disruptions to be seen in the not so distant future. Here you can build or develop brand new skills. As a business person you become a real estate analyst/investor to diversify your portfolio. As a working professional you become a fitness trainer. As a Marketing Analyst you become a Public Speaker. As a Software Engineer you become a Solutions Architect. As a Quality Assurance Engineer you become a Business Analyst. As an Employee of an Organization you start a side gig. As a commuter you become a part-time Uber driver. There are so many possibilities.

A quick intro to Parallelization. The term ‘Parallelization’ is largely referred to in computing architecture. CPU uses multiple threads to execute different parts of a program simultaneously. Each thread handles separate tasks improving overall productivity. Does this sound Oxymoronic compared to the Multi-tasking narrative. Not quite. This is about handling. As a leader sometimes you manage multiple functions, as a home maker you have several home units to manage and as a multi-skilled talented individual you have the ability to work on several skills. A key differentiator between multi-tasking and parallelization is between how you divide your time in chunks and how you stop doing one thing before you start a new thing, with a seamless hand-off.

### Importance of Multi-skilling and Parallelization

Many facets of Multi-Skilling

- Given how easy it is to start a new side gig in the AI-age, everyone must be encouraged to leverage the tools, learn new skills and make hay while the sun shines
- This will also allow you to defend obsolescence of a skill set. It is projected that 80% of jobs will be automated for 80% of the tasks in the near future. This will make our multi-skilled mindset an imperative
- Multi-skilling will help to easily Transition to a new phase of life: as a mother, as a retiree, as a student, as a curious person, as someone seeking a promotion, as to increase your value through additional offerings in your workplace, as being relevant with the new generation, as an aim to build/support your purpose, and to create a brand new thinking perspective Parallelization Playbook
- You will build economies of scale from guiding/directing multiple teams. This needs, as mentioned earlier, trust in people who do the work for you and the guidance you provide to them with a very effective hand-off
- Have a fool-proof mechanism to ensure that the task you initiate with a (wo)man or machine is performed with a high degree of confidence
- Ensure that you are not parallelizing unattended high risk activities How to become an effective Multi-Skilled Parallelizer?

This to me is more an art than a science.

- Malcolm Gladwell’s book Outlier introduced the mindset to spend 10,000 hours to master a skill. At the same time, to learn a skill you don’t need to master it. Sometimes a skill can be learnt in just 20 hours.
- We impose a mindset on our loved ones, especially the children. Sometimes we even conspire with them to be like us. Instead use the imposition and conspiracy on self to reinvent yourself with new skills
- Become a vibe coder to write your software or a vibe prompter to solve many problems with AI Have you built new skills recently? Have you used your vibes to tinker with AI tools? Are you aspiring to build new skills? Let me know.

title: Characterizing an AI Architect
content:
Oh no, AI again — don’t be scared. As technical as it sounds, anyone reading this write-up can aspire to be an AI Architect. Read on. I’ve tried to capture the themes that define an AI Architect or help you build your AI Architecture. Five characteristics, followed by a concluding question:

1. ‘AI-Driven’ Brand Ambassadors An AI Architect is today’s equivalent of a Webmaster.

A webmaster — now a forgotten term in the AI age — was once responsible for creating and maintaining a company’s internet storefront. She ensured performance, content, functionality, and security to manage an organization’s online presence.

Similarly, an AI Architect represents customer-facing AI functions for both internal and external consumers. As we progress, websites will increasingly be replaced by AI Agents. Imagine a branded AI Agent for customer support instead of a “Support” section on a website. The AI Architect builds, coaches, and continuously improves these agents.

2. ‘Open to All’ Opportunity AI Architecture is more than technical plumbing. It includes a secured AI-enabled front end, design, aesthetics, values, tone of brand, and — importantly — the ability to drive business outcomes. Given the engineer-like and human-like qualities of today’s AI stack, professionals in User or Customer Experience roles can easily wear this hat.

3. Thought Leaders In 2022–23, when Large Language Models first emerged, we did not know of AI Agents or Model Context Protocols. Model accuracy, capabilities, and modalities are improving rapidly, and an AI Architect must understand where AI is headed, not just where it stands today.

They analyze problems across time: how they would have been solved six months ago, how they are solved today, and how they could be solved in the future. For instance, we started with text-only inputs and outputs. Today we have text, images, audio, and video. Tomorrow, we will have robotics, AI glasses, and ambient inputs and outputs.

An AI Architect avoids repeating mistakes of the past. Many organizations failed in cloud transitions by simply porting on-premise systems “as-is.” Similarly, customer service teams will evolve from directly and conventionally engaging with customers to instead coaching and refining AI agents — embedding empathy, judgment, and resolution into them.

4. Scrappy & Myopic AI Architects don’t let “perfect” be the enemy of “good.” They live iteratively, tolerate risk, and design strategies to improve predictability while reducing model hallucinations. This requires building a robust data strategy so that models can be trained on organizational knowledge and applied to specific functions.

Their action orientation means not spending six months drafting an AI strategy and another six months implementing it. Instead, they focus myopically on solving real customer and business problems with simple, usable AI Agents — and then refining them using first principles.

5. AI Agents — Buy or Build?

Should you buy or build your AI Agents?

- Buy: Customer support leaders often prefer ready-to-use frameworks from companies like Yellow.ai, Five9, Sierra, Forethought, IBM WatsonX, or Zendesk AI.
- Build: Engineering teams are naturally inclined to design their own. A homegrown Customer Support Agent requires choosing the right language model(s), embeddings, vector database, and integrations with telephony, CRM, knowledge repositories, and more. It involves planning for testing, upgrades, and model migrations.
- Middle Path: Platforms with no-code frameworks let you build AI Agents with a full development lifecycle. They provide tools for testing, feedback, and improvements, including scenarios where the agent escalates to a human before going live.

Art or Science?

So, is AI Architecture an art or a science? Honestly, it has become a commoditized artistic platform built on the fundamentals of science.

Do you agree?

Thoughts inspired by: YouTube link

title: Turning Butterflies Into the Butterfly Effect
content:
When I started writing about this topic over the weekend, I did not expect that today would bring me a fresh story to share. Before I get to that, let me go back to my early leadership years. I vividly remember presenting a mock topic at a leadership development public speaking training. I had chosen the wrong topic, froze midway, and embarrassed myself.

Fast forward to now, decades later, with training through experience and bumpy rides, I found myself presenting our strategy at a townhall meeting. This was my first time addressing such a large gathering in my new role. I had spent 15 hours preparing for a 15-minute talk. Just before I could get to the virtual podium, I was told my time was cut short to 7 minutes. Did I freeze? Did I have butterflies in my stomach? Neither.

And it wasn’t just the years of experience that made the difference. It was a combination of

### several things

- Preparation matters. I stared at the content for hours. Even 30 minutes before the session, I hadn’t fully finalized my pitch—not because I wasn’t confident, but because I kept refining the message.
- Adversity strengthens character. Instead of worrying about my time being halved, I trained my mind to view these moments as steps toward success.
- Expect the unexpected. My years of working with Private Equity firms were the best training. They set the bar high—not only for returns but for people. Every presentation taught me one certainty: always expect the unexpected.
- Connection over content. What ultimately matters most is how you connect with your audience—what’s in it for them.

And here’s a thought I keep repeating: kids today use AI as an assistant in their studies. Teachers, knowing this, now expect 8th graders to perform at graduate level. If that’s what we expect of the next generation, why should we expect less of ourselves? Leverage tools—AI included—to shape your narratives. Doing so makes it much easier to respond to the unexpected.

### Key takeaways

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- Build an appetite to step out of your comfort zone—often.

Make yourself visible, no matter how visible you already are.

Look for moments and opportunities—they don’t always knock on your door.

Practice, practice, practice—don’t let fear fuel self-doubt.

Be bold enough to strike conversations with anyone.

Turn your butterflies into the butterfly effect.

In chaos theory, the butterfly effect suggests that a butterfly flapping its wings in one part of the world could contribute to a tornado or storm in another. Tiny actions, seemingly insignificant, can create large and unpredictable outcomes.

So, the next time you feel butterflies in your stomach, remember: they could be the very wings that set a storm of success in motion.

title: The Subtle Art of Getting Things Done
content:
I’ve been dissecting a short podcast and, believe me, the podcast is far more entertaining in value than what you will learn from this Sunday capsule. Still, with the occasional miss, my job is to write—so I am writing, irrespective. Here are some lessons of life.

Optimist or Pessimist?

There is no upside to optimism; one should rather be a pessimist. Over the years, we’ve been conditioned to always be hopeful. This time, let’s weigh the value of being a pessimist. Imagine giving your manager or client a deadline to deliver a proposal by Friday close of business. Instead, you submit it Thursday evening—complete, polished, and with tips they didn’t expect. You not only beat the deadline but also add unexpected value. That’s the power of underpromising and overdelivering.

Never Let Them Know What They’re Working Toward Think of a pawn in chess: the smallest, most ordinary piece. Its only instruction is to move one square forward. Nothing glamorous, nothing heroic. The pawn doesn’t know that by simply following this humble rule, one step at a time, it may reach the other side of the board and transform into a queen.

Similarly, when you read one book a month or write one page a day, you build compounding habits that one day transform you into a critical thinker capable of solving life’s hardest problems.

Punctuality Many workplaces accept meetings starting 5–10 minutes late. But if six people sit waiting for two stragglers, that’s an hour of collective time wasted.

Punctuality follows a simple rule: being early is on time, being on time is late, and being late makes you the punchline.

Don’t Work Your Way Up the Ladder Incrementalism rarely gets you to the top. Disruption does. True breakthroughs come from challenging norms, not following them. Airbnb redefined hospitality, Uber redefined transportation, and Full Self Driving is redefining mobility. Astonishing results demand disruptive thinking, not small steps up the ladder.

Let It Be Their Decision Henry Ford famously said, “You can have a car of any color, as long as it’s black.” Contrast that with today, where customers demand customization, choices, and delightful surprises. Apple didn’t just give people what they asked for—they designed products that became indispensable. Jony Ive and Sam Altman are now collaborating to design AI-native hardware. Their genius will be in letting people perceive the product as their own desired choice.

Standout and Don’t Stand Out If you’re at the edge of a group photo, you might look wider—or worse, be cropped out. The trick is finding the right place, the right people, and the right opportunities to achieve scale. Out-Juvenile a Screaming Body Bullies don’t disappear after school; they reappear in workplaces, bringing toxicity. Don’t let them win. Build a Fort Knox of capabilities and confidence. Use your voice. Stand your ground. Assimilate First, Then Shine When you enter a new workplace or culture, understand its conventions. Learn the idioms: “all bases loaded” in the U.S., “sticky wicket” in India, “red card” in Europe. Blend in first. Once you’ve marked your presence and built your brand, reveal your authentic self. Authenticity, more than anything else, will carry you forward.

This piece took me a while to write, especially as I translated Barry Sonnenfeld’s PodFuncast into this capsule. But the lessons are worth the effort.

title: Growing Pains and Scaling Laws
content:
Benefits from Growth & Growing Pains: Scaling laws are mathematical relationships that describe how certain properties of a system change as the system grows in size. They’re found across physics, biology, economics—and recently, artificial intelligence. If this could not be

### understood easily, just look at the table below

Animal Body weight (g) Daily intake (% body weight) Daily (g) Ant 0.005 intake 30.0 0.0015 Hummingbird 3.0 100.0 3.0 Rat 300.0 7.0 21.0 Cat 4000.0 2.5 100.0 Dog 20000.0 2.5 500.0 Human 70000.0 2.5 1750.0 Elephant 5000000.0 3.0 150000.0

- Relatively smaller species like Ant or Hummingbird consume far more food proportionate to their body weight owing to very high metabolic rates
- Similarly, if you were to build a bridge then the suspension dynamics of a longer bridge will be nonlinearly more comprehensive than that of a shorter bridge. You may have noticed this in the suspension structure or pillar sizes of longer bridges
- In the context of growth of cities, as the population grows within a city, economies scale the collective productivity of cities grows non-linearly with the population size. The costs (look at the housing costs of big cities) and crime rates also grow disproportionately Scaling in AI: In the age of AI, a lot has come to the surface about Scaling Laws in AI. Ever since OpenAI’s first release of ChatGPT in winter of 2023, the model sizes and capabilities have grown but certainly for a price tag. The following estimated illustration shows how OpenAI models “metabolize” compute, data, and dollars to produce intelligence. This also illustrates that demand for resources like GPUs and AI engineers will increase for the foreseeable future. No wonder you have companies like NVIDIA that are valued greater than 5th largest GDP equivalent economies. Top notch AI engineers are offered compensation in $100M+, much greater than top paid sports athletes and celebrities: Model Params (B) Compute (FLOPs, est.)

Storage (GB) Training Cost (est.

$M) Capability “Skill Level” Accuracy / Sophistic ation GPT-2 (2019) 1.5 ~1e17 ~5 < $1M Word prediction Basic autocompl ete GPT-3 (2020) 175 ~3e23 ~700 $5–15M Basic reasoning, few-shot Good language tasks GPT-3.5 ~175+ (2022) finetune ~1e24 ~800 $15–30M Conversati onal reasoning Stronger logic, code GPT-4 (2023) ~1T (MoE) ~1e25–1e26 TB-scale $50–100M + Advanced reasoning, coding State-of-art accuracy GPT-4o (2024) Similar scale, optimized ~few e25 Smaller per parameter Lower cost Multi-moda / token l, real-time High sophisticati on Scaling Our Personal Journeys: Where Do You Map Yourself?

At the entry level, we burn fast and hot. High energy, high frequency, tackling repetitive tasks with youthful metabolism — a bit like hummingbirds. It’s the formative stage of our journey, where intensity matters more than scope.

By mid-career, the equation changes. You’re not just executing, you’re leading functions of scale. The work becomes more strategic, less about the number of tasks and more about directing outcomes. Think of it as moving from sprinting to pacing a marathon. At the executive level, you’re steering large organizations. Your decisions ripple across thousands of lives, entire markets. Like the hidden stresses of big cities, responsibilities multiply in nonlinear ways. Compensation rises, yes — but so does the complexity, the politics, the sleepless nights.

Breaking the Scaling Curve Here’s the beauty of being human — especially in the age of AI:

- A young entrepreneur can operate like a mammoth. High-energy, high-frequency, yet making decisions of enormous weight and consequence.
- A seasoned leader can flip the curve. By harnessing curiosity, fresh tools, and youthful methods, they can act with the energy of a twenty-something, no matter their age. The Ageless Opportunity In biology, scaling laws are destiny: a hummingbird can’t act like an elephant. In organizations, they’re strong tendencies. But humans? We’re outliers. We can break the pattern. 👉 The opportunities of this new age are ageless. The real question is: Where do you map yourself on this scaling journey? And is it where you want to be?

title: Bullying and Condescension
content:
While writing this week, I looked up the origin of the word bully, assuming it had something to do with being “bull-like.” Actually, not quite.

Bully originated in the 16th century from the Dutch word boel, meaning affectionate lover, brother, or sweetheart. Over the years, it shifted into describing someone who pushed love and affection too forcefully. Today, of course, a bully is understood as someone who habitually mistreats those perceived as weaker.

Condescension—or condescending behavior—is about belittling others. In both bullying and condescension, the weaker ones are diminished.

In childhood, overt bullying often manifests as physical or verbal abuse. Covert bullying is subtler—exclusion from games or social groups. Condescension, meanwhile, shows up as superiority through wealth, skills, or status. Both mistreat those considered weaker. In Organizations In established organizations, bullies rarely show up with physical aggression; that would get them out the door quickly. Instead, they adopt condescending behaviors. We’ll use the terms interchangeably here. Why do they condescend?

- A newcomer trying to fit in may overemphasize how things were done in a prior job.
- Some leverage implicit expectations from the food chain, acting as though they know the “secret sauce” of what management or customers want.
- Patronizing tones instill fear, often covering up their own insecurities.
- Their social life outside work is constrained; they may themselves be bullied at home by a dominating partner.
- They feel unappreciated or unrecognized.
- They seek to protect underperforming peers from being exposed by your competence. And surely, there are more reasons.

How Do You Deal With Them?

The Being/Doing Part

- Show up every day to give your best to your assignment.
- Connect your contributions to the mission and purpose of the larger organization.
- Ensure your work and value creation are impeccable.
- Build relationships with colleagues who can foster your intellectual development.
- Avoid watercooler conversations.
- Create a strong narrative of your contributions and share your impact periodically—without overplaying your trumpet.

The Mental Being

- If their behavior keeps you restless or sleepless despite your strong performance, then you’re part of the problem too.
- Recognize that they have minimal control over your assignments, career, or rewards.
- Focus on continuously raising your bar on the being/doing side.
- Build a plan with a longer horizon than this individual’s potential success.
- Don’t be distracted by the fact that some thrive by “sweet talk.” Like excessive sugar in the system, it eventually takes its toll.

The Giving Back Part

- If your workplace fosters open-door culture, knock and have an honest conversation.
- Acknowledge strengths where you see them; if you can’t find any, don’t overengineer praise.
- Tell the person directly how their behavior affects you and its impact on your work and mental well-being.
- Be direct and objective—this isn’t the place for subtlety.
- Document instances. At some point, you may need factual records. I’ve also seen younger professionals take a more intolerant stand: they demonstrate courage to have direct conversations and, importantly, the courage to walk out of toxic workplaces. A Final Reflection How do you want to be remembered? While processing this, don’t assume you yourself are never a bully. It’s possible—unknowingly—you may be one. Seek continuous feedback and self-assess.

Imagine a disgruntled co-worker who leaves a job and tells her new colleagues in the break

### room

“When this great ex-manager who isn’t around anymore looks down at me…” (a sarcastic sob and pause) “…Not from the heavens. He was just condescending.” That’s the thing—condescension makes others feel like living ghosts hovering beneath you. The idea is not to become the villain in someone else’s horror story.

title: Are You Getting the Best From Your Models?
content:
Model Interactions?

Where Are We With Large Language Models Today?

Over the last year (and then some), model performance has become less of a deciding factor for adoption. Most major providers are pushing toward better reasoning, larger context windows, and multimodal understanding (inputting and generating text, images, and voice). Meta continues to position the openness of Llama for control and customization, while closed models like OpenAI, Claude, and Google emphasize performance.

By now, most of us have heard of Transformers (the “T” in GPT: Generative Pre-trained Transformer). A Transformer reads an entire sentence and relates words to one another—imagine taking in a full page at once, grasping its context, rather than reading line by line.

Models also rely on attention, focusing on the right meaning in context. If you ask about “iPhones sold by Apple,” attention helps the model lock onto Apple the company, not the fruit. Encoding/Decoding & Multimodality. When you provide non-English text (say, Spanish or Hindi), the model uses encoding to represent it internally and decoding to generate the response (often in English). Similarly, an image can be encoded so the model can describe it, and decoding can be used to generate new images.

Decoder/Auto-regressive models (like ChatGPT or Llama) generate outputs by predicting the next token based on what they’ve learned.

Modern systems also include Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) layers that activate only the most relevant “experts”—like calling in the right specialists for each problem—boosting accuracy without running every parameter every time.

Lastly, many models employ a reasoning loop—a “think before speaking” phase. If we adopted more of that as humans, we’d probably have fewer arguments.

Beyond Basic Prompting: Getting More From LLMs Models have learned to be pleasant—you’ll sometimes see “That’s a great question!”—but unlike a mentor, they may not push back or offer tough alternatives. To avoid that trap, upgrade how you interact.

1) Ask Better Questions We’ve all seen long, winding questions that drain a room. Clarity is a gift. Get to the point, add only the context that matters, and specify the outcome you want.

2) Zero-Shot vs. Few-Shot

- Zero-shot: Ask without examples and let the model infer the pattern.
- Few-shot: Provide 1–3 examples so the model mimics the style or decision criteria.

### Example

“Can you name an apparel store in France with value-and-fashion elements like Zara or H&M?” (zero-shot) once yielded Galeries Lafayette (a luxury department store).

When I provided more guidance (few-shot framing), the model suggested Promod, which better matches accessible fashion.

3) Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Ask the model to show its steps.

Example: “Name an apparel store in France like Zara/H&M—explain which you pick and how.” The model justified Promod by citing French roots, reasonable pricing, frequently refreshed fashion, and appeal to fashion-conscious shoppers.

4) Reason + Act (Use Tools) Instruct the model to research or calculate before answering.

Example: “Name an apparel store in France like Zara/H&M, explaining why after doing a little more research.” Result: Kiabi, with reasoning around value positioning, sustainability efforts, and breadth of styles. It took longer—and rightfully so.

5) Tree of Thoughts (Explore Alternatives) Ask the model to explore multiple options and select the best.

Example (as I flew to California from New York):

“Recommend a great Mexican dining experience in San Francisco’s Mission District by thinking through all alternatives and explaining your choice.” After a thoughtful pass, it chose Loló (974 Valencia St.)—a Mission original—for distinctive cuisine, not being a copycat, and for its setting on energetic Valencia Street. It also compared alternatives and offered a clear bottom line.

If you’re a local in SF, give me a shout—I’ll be around this week. I’ll share more thoughts on making the most of your model interactions as I keep writing.

- 
- Chain-of-Thought (CoT): Ask the model to “show its steps.” You get clearer reasoning and fewer leaps to wrong answers (e.g., “explain step-by-step before the final result”).
- ReAct: “Reason + Act.” The model thinks out loud, then uses tools (like a calculator or web search) before answering. It’s like a detective who alternates between theorizing and checking clues.
- Self-consistency & Tree of Thoughts: Instead of trusting a single path, the model explores multiple solution branches and picks the most consistent or best one—like brainstorming several drafts and choosing the winner.

Prompt Management (how you keep it organized)

- Prompt templates: Reusable scaffolding: “You are a helpful analyst… Follow format A, B, C…” Fill in variables (topic, tone, audience). Consistency = higher quality.
- Version control for prompts: Treat prompts like code. Save versions, note what changed, and why (e.g., Git). It prevents “mystery regressions” when a tweak breaks results.
- A/B testing frameworks: Try two prompt variants on the same task. Measure success (accuracy, user ratings, latency) and keep the superior one. Rinse and repeat.
- Prompt optimization tools: Utilities that auto-mutate prompts (reword, reorder, add constraints), run batches, score outcomes, and suggest improvements. Think “spell-check for prompts,” but smarter.

Advanced Techniques (how you level up)

- Function calling: Structure the model’s output so your app can use it directly (e.g., return a JSON object to book a meeting). Fewer misunderstandings, cleaner automation.
- Tool use & planning: Give the model a toolbox (web, code, database) and let it plan steps: “search → summarize → compute → answer.” The prompt defines roles and guardrails.
- Memory management: Keep a light, durable memory of user preferences (“vegetarian,” “US English,” “bullet points”) and session context. Prompts fetch what matters and avoid stuffing everything in.
- Multi-modal prompting: Combine text with images, audio, or PDFs. Ask: “Describe this chart and write an email summary.” Clear instructions plus examples of desired output format are key.

Bottom line: Great prompts are specific, structured, and tested. Start simple (few-shot + format), add reasoning (CoT/ReAct), manage versions, A/B test, and integrate tools/functions when you need reliability at scale. It’s not magic—it’s disciplined conversation design. Agentic / toolable intelligence: GPT-5 and Claude 4.5 are being marketed as agentic systems—models that can drive their own tooling, planning, and execution over extended spans.

title: Potential Overdose: Efforts vs the Value Cycle
content:
Outrunning the Value Cycle?

Before I start with the thoughts, my apology for missing out for a couple of weeks. At some point, I will write about what transpired. For now, yet another tale of two cities. This time, I will only write about one of the two cities. I will follow up shortly to write about the second one and get into how individuals and economies can plan and respond to these stimuli.
